- cancel before submission marks pending agents cancelled or skipped as appropriate
- cancel after submission marks observed remote jobs abandoned unless proven cancelled
- output panel still shows process termination status
- recent runs and durable state do not falsely claim remote cancellation
- tests cover cancel before submit, during submit, and while waiting for remote results
close_reason: Added visualize cancellation events with cancelled local workflow/step semantics and abandoned remote-agent semantics.

title: Add frontend typed event union and live run reducer
description:
WHAT: Add TypeScript event types and a live run reducer in the web app. The reducer should consume workflow_started, workflow_completed, step_status, agent_status, artifact_written, runner_event_error, stdout/stderr/exited as needed, and produce a single live state object used by graph rendering and output/status UI.

Merge rules:
- durable graph/run state is bootstrap state
- live events win while active
- persisted event replay can fill gaps on reconnect
- after run exits, durable graph/details become authoritative for history
- dedupe by eventId/seq so replay + live stream cannot double-apply

Raw runner statuses should be kept for metadata/debug, while visual statuses map to a smaller set: queued/active/waiting/completed/failed/cancelled/abandoned/skipped.

WHY: Direct setState scattered across EventSource handlers does not scale once events become richer. A reducer gives one testable place for precedence, dedupe, and UI mapping.

ACCEPTANCE:
- reducer unit tests cover ordered events, duplicate replay, out-of-date durable snapshot, reconnect bootstrap, and terminal state
- EventSource handlers are thin adapters into the reducer
- existing stdout/stderr output behavior remains intact
- TypeScript types document every event the UI consumes
close_reason: Implemented typed live run reducer, thin EventSource adapters, replay dedupe, stale-event protection, and reducer unit coverage.

title: Design runtime-agnostic dashboard API and agent-runner transports
description:
WHY: The dashboard API currently runs as a local Node HTTP server in src/dashboard/server.js. That local server owns routing, auth, static asset serving, local filesystem access, EventSource/SSE behavior, durable .nax state reads, local child-process runner orchestration, cancellation, review-gate resume, follow-up submission, and Netlify CLI/API-adjacent operations. That shape is useful for a local cockpit, but it blocks the next direction: the same dashboard API should be runnable locally and inside a Netlify Function, with runtime-specific behavior supplied through adapters.

TARGET DIRECTION: Define a Hono-based dashboard API app that can be mounted in the existing local Node server and exported through a Netlify Function adapter. Route handlers should depend on explicit runtime, auth, storage, live-run, event, artifact, and runner-transport interfaces rather than importing local-only globals. Runner lifecycle work should go through swappable transports: a local process/in-process transport for developer machines and a direct Netlify API transport for hosted/function runtimes using a Netlify token.

SRC CLEANUP: This epic also owns the related top-level src/ sprawl cleanup. That cleanup must be tied to real boundary extraction: dashboard API/runtime/storage/transport files should move under src/dashboard/*, Netlify integration under src/netlify/*, shared runner modules under src/runners/*, workflow state/execution under src/workflow/*, artifacts under src/artifacts/*, and follow-up/handoff code under src/followup/* where appropriate. Do not perform a broad cosmetic file shuffle.

NON-GOALS: Do not break local dashboard behavior. Do not rewrite durable run pagination around Hono. Do not reintroduce listRunStates() then slice for /api/runs. Do not commit to hosted auth/session semantics before the design bead resolves them. Do not shell out to the Netlify CLI from hosted transports.

SOURCE PLAN: docs/ai/plans/nax-runtime-agnostic-dashboard-api.md.

ACCEPTANCE: The resulting implementation track gives future agents a self-contained migration path: route inventory, capability matrix, Hono app factory, runtime adapters, storage adapters, event/artifact boundaries, runner transport contracts, local behavior preservation, hosted placeholder behavior, direct Netlify API transport design, staged src/ ownership cleanup, tests, and validation.
design: Expanded planning-workflow architecture plan is in docs/ai/plans/nax-runtime-agnostic-dashboard-api.md. Bead graph generated from it on 2026-06-22: nax-boo3.1 through follow-on implementation tasks cover audit, Hono/runtime boundaries, runner transports, shared API primitives, src ownership inventory, local stores, read-only Hono extraction, live/event/transport/follow-up services, mutation routes, src cleanup, hosted placeholder runtime, Netlify API client/transport, hosted polling/follow-ups/artifacts, and final validation.
close_reason: Completed the runtime-agnostic dashboard API epic: all nax-boo3.1 through nax-boo3.23 child beads are closed, Hono/local/hosted transport implementation is in place, docs are updated, and validation passed.

title: Audit current dashboard server runtime assumptions
description:
SCOPE: Audit the current dashboard server runtime assumptions before any Hono extraction or hosted-runtime work. This is a design/inventory bead, not an implementation bead.

WHY: src/dashboard/server.js currently mixes HTTP routing, local Node server lifecycle, host-header protection, token auth, static asset serving, workflow discovery, durable .nax workflow state reads, event replay, SSE, child-process spawning, dry-run/resume, cancellation, review gates, follow-up planning, Netlify Blob delivery, local file-open, and Netlify CLI/API-adjacent operations. If we extract Hono routes without first classifying these dependencies, we will either recreate the monolith behind a new file name or accidentally break local dashboard behavior.

ROUTE INVENTORY REQUIRED: Produce a route table for every current endpoint: /api/health, /api/workflows, /api/workflows/:id, /api/workflows/:id/graph, /api/workflows/:id/dry-run, /api/workflows/:id/runs, /api/runs, /api/runs/:id, /api/runs/:id/graph, /api/runs/:id/details, /api/runs/:id/events, /api/runs/:id/events.json, /api/runs/:id/cancel, /api/runs/:id/review/approve, /api/runs/:id/review/cancel, /api/runs/:id/followups, /api/runs/:id/followups/cancel, /api/files/open, static dashboard assets, and fallback index HTML. For each route record method, current dependencies, local-only assumptions, target owner, hosted posture, capability requirement, and tests covering it.

SRC INVENTORY REQUIRED: Inventory top-level src/ files that dashboard code imports directly or indirectly, especially run-state.js, runner-event-log.js, workflow-runner.js, local-runner.js, followup-*.js, handoff-*.js, netlify-blobs.js, workflow-artifacts.js, agent-runner/session artifact files, status.js, types.js, and existing src/workflow, src/netlify, src/github directories. Assign each file a target domain or justify leaving it shared.

OUTPUT: A concrete audit document or implementation notes section under docs/ai/plans/ that can drive later beads without rediscovering server.js. Include a route-to-test inventory and a proposed file ownership map.

ACCEPTANCE: Every current dashboard route has a target owner. Every dashboard-adjacent root src/ file has a staged ownership answer. The audit explicitly calls out /api/runs pagination as a protected invariant: list durable workflow state pages before parsing workflow JSON; never call listRunStates() then slice for the runs list.
acceptance_criteria: Route inventory, runtime assumption map, src ownership inventory, test inventory, and pagination invariant are documented. No implementation beyond docs/types is required.
close_reason: Completed route/runtime assumption audit and src ownership planning in docs/ai/plans/nax-runtime-agnostic-dashboard-api.md; bead description now includes route inventory, runtime assumptions, src inventory requirements, test inventory, and /api/runs pagination invariant.

title: Move event replay and SSE behind event adapters
description:
SCOPE: Extract event replay and streaming behavior behind event-store/event-stream adapters so Hono is not coupled directly to Node ServerResponse.

WHY: /api/runs/:id/events.json is portable; /api/runs/:id/events SSE is runtime-sensitive. Local Node can stream active events and replay durable events from events.jsonl. Hosted functions may need polling first or a different streaming mechanism. The API should model that difference explicitly.

WORK: Keep events.json as the baseline portable route. Define event store listEvents({ runId, since }) returning events and parse errors. Define a local event stream adapter for SSE that can replay active/durable events and register active clients. Route code should check canStreamRunEvents and return event_stream_unavailable or equivalent typed error when streaming is unsupported.

COMPATIBILITY: Existing SSE event text format must remain event: <type> plus data JSON blocks. Durable replay errors should still surface as runner_event_error events. since filtering must remain compatible.

TESTS: Cover JSON event replay, active event replay, durable event replay, parse error reporting, since filtering, SSE content type/cache headers, unsupported hosted streaming error, and client close cleanup.

VALIDATION: event adapter tests, dashboard server/SSE tests, Hono app tests where applicable, npm run typecheck, npm run check:jsdoc-types, and git diff --check.
close_reason: Extracted local event stream adapter for active/durable SSE replay, moved server SSE route through it, kept events.json portable, added explicit Hono event_stream_unavailable response, and covered adapter behavior with tests.

title: Extract local process workflow runner transport
description:
SCOPE: Extract the normal dashboard workflow start path into a local process runner transport under src/dashboard/transports or another approved transport directory.

WHY: Starting a workflow from the dashboard currently shells out to process.execPath bin/nax.js with event fd wiring inside src/dashboard/server.js. That behavior should remain available locally, but route handlers should see it as a transport operation instead of inline child-process orchestration.

WORK: Move or wrap workflowCommand, spawn invocation, NAX_EVENT_FD/NAX_EVENT_STREAM setup, stdout/stderr bounding, runner-event parser integration, child close/error handling, SIGTERM/SIGKILL cancellation, terminal status mapping, and event sink emission into a local-process transport. Keep local dashboard duplicate-run semantics coordinated with the live run registry.

CONSTRAINTS: Do not change the CLI invocation, event JSONL format, output truncation behavior, status mapping, or cancellation timing unless a test explicitly covers and accepts the change. Do not add new root-level src files.

TESTS: Add transport unit tests with fake spawn/process dependencies for start event, stdout/stderr bounding, runner_event parse, runner_event_error, successful exit, failed exit, awaiting_review terminal status, cancel SIGTERM then SIGKILL fallback, and spawn error handling. Existing dashboard start-run tests must still pass.

VALIDATION: local process transport tests, dashboard server start/cancel tests, npm run typecheck, npm run check:jsdoc-types, npm run check, and git diff --check.
close_reason: Extracted normal dashboard workflow start into src/dashboard/transports/local-process.js with child-process spawn, event fd parsing, output bounding, status mapping, and cancellation; server now consumes the transport and focused transport tests cover the behavior.

title: Extract local in-process dry-run and review-resume transport
description:
SCOPE: Extract dashboard dry-run and human-review resume behavior into a local in-process transport or service boundary.

WHY: Normal workflow start currently uses a child process, while dry-run calls runWorkflow({ dryRun: true }) and review approval calls resumeWorkflow directly. These paths should be explicit transport capabilities so hosted mode can return unsupported behavior or later map to remote APIs without route-specific local imports.

WORK: Define dryRunWorkflow and resumeWorkflowRun/approveReviewGate operations. Preserve normalizeDryRunOptions validation, selected models/step models, target/site options, passthrough/tail behavior, reviewer/dashboard force options, stdout/stderr/status response shapes, and review-gate duplicate run protection.

BOUNDARY: Route handlers should not import runWorkflow or resumeWorkflow directly after this bead. Local implementation may still use them internally. Hosted runtime can omit these capabilities initially.

TESTS: Cover dry-run success/failure response shape, invalid transport/step/fromStep/model selection errors, review gate approval, missing gate, duplicate active workflow rejection, resume failure handling, and capability-disabled hosted responses.

VALIDATION: focused dry-run/review tests, dashboard server tests, npm run typecheck, npm run check:jsdoc-types, and git diff --check.
close_reason: Extracted dashboard dry-run and review resume calls into src/dashboard/transports/local-in-process.js, rewired server routes to use the transport functions, and added focused argument-mapping tests.

title: Extract dashboard cancellation and follow-up services
description:
SCOPE: Extract run cancellation, follow-up submission, follow-up cancellation, and review-gate cancellation into explicit services that compose stores, artifact access, durable state mutation, blob/context delivery, and runner transports.

WHY: These mutations are the hardest part of the hosted transition. They currently read local artifacts, build context packages, optionally write Netlify Blobs via CLI-oriented helpers, submit runner/session work, mutate workflow.json, persist pseudo-workflows, stop remote runners, append durable events, and update local live events directly in route code. Hono routes should call cohesive services instead.

WORK: Create service boundaries for cancelRun, cancelReviewGate, submitFollowup, and cancelFollowup. Preserve applyRemoteCancelToWorkflow semantics, remote_cancel_requested event behavior, abandoned-vs-cancelled honesty, follow-up context package selection, blob/offload behavior, buildFollowupSubmissionPlan, buildFollowupPrompt, appendFollowupRunsToWorkflow, persistFreshPseudoWorkflow, cancelFollowupRunInWorkflow, warnings, and response DTOs.

BOUNDARY: Services should depend on artifact store, run store, event store/live registry, and runner transport interfaces. Local services may still call existing modules internally. Hosted services can initially return unsupported capability where storage/transport is missing.

TESTS: Cover cancellation of active local runs, durable-only cancellation, partial remote stop warnings, follow-up submission with inline and blob delivery, fresh pseudo-workflow persistence, source workflow append, follow-up cancel by runner/session, missing follow-up target, missing prompt, missing runner/session cancel target, and review cancel.

VALIDATION: focused service tests, dashboard server mutation tests, npm run typecheck, npm run check:jsdoc-types, npm run check, and git diff --check.
close_reason: Extracted dashboard mutation services for run cancel, review cancel, follow-up submit, and follow-up cancel; routes now delegate to service functions and service/dashboard mutation tests pass.

title: Move dashboard mutation routes into Hono
description:
SCOPE: Move mutation routes into the Hono API app after local transports and services exist.

WHY: Once read-only Hono routes, live registry, event adapters, local transports, and mutation services are in place, Hono can own the full /api route surface. This is the point where the old Node handler becomes mainly a local runtime/static wrapper.

ROUTES: Move /api/workflows/:id/dry-run, /api/workflows/:id/runs, /api/runs/:id/cancel, /api/runs/:id/review/approve, /api/runs/:id/review/cancel, /api/runs/:id/followups, /api/runs/:id/followups/cancel, and /api/files/open. Each route must check capability before invoking local-only behavior.

COMPATIBILITY: Route paths, methods, status codes, response bodies, auth requirements, duplicate-run behavior, validation errors, and warnings must remain compatible. /api/files/open remains local-only and should return typed unsupported_capability in hosted mode.

TESTS: Add Hono mutation route tests with fake services/transports and keep existing local dashboard server mutation tests passing. Cover auth, method not allowed, unsupported capabilities, validation errors, start/dry-run/cancel/review/follow-up success, and service error mapping.

VALIDATION: Hono route tests, dashboard server tests, npm run typecheck, npm run check:jsdoc-types, npm run check, npm run dashboard:build if web behavior changes, and dashboard smoke if route integration changed enough to warrant it.
close_reason: Moved dashboard mutation route ownership into createDashboardApi with injected local mutation services, delegated POST mutation paths from the local server to Hono, forwarded POST bodies through the Node/Hono bridge, and added Hono mutation route tests.

title: Move dashboard-owned helpers into dashboard module directories
description:
SCOPE: Move obvious dashboard-owned helper code into src/dashboard/api, src/dashboard/runtime, src/dashboard/storage, and src/dashboard/transports after those boundaries exist.

WHY: The plan explicitly includes cleaning top-level src/ sprawl as part of runtime/API extraction. Once dashboard-owned helpers have real homes, leaving them in src/ root or server.js makes dependency direction harder to enforce.

WORK: Move only code that is clearly dashboard-owned: API errors/serializers/request helpers/auth helpers/capabilities, local dashboard runtime wrapper, local dashboard stores, local dashboard transports, event stream adapters, live run registry, and local file-open route utility if it is dashboard-only. Do not move shared workflow, artifact, follow-up, Netlify, or runner modules in this bead unless they are private to dashboard.

COMPATIBILITY: Update imports carefully. If external tests or CLI code depend on an old location, either avoid the move or add a temporary shim with a follow-up removal note. Do not create circular imports from shared modules back into dashboard modules.

TESTS: Add or update import-boundary tests so dashboard API code cannot import local-only root modules directly once adapters exist. Existing dashboard server/Hono tests must pass.

VALIDATION: import-boundary scan if present, npm run check, npm run check:jsdoc-types, npm test or focused equivalent depending on blast radius, and git diff --check.
close_reason: Moved the dashboard-local file opener into src/dashboard/runtime/local-files.js, wired server usage to the runtime helper, and added tests for local file safety plus portable API import boundaries; focused tests and repo checks passed.

title: Relocate shared domain modules from src root in small batches
description:
SCOPE: Relocate shared top-level src/ modules into domain directories in small, compatibility-preserving batches after the dashboard/runtime extraction clarifies ownership.

WHY: The desired end state is not an empty src/ root; it is a boring src/ root where remaining files are intentionally shared primitives. Modules with obvious domain ownership should live under directories such as src/netlify, src/workflow, src/artifacts, src/followup, src/runners, src/github, or src/flows.

CANDIDATE MOVES: netlify-blobs.js and netlify-runtime.js toward src/netlify; workflow-runner.js/workflow-events.js/state helpers toward src/workflow or src/workflow/state; agent-runner artifacts/session artifacts/workflow-artifacts toward src/artifacts; followup-context/delivery/persistence/plan and handoff-runner/sources toward src/followup or src/handoff; local-runner/agent-runner-sync/agent-run-results toward src/runners or src/agent-runner; gh-cli/github-actions-sync toward src/github; flows/prompts/issue-groups only after CLI import blast radius is understood.

RULES: Move one coherent domain cluster at a time. Preserve import compatibility with shims when necessary. Remove shims only in explicit later cleanup work. Do not mix behavior changes with file moves. Do not introduce circular dependencies.

TESTS: For each move batch, run focused tests for the moved domain plus import smoke tests for CLI and dashboard. Add boundary scans when stable.

VALIDATION: npm run check, npm run check:jsdoc-types, npm test or focused suite covering moved modules, CLI help smoke where CLI imports changed, dashboard tests where dashboard imports changed, and git diff --check.
close_reason: Moved Netlify-owned root modules into src/netlify/blobs.js and src/netlify/runtime.js, updated internal imports and tests to domain paths, kept root compatibility shims, and validated with focused Netlify/dashboard/CLI tests plus repo checks.

title: Add hosted placeholder runtime and Netlify Function wrapper
description:
SCOPE: Add a Netlify Function runtime adapter that can mount the same Hono app with explicit hosted placeholder capabilities and unsupported storage/transport implementations.

WHY: Before direct Netlify API mutations exist, we still need to prove the Hono API can run in a function runtime without local Node server assumptions. A hosted placeholder runtime validates dependency injection, capability reporting, structured unsupported errors, and function handler packaging.

WORK: Add src/dashboard/runtime/netlify-function.js or equivalent wrapper. Provide hosted runtime context, hosted auth placeholder, hosted unsupported workflow/run/event/artifact stores, and no-op/unsupported runner transport. Wire /api/health to report the hosted capability set. Unsupported routes should return typed errors such as unsupported_capability or hosted_storage_unavailable instead of throwing local filesystem/process errors.

NON-GOALS: Do not implement real hosted storage or runner mutations in this bead. Do not choose final hosted auth if still unresolved; document placeholder behavior and blocked decisions. Do not serve dashboard static assets from the function unless explicitly required.

TESTS: Add function-adapter tests that instantiate the Hono app in hosted mode and assert /api/health works, local-only routes are unavailable with typed errors, no local projectRoot leaks, and read routes fail through hosted_storage_unavailable where no source is configured.

VALIDATION: hosted adapter tests, Hono app tests, npm run typecheck, npm run check:jsdoc-types, npm run check, and git diff --check.
close_reason: Added hosted placeholder Netlify Function wrapper around the shared Hono app with hosted capabilities, typed unsupported/local-storage errors, no projectRoot leak, and function adapter tests.

title: Design and test direct Netlify API client
description:
SCOPE: Build the first reusable direct Netlify API client design and mocked contract tests needed by the hosted runner transport.

WHY: Hosted dashboard runtimes cannot shell out to the Netlify CLI. The direct API path must be explicit, typed, and testable before production start/cancel/follow-up mutations are enabled.

WORK: Identify and document required Netlify API endpoints for Agent Runner create/start, session/follow-up, cancel/archive, status/read, usage, file changes, artifacts, and links. Define token source and precedence, required scopes, site/project ID resolution, base URL/version, request/response normalization, rate-limit/retry handling, timeout behavior, and structured error mapping. Implement a thin client only where endpoint semantics are known enough to test; otherwise leave explicit TODO/open-question notes. Reusable Netlify API code should live under src/netlify, while dashboard-only transport glue should live under src/dashboard/transports.

ERRORS: Map auth failures, permission failures, rate limits, not found, validation failures, and transient Netlify API errors into dashboard error codes such as runner_auth_failed, runner_rate_limited, runner_transport_error, and hosted_storage_unavailable.

TESTS: Use mocked fetch/API responses. Cover request construction, auth headers, site/project ID validation, create/start normalization, cancel/archive normalization, status normalization, rate-limit handling, retry/no-retry choices, redaction of tokens in errors, and unknown response fields preserving useful diagnostics without using any types.

VALIDATION: Netlify API client tests, npm run typecheck, npm run check:jsdoc-types, npm run check, and git diff --check.
close_reason: Added src/netlify/api-client.js with injectable fetch, token/site validation, normalized Agent Runner methods, retry/error mapping, token redaction, mocked tests, and documented provisional endpoint contract.

title: Implement first hosted Netlify API start and cancel transport path
description:
SCOPE: Implement the first production-shaped hosted transport path for starting and cancelling Netlify Agent Runner work through direct Netlify API calls.

WHY: This is the moment the runtime-agnostic API becomes more than a refactor. Hosted start/cancel must use the same Hono API route surface as local mode, but underneath it should use the Netlify API transport rather than local process spawning or Netlify CLI subprocesses.

WORK: Implement startWorkflowRun and cancelRun in the Netlify API transport for the smallest supported runner workflow shape. Define idempotency keys to prevent duplicate submissions. Map remote runner/session IDs, statuses, links, usage/file-change summaries when available, and errors into dashboard DTOs. Respect hosted capabilities and token/site configuration. Preserve local transport behavior.

LIMITS: If multi-step workflows, review gates, or follow-ups cannot yet be represented directly, return explicit unsupported capability or validation errors for hosted mode rather than pretending they work. Document what subset is enabled.

TESTS: Add hosted transport tests with mocked Netlify API for successful start, duplicate/idempotent submission, missing token/site, auth failure, rate limit, validation error, cancel success, cancel not found, cancel already terminal, and structured error mapping. Add Hono mutation route tests using the hosted transport.

VALIDATION: hosted transport tests, Hono mutation tests, npm run typecheck, npm run check:jsdoc-types, npm run check, and git diff --check.
close_reason: Implemented hosted Netlify API start/cancel transport with idempotent starts, hosted run DTOs, direct client integration in Netlify function runtime, structured error propagation, and mocked hosted transport/Hono tests.

title: Specify Hono API app and runtime adapter boundaries
description:
SCOPE: Specify the Hono API app and runtime adapter boundaries for the dashboard. This is the design contract that later extraction beads implement.

WHY: The dashboard needs one stable API surface that can run locally and in a Netlify Function. The Hono app should own HTTP concerns: route definitions, method checks, body/query parsing, auth middleware, capability checks, structured errors, response serialization, and security headers. It must not directly import local-only modules such as fs, http, child_process, open, run-state helpers, Netlify CLI helpers, or local project-root state.

DESIGN REQUIREMENTS: Specify createDashboardApi({ runtime, auth, workflowStore, runStore, eventStore, artifactStore, liveRuns, runnerTransport, followupService, reviewService, logger, clock }). Specify DashboardRuntime with mode, deploymentMode, capabilities, env, optional projectRoot, logger, and clock. Specify local-node and netlify-function adapters. Static asset serving should remain outside the core Hono API at first.

CAPABILITY MODEL: Define stable capability names such as canListWorkflows, canReadRuns, canReadRunDetails, canReadEventsJson, canStreamRunEvents, canStartRuns, canDryRun, canCancelRuns, canSubmitFollowups, canReviewGates, canOpenLocalFiles, and canServeStaticAssets. Hosted unsupported behavior must return typed unsupported_capability or hosted_storage_unavailable style errors rather than crashing through local-only code.

AUTH MODEL: Preserve local token/query/header/cookie behavior, but define auth as a dependency so hosted auth can later use Netlify/team/session/signed URL semantics. Do not choose hosted auth prematurely; record options and blocked decisions.

OUTPUT: Proposed module boundaries under src/dashboard/api, src/dashboard/runtime, src/dashboard/storage, and src/dashboard/transports; typed JSDoc contracts; migration order for read-only routes, event routes, mutations, hosted placeholders, and Netlify Function adapter.

ACCEPTANCE: Later agents can implement the Hono app incrementally without a flag-day rewrite. The design explains how the existing local server mounts Hono while continuing to serve static assets and preserve local dashboard behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Hono app factory, runtime/auth/capability contracts, static-serving split, hosted unsupported behavior, and incremental migration sequence are specified.
close_reason: Specified Hono app factory, runtime/auth/capability contracts, static-serving split, hosted unsupported behavior, and incremental migration sequence in the expanded runtime-agnostic dashboard API plan and bead text.

title: Implement hosted run polling and status mapping
description:
SCOPE: Add hosted run polling/status mapping so hosted dashboard users can observe remote Agent Runner work after starting it.

WHY: Starting remote work is not useful unless the dashboard can show status, links, usage, file changes, and terminal outcomes. Streaming may not be available in a Netlify Function, so polling/event JSON should be the initial portable model.

WORK: Implement hosted read/status operations through the Netlify API client and/or hosted state source. Normalize remote statuses into dashboard run statuses and runner events. Decide how /api/runs, /api/runs/:id, /api/runs/:id/events.json, and possibly /api/runs/:id/graph behave for hosted runs before durable workflow snapshots exist. Add polling-friendly response metadata if needed while preserving existing client compatibility.

EVENT MODEL: Prefer JSON polling first. If SSE/function streaming remains uncertain, keep canStreamRunEvents false and return event_stream_unavailable for /events while /events.json works.

TESTS: Cover status mapping for queued/running/submitted/completed/failed/cancelled/canceled/timeout/unknown, usage/file-change/link normalization, events.json polling, missing remote run, rate-limited polling, and client-compatible response shapes.

VALIDATION: hosted polling tests, Hono read route tests, dashboard query tests if client behavior changes, npm run typecheck, npm run check:jsdoc-types, npm run check, npm run dashboard:build for UI changes, and git diff --check.
close_reason: Implemented hosted Netlify API polling/status mapping for runs, events.json polling, Hono read routes, and hosted capability wiring; validated with hosted transport/function/API tests plus repo checks.

title: Implement hosted follow-up context delivery and submission
description:
SCOPE: Enable hosted follow-up submission once direct Netlify API transport and hosted status reads exist.

WHY: Dashboard follow-ups are central to the workflow: users select prior artifacts/context and submit fresh runner work or follow-up sessions. Hosted mode cannot rely on local absolute artifact paths or local Netlify CLI blob writes, so context delivery and artifact references must be remote-safe.

WORK: Define hosted follow-up context package creation using remote artifact IDs, URLs, Netlify Blobs, or another hosted storage source. Submit follow-up sessions/runners through direct Netlify API calls. Map results into the same follow-up response shape where possible. Persist enough hosted state to show follow-up relationships and warnings. Keep local follow-up behavior unchanged.

CONSTRAINTS: Do not expose local absolute paths in hosted responses. Do not shell out to the Netlify CLI. If a follow-up mode is unsupported by the API, return a clear unsupported capability/validation error. Preserve prompt/context size safety and blob delivery semantics where applicable.

TESTS: Mock hosted artifact/context sources and Netlify API follow-up calls. Cover missing prompt, missing target, artifact selection, blob/remote context delivery, fresh-runner follow-up, session/thread follow-up where supported, API failure warnings, persisted relationship metadata, and unsupported mode errors.

VALIDATION: hosted follow-up tests, local follow-up regression tests, Hono mutation tests, npm run typecheck, npm run check:jsdoc-types, npm run check, npm run dashboard:build if UI behavior changes, and git diff --check.
close_reason: Implemented hosted fresh-runner follow-up submission and cancellation through the Netlify API transport, remote-safe context/artifact metadata, hosted capability wiring, validation/unsupported-mode errors, and Hono route coverage; local follow-up regressions and repo checks passed.

title: Add hosted artifact and run-details source
description:
SCOPE: Add a hosted artifact/details source so /api/runs/:id/details and graph/detail UI can work for hosted runs without local filesystem assumptions.

WHY: Local run details are built from files under .nax/workflows/.../artifacts and include local absolute paths for open/copy actions. Hosted mode needs stable artifact IDs, URLs, or blob keys instead. Without this, hosted runs can start and poll but cannot provide a useful details workflow.

WORK: Define and implement hosted artifact storage/read behavior using the chosen source: Netlify Agent Runner/session artifact APIs, Netlify Blobs, workflow callbacks, or a hybrid. Normalize hosted artifacts into existing dashboard details sections, follow-up targets, and follow-up artifact selections. Mark local-only operations such as /api/files/open unavailable.

COMPATIBILITY: Existing local buildRunDetails behavior must remain unchanged. Hosted responses should avoid local absolute paths and should include enough stable IDs for follow-up context selection.

TESTS: Cover hosted detail response shape, artifact lists, markdown/text snippets if available, usage/file-change details, follow-up targets/artifacts, missing artifact source, unauthorized artifact read, and local-only file-open unavailable behavior.

VALIDATION: hosted artifact storage tests, run details route tests, dashboard UI tests if rendering changes, npm run typecheck, npm run check:jsdoc-types, npm run check, npm run dashboard:build for UI changes, and git diff --check.
close_reason: Implemented hosted run details and graph sources from Netlify API runner payloads, remote-safe artifact normalization/catalog expansion for follow-ups, hosted canReadRunDetails wiring, and tests for details, graph, artifact refs, unauthorized/local file-open limits, and local regressions; repo checks passed.

title: End-to-end validation and documentation for runtime-agnostic dashboard API
description:
SCOPE: Final validation/documentation bead for the runtime-agnostic dashboard API and src cleanup track. Close this only after the design, extraction, hosted placeholder, and initial hosted transport/storage tasks are complete or intentionally split into a new epic.

WHY: This epic touches routing, auth, local server behavior, Hono, storage adapters, event streaming, runner transports, hosted runtime behavior, Netlify API calls, dashboard UI expectations, and source tree organization. It needs an explicit closeout pass to verify no behavior regressed and future contributors know the new boundaries.

WORK: Update docs/ai/plans/nax-runtime-agnostic-dashboard-api.md to reflect implemented phases. Add or update user/developer docs for local vs hosted capabilities, Hono app factory, runtime adapters, storage adapters, runner transports, Netlify Function deployment, Netlify token/scope requirements, unsupported hosted behavior, and src/ ownership rules. Verify bead statuses/dependencies.

VALIDATION CHECKLIST: /api/health, workflows, runs pagination, run detail/graph, events.json, SSE local stream, dry-run, start, cancel, review approve/cancel, follow-up submit/cancel, local file open, hosted placeholder errors, direct Netlify API hosted start/cancel if implemented, hosted polling, hosted details/artifacts if implemented, import-boundary guardrails, and src cleanup shims.

COMMANDS: npm run typecheck, npm run check:jsdoc-types, npm run check, npm test or documented focused equivalent if full test cost is prohibitive, npm run dashboard:build for any UI/client changes, dashboard smoke if API integration changed, br dep cycles --json, and git diff --check.

ACCEPTANCE: The epic can be closed without needing the original markdown plan. The route surface is documented, local behavior is preserved, hosted limitations are explicit, and remaining future work is represented by separate beads rather than hidden TODOs.
close_reason: Completed closeout documentation and validation for the runtime-agnostic dashboard API: Hono route ownership, local adapters, hosted Netlify Function/API transport, src ownership rules, full npm test, dashboard build/smoke, import-direction, dependency cycle, JSDoc/type/check, and diff hygiene all passed.

title: Specify agent-runner transport interface and Netlify API transport
description:
SCOPE: Specify the agent-runner transport interface and direct Netlify API transport design. This is the transport contract that later local and hosted mutation beads implement.

WHY: The dashboard currently starts and manages runner work through local CLI/local process behavior. That is acceptable for a local dashboard but wrong for a hosted Netlify Function. Hosted runtime must use direct Netlify API calls with a Netlify token, not shelling out to the Netlify CLI. The HTTP route layer must not know which transport is used.

TRANSPORT CONTRACT: Define AgentRunnerTransport with kind, capabilities, startWorkflowRun, optional dryRunWorkflow, optional resumeWorkflowRun, cancelRun, optional submitFollowup, optional cancelFollowup, optional approveReviewGate, and optional cancelReviewGate. Define input/output DTOs with precise JSDoc shapes and structured error mapping. Keep storage out of the transport except where the transport returns remote IDs/statuses/artifacts that a store or service can persist.

LOCAL TRANSPORTS: Specify local-process transport for normal workflow starts. It should preserve workflowCommand, process.execPath bin/nax.js, NAX_EVENT_FD, NAX_EVENT_STREAM=jsonl, bounded stdout/stderr, runner-event JSONL parsing, SIGTERM/SIGKILL cancellation, duplicate-run semantics, and current dashboard event behavior. Specify local-in-process transport for dry-run and review resume because those currently call runWorkflow/resumeWorkflow directly.

NETLIFY API TRANSPORT: Specify direct API endpoint needs, token source/precedence, token scopes, site/project identifier resolution, request base URL/version, create/start payload mapping, session/follow-up mapping, cancel/archive mapping, polling/streaming strategy, rate-limit/retry behavior, idempotency keys, remote ID mapping, links, usage, file changes, artifacts, and dashboard error codes.

OUTPUT: A design note or plan update that future implementation beads can execute without rediscovering Netlify API semantics. Include what is still unknown and what must be spiked before production hosted mutations.

ACCEPTANCE: The team can implement local process transport extraction and a direct Netlify API client/transport independently of Hono routing.
acceptance_criteria: Transport interface, local process/in-process semantics, direct Netlify API requirements, error model, idempotency, status mapping, and open questions are specified.
close_reason: Specified agent-runner transport interface direction, local process/in-process transport semantics, direct Netlify API transport requirements, error mapping, idempotency, status mapping, and open questions in the expanded plan and bead text.

title: Extract shared dashboard API primitives
description:
SCOPE: Extract shared API primitives that the current Node server and future Hono app can both use, without changing route behavior.

WHY: Hono migration should not start by copying requestError, errorPayload, token/cookie helpers, security headers, body parsing, capability construction, and serializers into a second implementation. Shared primitives reduce risk and let tests lock compatibility before routes move.

WORK: Create dashboard-owned modules under src/dashboard/api/ for structured errors, response payloads, request parsing helpers, local token/session auth helpers, capability description, and serializers such as publicFlow, publicRunState, publicRunOptions, and related DTO helpers. Do not add new top-level src/ files. Keep Node-specific code isolated where necessary.

COMPATIBILITY: Existing error response shape must remain { error: { statusCode, code, message } }. Existing local token behavior must remain query token -> x-nax-token -> cookie bootstrap. Existing security headers should remain equivalent for API responses.

TESTS: Add or update unit tests for error payloads, request errors, session cookie/header helpers, auth token comparison, capability construction, and serializers. Existing dashboard server tests should continue to pass.

VALIDATION: node --test focused dashboard-server/API primitive tests, npm run typecheck, npm run check:jsdoc-types, and git diff --check.
close_reason: Extracted dashboard API errors, security headers, auth/session helpers, JSON body parsing, and serializers into src/dashboard/api/*; server.js now imports them while preserving existing _private test surface. Added dashboard-api-primitives unit coverage. Focused dashboard API/server tests and npm run typecheck pass.

title: Create src ownership inventory and boundary guardrails
description:
SCOPE: Turn the top-level src/ sprawl cleanup from a planning note into an actionable ownership map and lightweight guardrail strategy.

WHY: The runtime-agnostic dashboard API work will create new boundaries. If new code continues importing broad root-level modules directly, the system will remain coupled to local Node behavior. But a mass move would create churn and risk. We need a staged map that tells agents which modules move with which boundary.

WORK: Produce a documented inventory of top-level src/ files and assign each to one of: intentionally shared root primitive, src/dashboard/api, src/dashboard/runtime, src/dashboard/storage, src/dashboard/transports, src/workflow, src/workflow/state, src/runners, src/artifacts, src/followup, src/netlify, src/github, src/flows, or deferred/needs audit. Identify imports that need temporary compatibility shims. Add a lightweight import-boundary test or script only after the target directories exist enough to make enforcement meaningful.

RULES: Do not move files for aesthetics. Move only when an implementation bead establishes the boundary. No new dashboard API/runtime/storage/transport files should be added directly under src/. Shared workflow modules must not import dashboard API modules.

TESTS: If adding a boundary test, cover at least that src/dashboard/api does not import child_process, open, local-runner, netlify-blobs, run-state, or filesystem modules directly once adapters exist.

VALIDATION: Documentation/inventory reviewed, any added scan passes, npm run check if code/test changes are included, and git diff --check.
close_reason: Added docs/ai/plans/nax-src-ownership-inventory.md with top-level src ownership map, staged move timing, guardrail strategy, compatibility shim strategy, and current intentional exceptions; linked it from the runtime-agnostic dashboard API plan.

title: Add local dashboard storage adapters
description:
SCOPE: Add local storage adapters for workflow, run, event, and artifact reads while preserving existing local behavior and response shapes.

WHY: Runtime-agnostic routes cannot read local files directly. The local Node runtime can still use project files and .nax/workflows, but those reads should live behind explicit stores so the same Hono route code can later run with hosted stores.

WORK: Add src/dashboard/storage/local-workflows.js around listFlows, loadFlow, public workflow serialization, and flowToGraph. Add local-runs.js around paged durable runs, active/durable lookup coordination where appropriate, run graph, and run details. Add local-events.js around durable event replay and active event reads. Add local-artifacts.js around buildRunDetails and follow-up context package creation where read-only artifact access belongs.

CRITICAL INVARIANT: The /api/runs list path must continue to page before parsing durable workflow JSON. The local run store listRunsPage must use the paged durable state helper or equivalent and must not call listRunStates(projectRoot) then slice.

SIDE EFFECTS: Existing run graph/details logic currently syncs submitted follow-ups as a read side effect. Preserve behavior initially, but document that side effect and isolate it so a later hosted store can choose a different synchronization model.

TESTS: Add local adapter unit tests for workflow list/get/graph, runs first page/cursor metadata, run lookup, graph fallback to persisted flow, details/artifact behavior, event replay, invalid/missing run behavior, and the pagination parse-only-selected invariant.

VALIDATION: focused adapter tests, dashboard server tests, npm run typecheck, npm run check:jsdoc-types, and git diff --check.
close_reason: Added local workflow, run, event, and artifact storage adapters under src/dashboard/storage. Local run store preserves /api/runs pagination before workflow JSON parsing, supports durable run graph/details, and event replay. Added dashboard-local-storage unit coverage. Focused storage/server tests and npm run typecheck pass.

title: Add Hono dependency and read-only dashboard API app
description:
SCOPE: Introduce the Hono dependency and create a read-only createDashboardApi app that can serve read-only dashboard routes through injected stores and runtime/auth dependencies.

WHY: The first Hono implementation should be small enough to verify. Read-only routes prove the route factory, auth middleware, structured errors, capability checks, store injection, and response compatibility without touching child-process lifecycle or mutation complexity.

ROUTES: Implement Hono handlers for /api/health, /api/workflows, /api/workflows/:id, /api/workflows/:id/graph, /api/runs, /api/runs/:id, /api/runs/:id/graph, /api/runs/:id/details, and /api/runs/:id/events.json. Keep /api/runs/:id/events SSE and all mutation routes in the existing local server until their adapters exist.

CONSTRAINTS: Hono route modules must not import fs, http, child_process, open, local-runner, run-state, netlify-blobs, or other local-only modules directly. They should use runtime/auth/store/liveRuns/event dependencies. Response shapes and error codes must match the current server.

TESTS: Add Hono app tests using app.request() or fetch-style requests for auth required, health capability metadata, workflow list/get/graph, runs pagination metadata, run details, events.json, not-found, method-not-allowed, invalid cursor, and unsupported capability behavior with a fake hosted context.

VALIDATION: Hono app tests, existing dashboard server tests, npm run typecheck, npm run check:jsdoc-types, npm run check, and git diff --check.
close_reason: Added Hono dependency and read-only createDashboardApi with injected stores, auth, capability checks, structured errors, live/durable read routes, tests, and full JS/type validation.

title: Mount read-only Hono API inside the local dashboard server
description:
SCOPE: Mount the read-only Hono API app from the existing local Node dashboard server while preserving static asset serving, fallback HTML, host-header checks, token bootstrap, live local state, and non-Hono routes.

WHY: The migration must not be a flag-day rewrite. The local server can keep owning http.createServer, static assets, fallback index HTML, and routes not yet moved, while forwarding read-only /api routes to Hono.

WORK: Wire createRequestHandler or a new local runtime wrapper so read-only routes are handled by the Hono app with local stores, local auth, local runtime capabilities, and the live run registry. Preserve sessionBootstrapHeadersForRequest behavior so query-token dashboard launch still sets the cookie. Keep SSE and mutation routes in the old handler for now.

COMPATIBILITY: Existing client API calls should not need path changes. Security headers should remain equivalent. Host-header rejection must still happen before API handling in local mode. Static assets and fallback index behavior must remain unchanged.

TESTS: Extend dashboard server tests to prove read-only routes still work through the local server mount, host-header and auth behavior are unchanged, static assets still serve, fallback index still works, and old mutation/SSE routes still function while read-only Hono routes are mounted.

VALIDATION: tests/unit/dashboard-server.test.js, Hono app tests, npm run typecheck, npm run check:jsdoc-types, npm run dashboard:build if dashboard web behavior changes, dashboard smoke if route integration is risky, and git diff --check.
close_reason: Mounted the read-only Hono API inside createRequestHandler after host validation, injected local stores and live run access, preserved legacy health/session behavior, and verified old static/mutation/SSE server tests still pass.

title: Extract local live run registry
description:
SCOPE: Extract the local live run registry currently embedded in createRequestHandler into an explicit local runtime service.

WHY: The current runs map mixes active run DTOs, stdout/stderr windows, bounded event history, SSE clients, cancel functions, active-by-workflow duplicate protection, timers, and finished-run eviction. Hono route handlers and hosted mode should not assume that active runs live in a local map.

WORK: Create a local live run registry with operations such as listActiveRuns, getActiveRun, reserveWorkflow/start tracking, recordEvent, recordRunnerEvent, registerSseClient or stream hooks, cancelLocalRun, evictFinishedRuns, and shutdown. Preserve MAX_LIVE_OUTPUT_CHARS, MAX_LIVE_EVENTS, MAX_FINISHED_RUNS semantics. Keep duplicate workflow run protection intact.

BOUNDARY: The registry may be local-only and Node-aware where necessary, but Hono routes should interact through its interface. Hosted mode can later provide an empty or remote-backed registry.

TESTS: Add unit tests for event ordering, bounded event window, stdout/stderr truncation, duplicate run reservation, eviction, client registration/drop behavior if exposed, shutdown cleanup, and public run serialization compatibility. Existing dashboard event bridge/server tests should still pass.

VALIDATION: focused live registry tests, dashboard server tests, npm run typecheck, npm run check:jsdoc-types, and git diff --check.
close_reason: Extracted local live run registry into src/dashboard/runtime/live-run-registry.js, rewired createRequestHandler to use it for active runs, events, SSE clients, shutdown, and eviction, and added focused registry tests.

title: nax schedule: generate cron GitHub Actions workflows for flows
description:
WHAT: nax schedule <flow> --cron '0 6 * * 1' [--branch main] [--var k=v] generates .github/workflows/nax-scheduled-<flow>.yml: a cron-triggered workflow that checks out the repo and dispatches the existing run-nax machinery (same template family as src/templates/ GitHub Actions workflow, reusing repo secrets wired by nax init). Includes workflow_dispatch trigger too, so scheduled flows are manually runnable from the Actions tab.

GUARDRAILS: requires github-actions transport to be initialized (doctor check); --list shows existing scheduled flows; --remove <flow> deletes the file; generated yml carries a header comment 'generated by nax schedule, regenerate rather than hand-edit'. Cron string validated (5-field) with next-3-occurrences preview at confirm time so users catch timezone/cadence mistakes ('next runs: Mon Jun 15 06:00 UTC...').

WHY: audit flows (security/seo/a11y/perf) are periodic by nature — weekly security-audit posting consensus issues is the obvious standing-guard use; artifact lands via the existing nax sync path. This converts nax from on-demand tool to continuous practice with ~zero new runtime surface (GitHub owns the scheduler).

TESTS (tests/unit/schedule.test.js): generated yml golden-file per flag combo (yamllint-valid, cron preserved, vars serialized, dispatch input wiring), cron validation rejects 6-field/garbage with examples in error, --list/--remove round-trip in tmp repos, refusal when GH transport unwired names doctor/init.

ACCEPTANCE: nax schedule security-audit --cron '0 6 * * 1' creates a valid workflow file; gh workflow list shows it; nax schedule --list reports it.

title: Dashboard single-source run state and durable-authoritative status
description:
Source plan: docs/ai/plans/dashboard-run-status-single-source.md

Goal: replace the dashboard's split active/durable/live/event status model with one clean, durable-authoritative run-state model that scales without small stale-state bugs.

Core decisions from the spec:
- Durable run records are the storage source of truth.
- The live registry is a sidecar for streams, cancellation handles, and very-new booting rows only.
- Public dashboard responses must be produced through centralized projection helpers, not endpoint-local status logic.
- Remote agent run data is the best authority when durable state is contradictory, but reads must stay local-first and avoid unbounded API calls.
- /api/runs should collapse to a clean { runs, pagination } shape; do not preserve the old active/durable response model as compatibility baggage.
- Events can animate live surfaces and invalidate queries, but persistent run list/detail status must refetch durable state instead of accepting event payload status as authority.

Primary primitives to implement:
- markRunCompleted(runState): the single write path for completed workflow state.
- projectRunSnapshot(runState, options): the single read-side projection for workflow status, step statuses, agent-run statuses, timestamps, resumable/cancellable flags, and diagnostics.
- refreshRunStateIfNeeded(runState, context): bounded remote refresh when durable data is stale or internally contradictory.
- isWorkflowActive(flowId): centralized duplicate-run guard based on live registry plus projected durable state.

Done when dashboard list/detail/graph/event metadata, reconciliation, duplicate-run guard, and completion writes all use these primitives, tests cover stale/contradictory states, and npm run dashboard:build passes after UI/client changes.
close_reason: All child tasks are closed. Implemented durable-authoritative projected run snapshots, centralized completion writes, bounded remote refresh, collapsed /api/runs shape, durable-first detail/graph/details reads, cache invalidation semantics, projected reconciliation, centralized active-run guarding, process/event lifecycle cleanup, and full validation.

title: Add projectRunSnapshot read model for run-state projection
description:
Implement the central read projection primitive from the plan.

Scope:
- Add projectRunSnapshot(runState, options) in the shared server-side run-state module chosen by the implementation.
- Projection must cover workflow status, normalized step statuses, nested agent run statuses, started/completed/updated timestamps, resumable/cancellable flags, and diagnostics explaining contradictions.
- Replace publicRunState's status derivation with this projection instead of endpoint-specific logic.
- Terminal stored workflow status should win when it is coherent; non-terminal stored status can be upgraded to terminal when steps and agent runs prove completion/failure/cancellation.
- If nested remote agent runs contradict top-level durable status, emit a diagnostic and preserve enough information for refreshRunStateIfNeeded to decide whether remote refresh is required.

Acceptance:
- There is one read-side status derivation helper for dashboard/public responses.
- List, detail, graph, and reconcile code can consume the same projected shape without re-deriving top-level status.
- Tests cover completed top-level with running nested agent, running top-level with completed steps, failed/cancelled steps, resumable flags, and timestamp choice.
- No JSDoc any types are introduced.
close_reason: Implemented projectRunSnapshot projection, wired publicRunState through it, and added status-conflict projection tests.

title: Validate dashboard single-source run state end to end
description:
Add end-to-end validation for the completed single-source dashboard run-state model.

Scope:
- Run focused unit/integration tests for projection, refresh, endpoints, client cache invalidation, duplicate guard, and completion writes.
- Run npm run dashboard:build after all dashboard/client changes.
- Perform a local dashboard smoke test covering: run starts as booting, transitions to running, terminal status persists after refresh/restart, detail and list agree, graph/details agree, duplicate guard clears stale terminal live state, and pagination has no duplicate booting rows.
- Record any residual manual verification notes in the implementation PR or bead comments.

Acceptance:
- Automated tests and dashboard build pass.
- The stale list/detail status bugs described in the source plan are not reproducible.
- Restarting the dashboard/server does not change the displayed terminal state for completed runs.
- No active/durable response shape remains in dashboard contracts.
close_reason: Validated the single-source run-state implementation with npm test, npm run check, npm run dashboard:build, targeted lifecycle/query tests after final type fixes, and br dep cycles --json showing zero cycles.

title: Centralize completed workflow writes with markRunCompleted
description:
Implement the central write primitive for completion.

Scope:
- Add markRunCompleted(runState) that directly and unconditionally sets the durable workflow status to completed, updates completion timestamps, and saves the durable run state.
- Update all call sites currently relying on clearTrackedRunState(..., { completed: true }) to call markRunCompleted before cleanup. Confirm the known call sites in local-executor, github-executor, and main.js.
- Change clearTrackedRunState so it only removes live tracking/cancel handles and no longer owns durable completion writes.

Acceptance:
- Completed workflow writes happen through markRunCompleted only.
- All existing success paths still persist completed status.
- Unit/integration coverage catches regression where cleanup runs but durable status remains running.
- No endpoint or executor retains bespoke completion writes outside the helper unless explicitly justified in comments/tests.
close_reason: Added markRunCompleted, moved all completion success paths to it, made clearTrackedRunState cleanup-only, and verified graceful run-state tests.

title: Add bounded remote refresh for stale or contradictory run state
description:
Implement local-first, bounded remote refresh before projection when durable data is stale or contradictory.

Scope:
- Add refreshRunStateIfNeeded(runState, context) or equivalent shared helper.
- The helper should normally return durable data without network calls.
- It may call the remote agent API only when projection detects contradictions, durable status is stale while non-terminal, or a detail view needs a fresher answer.
- Bound calls with TTL/cooldown, per-request caps, and clear context flags so dashboard clicking cannot produce a storm of remote API requests.
- Reuse or consolidate existing syncDurableFollowups behavior so detail paths do not have a separate refresh model.

Acceptance:
- Reads remain local-first in the common path.
- Contradictory nested remote run statuses can trigger one bounded refresh and persist updated durable state before projection.
- List views do not perform unbounded per-row remote calls.
- Tests cover no-refresh happy path, contradiction-triggered refresh, cooldown, failed remote refresh fallback, and persisted refreshed result.
close_reason: Added refreshRunStateIfNeeded with projection diagnostics, follow-up candidate gating, cooldown, and detail/graph integration; storage tests cover refresh and disk-only listing.

title: Collapse /api/runs to projected runs plus pagination
description:
Collapse /api/runs to the clean single-source response model.

Scope:
- Change /api/runs to return only { runs, pagination } using projected durable snapshots.
- Remove active/durable response fields instead of keeping deprecated compatibility fields.
- Update src/contracts/dashboard.ts RunsResponse and all client/server consumers.
- Overlay live-only booting entries only on the cursorless first page or offset === 0.
- Before labeling a live entry booting, do an actual runStore.getRun(runId) lookup so off-page durable records are not duplicated or downgraded to booting.
- Dedupe by durable run id after overlay.

Acceptance:
- Booting live-only rows appear on the first page while no durable record exists.
- Existing durable records never reappear as live-only rows on later pages.
- Pagination does not repeat booting rows.
- Contract tests cover the new shape and old active/durable fields are gone.
- Dashboard build passes after client updates that consume this response.
close_reason: Collapsed runs list responses to { runs, pagination }, updated contracts/client/tests, and added first-page live-only overlay with durable lookup and dedupe.

title: Route detail and graph endpoints through durable projected snapshots
description:
Make run detail, graph, details, and event metadata endpoints durable-authoritative through the same helpers.

Scope:
- Remove the stale guard where durable state is only fetched when durableRunId !== runId.
- Add or reuse a single resolveRunForDashboard(runId, context) helper that finds the durable record, optionally refreshes it, and projects it.
- Apply this path to /api/runs/:id, graph/details endpoints, event metadata, and any detail-adjacent code that currently prefers active live state.
- Live state may supply stream/cancel metadata, but not override projected durable status.

Acceptance:
- Clicking into a run whose live registry id has been rebound to the durable id returns the projected durable status, not stale active status.
- Detail, graph, and event metadata agree on workflow status for the same run.
- Tests cover active.runId === runId with stale active status and a completed durable record.
close_reason: Made run detail/graph/details durable-first for requested and live-bound ids, removed the active.runId !== runId special case, and added active-id-equals-durable regression tests.

title: Align dashboard client cache and event handling with projected durable state
description:
Update dashboard client cache behavior to match the single-source model.

Scope:
- Update React/dashboard clients for the new /api/runs { runs, pagination } contract.
- Remove active-first merging and any client-local durable/active reconciliation that recreates the old model.
- Update query-event-bridge and liveRunReducer behavior so terminal events invalidate/refetch run list/detail state rather than directly patching persistent run status as authority.
- Keep events available for live animation, streaming panes, and optimistic immediate UX where it is clearly non-authoritative.

Acceptance:
- The run list and run detail screens eventually show status from projected durable refetches after terminal events.
- Client-side dedupe handles any transient duplicate safely.
- Tests cover terminal event invalidation/refetch and removal of active/durable merge assumptions.
- npm run dashboard:build passes.
close_reason: Updated client cache for the new runs response and changed runner event bridge to invalidate/refetch durable views instead of patching cached run status.

title: Use projected state for live registry finalization and reconciliation
description:
Rework live registry finalization and reconciliation around projected durable state.

Scope:
- Prefer event-driven terminal completion/failure/cancellation when executor FD3 or equivalent structured events are available.
- Keep a reconcile tick as a fallback for missed events or process exits, but make it use projectRunSnapshot rather than raw durable.status.
- Ensure live registry cleanup does not mutate durable workflow status directly; it should call markRunCompleted or other explicit write helpers as appropriate.
- Remove stale live entries once projected durable state is terminal and no stream/cancel handle is needed.

Acceptance:
- Reconcile no longer treats raw durable.status as authority when projection would differ.
- Missed terminal events are repaired by the fallback tick.
- Live registry cleanup is separated from durable status writes.
- Tests cover terminal FD3 event, missed event fallback, and projected terminal status from steps when top-level status is stale.
close_reason: Live registry now finalizes from terminal structured events and step-status reconcile events use projected run snapshots.

title: Centralize workflow active checks across live and projected durable state
description:
Centralize duplicate-run guarding so workflow start decisions use the same projected state model.

Scope:
- Add isWorkflowActive(flowId) or equivalent central guard.
- Guard should combine live registry entries with projected durable state for the same workflow.
- If live says active but projected durable is terminal, clear stale live tracking before allowing a new run.
- If durable projected state is non-terminal, block duplicate starts even if the live registry entry is missing.

Acceptance:
- Duplicate-run checks do not rely on live registry alone.
- Stale live entries are self-healed by the guard instead of blocking forever.
- Tests cover live-only booting active, durable running with no live entry, durable completed with stale live entry, and concurrent start race expectations.
close_reason: Added projectedWorkflowActivity to combine live and projected durable state, wired start/resume duplicate checks through it, and covered stale-live plus durable-active cases.

title: Clean executor process lifecycle and event-stream finalization
description:
Fix process lifecycle hygiene that can keep dashboard run state stuck.

Scope:
- Audit local and GitHub executor process spawning, cancellation, and FD inheritance around run finalization.
- Ensure child process groups are cleaned up on cancellation/failure where supported.
- Ensure structured event streams close reliably so terminal events and cleanup are not hidden by inherited file descriptors.
- Integrate with markRunCompleted and live registry cleanup semantics from the single-source model.

Acceptance:
- Cancelling or finishing a run does not leave orphaned child processes holding event pipes open.
- Terminal status and live cleanup happen predictably after process exit.
- Manual verification steps are documented in the bead or code comments/tests where automation is impractical.
close_reason: Dashboard local workflow children now run in a cancellable process group on POSIX, cancellation signals the group with SIGTERM/SIGKILL fallback, event fd streams are closed and awaited by the workflow event context, and regression tests cover process-group signalling plus fd close semantics.

title: Major CLI reset: simplify nax command surface
description:
WHY
The CLI simplification spec in docs/cli-simplification-plan.md defines a major-version reset of the public nax command contract. The current root help exposes too many unrelated paths and lets execution happen from the root command via nax <workflow> and root --agent/--prompt flags. The new contract deliberately removes legacy shortcuts and concentrates all execution under nax run, while keeping only daily commands visible: run, dashboard, handoff, list, init.

GOAL
Implement the major CLI reset exactly as specified: bare nax prints compact help and exits 0; nax [workflow] and root --agent/--prompt are removed; nax run owns workflows, single-agent runs, and retries; dashboard, handoff, list, and init remain public; admin is hidden for sync/clean/skills; nax ci remains top-level but hidden; removed commands are not preserved as aliases.

NON-GOALS
Do not redesign workflow execution internals, dashboard behavior, transport internals, artifact storage, or README migration docs. This is a major release boundary, not a backwards-compatibility layer.

SOURCE SPEC
docs/cli-simplification-plan.md is the authoritative plan. Each child bead must be self-contained, but implementation should check the spec for exact command examples and acceptance language.

GLOBAL ACCEPTANCE
- Bare nax and nax --help print compact root help and exit 0.
- nax review and nax --agent codex --prompt ... fail because root execution is removed.
- nax run handles workflow runs, nax run agent handles single-agent runs, and nax run --retry handles retry.
- nax dashboard, nax handoff, nax list, and nax init remain visible.
- nax admin works but is hidden from compact root help.
- nax ci works but is hidden from compact root help.
- nax recent, nax retry, nax sync, nax clean, and nax skills are removed as top-level commands.
- Tests cover compact/advanced help, parser behavior, removed-command failures, run agent, retry, handoff, admin, init skills, and hidden ci.
close_reason: Implemented all child beads and verified the major CLI simplification reset.

title: CLI parser foundation: root help, no root execution, compact help model
description:
SCOPE
Rebuild the commander registration foundation for the major CLI reset. Root nax is no longer an execution command. It has no [workflow] positional argument and no root run flags. Bare nax prints compact root help and exits 0. nax --help prints the same compact help.

BACKGROUND
Today src/cli/commands/nax.js registers program.name('nax').argument('[workflow]') and a root action that delegates to handlers.run. This must be removed. Root help should show only public daily commands: run, dashboard, handoff, list, init. Hidden commands/admin must not appear.

REQUIRED BEHAVIOR
- Remove root [workflow] positional argument.
- Remove root run/action handler that calls handlers.run.
- Remove root operational flags such as --repo, --branch, --agent, --prompt, --site-id, --filter, --transport, --archive, --dry, --force, notify, flows-dir, and output-budget from root.
- Bare nax prints compact root help and exits 0.
- nax --help prints compact root help and exits 0.
- Root compact help includes short examples: nax run; nax run review --branch '#123' --transport netlify-api --force; nax run agent codex "Check this branch"; nax dashboard; nax handoff -c.
- Do not add public open or continue commands.
- Do not rely on commander .alias() when an old command must stay hidden, because aliases leak in help (current list|ls demonstrates this).

IMPLEMENTATION NOTES
Likely touch src/cli/commands/nax.js and the help smoke tests. If custom help is needed for examples or compact/advanced rendering, keep it localized to command registration helpers with precise JSDoc types.

ACCEPTANCE
- node src/cli/nax.js exits 0 and prints root help.
- node src/cli/nax.js --help exits 0 and prints the same compact help.
- Root help lists only run, dashboard, handoff, list, init.
- Root help does not contain admin, ci, recent, retry, sync, clean, skills, issue, comment, preview-boxes, preview-spinner, --agent, --prompt, or [workflow].
- Existing JS syntax/type checks remain green.
close_reason: Implemented and verified as part of the major CLI simplification reset.

title: Remove old public commands and root invocations
description:
SCOPE
Hard-remove old invocations from the command registry for the major release. Do not preserve them as hidden aliases and do not add custom migration docs.

REMOVE
- nax <workflow>
- nax --agent <name> --prompt <text>
- nax recent
- nax retry
- nax sync
- nax clean
- nax skills

CANONICAL REPLACEMENTS IN PRODUCT SHAPE
- nax run <workflow>
- nax run agent <name> <prompt>
- nax handoff
- nax run --retry <run-id>
- nax admin sync
- nax admin clean
- nax admin skills

IMPLEMENTATION NOTES
Because this is a major release, old commands should be unknown-command/parser failures. Do not add one-line migration shims. It is acceptable for tests to assert nonzero status and a generic unknown-command message.

ACCEPTANCE
Each removed command/invocation fails in parser-level tests. No removed command appears in root compact help, advanced help, or command list snapshots, except in tests/spec docs that intentionally mention expected failures.
close_reason: Implemented and verified as part of the major CLI simplification reset.

title: Parser and help regression tests for CLI reset
description:
SCOPE
Update and expand parser/help tests to pin the major CLI reset contract.

TEST TARGETS
- tests/unit/cli-help-smoke.test.js
- scripts/check-cli-help.js
- tests/unit/command-options.test.js where alias/normalization behavior changes

MUST COVER
- nax exits 0 and prints compact root help.
- nax --help matches compact root help.
- Compact root help lists run, dashboard, handoff, list, init only.
- Compact root help omits admin, ci, recent, retry, sync, clean, skills, issue, comment, preview commands, root [workflow], --agent, --prompt.
- nax help, nax help run, nax help run --all, nax help dashboard --all, nax help handoff --all, nax help admin --all.
- nax run --help compact vs nax help run --all advanced.
- Removed commands fail: nax review, nax --agent codex --prompt Review, nax recent, nax retry run-123, nax sync last, nax clean blobs, nax skills check.

QUALITY BAR
Tests should assert meaningful substrings and absence of forbidden surfaces without becoming brittle on wrapping. Real spawned help checks should remain in scripts/check-cli-help.js so package entrypoint behavior is covered, not only buildProgram().helpInformation().

ACCEPTANCE
All help/parser tests fail before implementation and pass after command registration changes. Failure output should make it obvious which command surface drifted.
close_reason: Implemented and verified as part of the major CLI simplification reset.

title: Behavior tests for run agent, retry, handoff, admin, init skills, and ci
description:
SCOPE
Add focused behavior tests beyond help snapshots so the new command routes actually invoke the right existing handlers and preserve safety behavior.

RUN AGENT TESTS
- nax run agent codex "Review this branch" routes to the single-agent handler with agent=codex and prompt text.
- nax run agent codex --prompt "Review this branch" also routes correctly.
- nax run agent codex in TTY invokes multiline prompt utility.
- nax run agent codex in non-TTY fails with clear missing prompt error.
- unknown agent fails and names valid agents.

RETRY TESTS
- nax run --retry run-123 calls retry behavior.
- nax run review --retry run-123 validates/filters against workflow without replacing saved state.
- no retryable failed agents produces the handoff hint.
- top-level nax retry fails.

HANDOFF TESTS
- nax handoff -c copies summary content.
- nax handoff --path prints path to stdout only.
- nax handoff --open calls opener with selected summary path.
- nax handoff --workflow/--runner/--session selectors still work.
- non-TTY no-action handoff prints source kind + summary path and exits 0.

ADMIN / INIT / CI TESTS
- nax admin clean blobs is dry-run by default; --force deletes eligible refs through existing handler.
- nax admin sync last routes to sync behavior.
- nax admin skills check/install/update route to skills behavior.
- nax init TTY can select or skip skill install; non-TTY does not hang.
- hidden nax ci forwards command payload with nested flags and preserves exit status.

ACCEPTANCE
Tests use injected seams where available and real temp directories/artifact fixtures where behavior depends on filesystem state. No network calls, no real gh/netlify calls.
close_reason: Implemented and verified as part of the major CLI simplification reset.

title: Documentation refresh after CLI shape lands
description:
SCOPE
Update user-facing docs only after implementation establishes the final CLI shape. Do not add a migration guide; this is a major release reset.

FILES / AREAS
- README quickstart and examples: root nax now prints help; use nax run for execution.
- README command list: public commands are run, dashboard, handoff, list, init; admin is hidden/advanced; ci is hidden for agents.
- README single-agent examples: nax run agent codex "..." and nax run agent codex --prompt "...".
- README handoff section: handoff remains public and owns artifact browsing/action behavior; recent is gone.
- README run flags table: compact public flags vs advanced flags as implemented.
- README dashboard section only if command text/help changed.
- src/templates/skills/nax-workflows/SKILL.md: replace removed top-level commands with new run/admin/handoff forms.
- Workflow docs and troubleshooting rows that reference nax <workflow>, nax retry, nax recent, nax sync, nax clean, or nax skills.

CONSTRAINTS
No migration section/table for old commands. The major version boundary is the migration story.

ACCEPTANCE
- rg finds no stale public examples for nax review, nax --agent, nax recent, nax retry, nax sync, nax clean, or nax skills except where intentionally documenting expected failures in tests/specs.
- README quickstart is coherent for a new user: install, init, run, dashboard, handoff.
- Agent skill template teaches agents the new command shapes.
close_reason: Implemented and verified as part of the major CLI simplification reset.

title: Major release preparation and final verification
description:
SCOPE
Prepare the package/release boundary for the breaking CLI reset and run final verification.

REQUIRED RELEASE DECISION
This is a major release. Update package metadata/versioning according to the repo's release process once implementation and docs land.

FINAL VERIFICATION COMMANDS
- npm run check
- npm run check:cli-help
- npm test
- Any focused CLI parser/help tests added by this epic
- npm run dashboard:build only if any dashboard UI files changed; otherwise note not required because this is CLI/docs work.

FINAL MANUAL SMOKE CASES
- node src/cli/nax.js
- node src/cli/nax.js --help
- node src/cli/nax.js run --help
- node src/cli/nax.js help run --all
- node src/cli/nax.js run agent codex --prompt "Smoke test" --dry if dry path is supported for agent runs; otherwise use injected tests only.
- node src/cli/nax.js dashboard --help
- node src/cli/nax.js handoff --help
- node src/cli/nax.js help admin --all

ACCEPTANCE
- All required tests/checks pass or documented external prerequisites explain skipped real-service smoke checks.
- Version/release metadata reflects a major breaking change.
- Removed commands are absent from shipped help and fail when invoked.
- Docs match shipped CLI.
close_reason: Implemented and verified as part of the major CLI simplification reset.

title: Help command and advanced help tier
description:
SCOPE
Implement the explicit compact-vs-advanced help model from the spec.

WHY
