The simplified CLI only works if ordinary help is short and advanced/admin surfaces remain discoverable. The spec prefers explicit help commands rather than cluttering normal --help output.

REQUIRED BEHAVIOR
- nax help prints the same compact root help as bare nax.
- nax help run prints compact run help.
- nax help run --all prints advanced run help including all currently-supported advanced run flags.
- nax help dashboard --all prints advanced dashboard help.
- nax help handoff --all prints advanced handoff help.
- nax help admin --all prints hidden admin help.
- Normal command --help stays compact: nax run --help, nax dashboard --help, nax handoff --help, nax list --help, nax init --help.
- Compact help must not imply compatibility aliases for removed commands.

ADVANCED RUN FLAGS TO KEEP AVAILABLE BUT HIDE FROM COMPACT HELP
--repo, --site-id, --filter, --sha, --pr-limit, --label, --runner, --issue, --from-issues, --timeout-minutes, --notify, --notify-url, --notify-events, --flows-dir, --output-budget, --output-budget-bytes, --archive, --project-root, --step-models, --date, --no-auto-context, --no-fetch-results.

IMPLEMENTATION NOTES
Commander has no native tier concept. A custom help command, cloned command metadata, or helpInformation override is acceptable. Keep the implementation deterministic and testable. Avoid exposing old command aliases via commander alias rendering.

ACCEPTANCE
- Compact help is short and stable.
- Advanced help reveals hidden advanced flags without registering removed top-level commands.
- scripts/check-cli-help.js covers compact and advanced help entrypoints.
- tests/unit/cli-help-smoke.test.js asserts both absence of hidden/removed commands in compact help and presence in explicit advanced/admin help where appropriate.
close_reason: Implemented and verified as part of the major CLI simplification reset.

title: nax run workflow mode: canonical workflow execution command
description:
SCOPE
Make nax run [workflow] the canonical workflow execution path and move all public workflow run flags under it. Preserve existing workflow engine behavior behind the command; this bead is command-surface work, not an execution rewrite.

REQUIRED BEHAVIOR
- nax run with no workflow opens the existing interactive picker with the current shape: single-agent run plus available workflows in one flow.
- nax run <workflow> runs that workflow.
- Public compact run flags: --branch, --context, --context-file, --models, --step, --from-step, --transport, --dry, --force, --retry.
- Advanced flags remain parseable through run but are hidden from compact help and shown through advanced help.
- Root nax <workflow> no longer works. nax review must fail as an unknown command or equivalent parser error.
- No behavior changes to transport resolution, flow loading, dry-run, force confirmation, context assembly, output-budget internals, or workflow artifacts beyond moving the public surface under run.

IMPLEMENTATION NOTES
The existing run command already has most flags. This bead should delete root duplication and make run help the clean public contract. Keep JSDoc precise; no any types.

ACCEPTANCE
- nax run review --dry --force behaves like the old workflow execution path.
- nax run with no args still reaches the current picker in a TTY.
- nax review fails.
- Root help no longer advertises workflow argument or run flags.
- Run compact help advertises only the common flags.
close_reason: Implemented and verified as part of the major CLI simplification reset.

title: nax run agent submode for single-agent runs
description:
SCOPE
Move single-agent runs from root flags into the explicit submode nax run agent <type> [prompt]. Remove root --agent/--prompt support.

REQUIRED BEHAVIOR
- nax run agent codex "Review this branch" starts a single Netlify agent run.
- nax run agent codex --prompt "Review this branch" also works.
- Prompt text can be positional or supplied via --prompt.
- If prompt is missing in a TTY, reuse the existing multiline prompt utility.
- If prompt is missing in non-TTY mode, fail clearly: nax run agent <type> requires prompt text.
- Supported agents are exactly claude, codex, and gemini.
- Unknown agents fail with a helpful error and a did-you-mean hint when possible.
- agent is a reserved keyword under run; a workflow named agent is not addressable as nax run agent.
- nax --agent codex --prompt "..." is removed and must fail.

IMPLEMENTATION NOTES
The current handleAdHocAgentRun path and interactive picker already know how to launch single-agent runs. Reuse that behavior rather than duplicating transport or artifact logic. The new submode should route to the same internal handler with normalized options.

ACCEPTANCE
- nax run agent claude/codex/gemini works with positional and --prompt text.
- Missing prompt in TTY opens multiline input.
- Missing prompt in non-TTY exits non-zero with a precise error.
- Unknown agent exits non-zero and names valid agents.
- Existing single-agent artifact, transport detection, Netlify project selection, and handoff support still work.
close_reason: Implemented and verified as part of the major CLI simplification reset.

title: nax run --retry: move retry under run
description:
SCOPE
Replace public top-level nax retry with nax run --retry <run-id>, while preserving retry behavior for failed Netlify API agent runners.

REQUIRED BEHAVIOR
- nax run --retry <run-id> retries one failed Netlify API runner and continues workflow execution using saved run state as the source of truth.
- nax run <workflow> --retry <run-id> is allowed. The workflow argument acts as validation/filter for the saved run, not a replacement workflow definition.
- nax run --retry with no run id in a TTY may open a picker over retryable failed runs.
- nax run --retry with no run id in non-TTY mode fails clearly and names the required run id argument/flag.
- If the selected run has no retryable failed agents, fail with: No retryable failed agents found for <run-id>. Use nax handoff <run-id> to work from completed results.
- Top-level nax retry is removed and must not be registered as a hidden alias.

IMPLEMENTATION NOTES
Current handleRetry already contains the core behavior. This bead should adapt command routing and any picker/filter semantics without rewriting retry internals. Watch interactions with flow argument parsing: nax run review --retry run-123 must not accidentally start a normal review run.

ACCEPTANCE
- Retry success path preserves current state mutation, artifact persistence, compact retry prompt, and downstream continuation behavior.
- Retry no-candidate path gives the handoff hint.
- Top-level nax retry run-123 fails because the command is removed.
- Unit/integration coverage covers with-workflow and without-workflow retry invocations.
close_reason: Implemented and verified as part of the major CLI simplification reset.

title: nax handoff as public artifact browser and action command
description:
SCOPE
Keep handoff public and make it absorb the useful artifact browsing/action role formerly split across recent and handoff. Remove public/callable recent.

REQUIRED BEHAVIOR
- nax handoff keeps existing TTY interactive artifact/action picker.
- nax handoff <run-id> selects a workflow run source when applicable.
- -c/--copy copies selected/latest summary content to clipboard.
- --open opens the selected/latest summary file in the editor.
- --path prints the selected/latest summary path to stdout only.
- If clipboard path support is kept, expose it as --copy-path, not --path.
- --flow <id> runs a workflow with selected summary as context.
- --agent <claude|codex|gemini> starts a fresh single-agent run seeded with selected summary.
- --workflow <id>, --runner <id>, and --session <id> remain explicit selectors.
- Remove or hide --source and --source-type unless implementation still needs them internally.
- In non-TTY mode, nax handoff with no action prints selected/latest source kind and summary path and exits 0.
- If selected run has retryable failures, handoff may print a short hint: Retry failed agents with nax run --retry <run-id>. It must not auto-switch into retry behavior.
- Top-level nax recent is removed and must not be registered as a hidden alias.

IMPLEMENTATION NOTES
Most behavior exists in src/cli/main.js handleHandoff and src/cli/commands/handoff.js. This bead should avoid a broad handoff refactor unless required for clean command registration. Keep command-context overload of --runner acceptable: in handoff it means artifact runner id.

ACCEPTANCE
- Handoff interactive behavior remains intact.
- --copy, --open, --path, --flow, --agent, --workflow, --runner, --session are covered.
- Non-TTY behavior is composable and exits 0.
- nax recent fails because the command is removed.
close_reason: Implemented and verified as part of the major CLI simplification reset.

title: Hidden admin command group for sync, clean, and skills
description:
SCOPE
Move maintenance commands under hidden nax admin while removing old top-level sync, clean, and skills invocations.

REQUIRED BEHAVIOR
- nax admin is hidden from compact root help.
- nax admin sync last owns current sync behavior.
- nax admin clean blobs owns current clean behavior.
- nax admin clean blobs remains dry-run by default.
- nax admin clean blobs --force deletes eligible stale/pending refs, preserving today's safety model.
- nax admin skills install/check/update owns repeat skill maintenance after init.
- Top-level nax sync, nax clean, and nax skills are removed and must not be hidden aliases.
- nax help admin --all exposes admin help.

IMPLEMENTATION NOTES
Existing handlers can be reused. The command tree changes, not the sync/clean/skills internals. Be careful with variadic/positional parsing for clean target and sync target. Skills help currently has a hand-rolled printSkillsHelp; adapt or replace for admin context.

ACCEPTANCE
- nax admin sync last behaves like old nax sync last.
- nax admin clean blobs and --force preserve dry-run/delete behavior.
- nax admin skills check/install/update work.
- nax sync last, nax clean blobs, and nax skills check fail as removed commands.
- Compact root help does not show admin.
close_reason: Implemented and verified as part of the major CLI simplification reset.

title: nax init optional skills installation
description:
SCOPE
Extend nax init so setup can offer bundled skill installation interactively, while repeat skill maintenance lives under hidden admin skills.

REQUIRED BEHAVIOR
During nax init in a TTY, offer an optional prompt:
Install nax workflow skills for local agents?
Choices: Codex, Claude, Cursor, Gemini, Skip.

The init flow should still perform existing repo setup/linking work. Skill installation is additive and should not block core init unless the user selected it and installation fails in a meaningful way.

NON-TTY / SCRIPTING
- Existing nax init --dry, --create, --site-name, --no-github-actions, --skip-secrets behavior must remain stable.
- Non-TTY init should not hang on skill prompts. It should either skip skill install by default or require explicit admin skills invocation.

IMPLEMENTATION NOTES
Reuse current integrations/skills logic. Do not keep top-level nax skills. Ensure provider naming and available harness directories remain precise. Keep JSDoc annotations and no any.

ACCEPTANCE
- TTY init can install selected bundled skills.
- TTY init can skip skills.
- Non-TTY init does not prompt forever.
- admin skills remains the repeat install/check/update path.
- Tests cover skill prompt branching and no-prompt non-TTY behavior.
close_reason: Implemented and verified as part of the major CLI simplification reset.

title: Hidden top-level nax ci remains agent-only utility
description:
SCOPE
Keep nax ci <command...> as a hidden top-level command while simplifying the public command surface.

WHY
nax ci is mainly for prompts and Netlify Agent Runner environments. It should remain callable for agents but should not appear in compact root help or admin help unless explicitly chosen later.

REQUIRED BEHAVIOR
- nax ci <command...> still no-ops outside Netlify Agent Runner environments and executes inside Agent Runner as today.
- nax ci remains top-level and hidden.
- Do not add nax admin ci in this pass.
- Unknown options after the command payload must still be forwarded correctly; e.g. nax ci 'npm test -- --runInBand' and equivalent shell payloads work.

IMPLEMENTATION NOTES
Current command uses .allowUnknownOption(true). Preserve this parser behavior when root command registration changes.

ACCEPTANCE
- Compact root help does not list ci.
- nax ci still works for existing prompt usage.
- Tests cover payload forwarding with nested flags and nonzero status propagation.
close_reason: Implemented and verified as part of the major CLI simplification reset.

title: Deconstruct bin/nax.js into typed src modules
description:
WHY
`bin/nax.js` is still roughly 7.5K lines because it is not just an executable entrypoint. It currently owns command handlers, GitHub issue/comment planning, prompt-budget enforcement, handoff UX, flow selection, resume UX, local prompt delivery, blob cleanup, progress rendering, GitHub polling, local/GitHub workflow execution, retry/resume behavior, and a large `_private` compatibility export used by tests. The previous extraction moved Commander registration into `src/commands/nax.js`, but intentionally left almost all behavior in place.

GOAL
Turn `bin/nax.js` into a thin executable entrypoint while preserving CLI behavior, public command shape, tests, and JSDoc type quality. The final entrypoint should contain the shebang, `buildProgram`, `parseAsync(process.argv)`, and only intentional compatibility exports. All workflow logic, display helpers, command handlers, prompt planning, polling, and execution engines should live under `src`.

SOURCE PLAN
`docs/nax-bin-deconstruction-plan.md`. This bead graph is meant to be self-contained, but that file records the original architectural rationale and phase order.

TARGET ACCEPTANCE
- `bin/nax.js` is under 300 lines.
- No workflow execution, prompt construction, polling, display formatting, or artifact logic remains in `bin/nax.js`.
- No tests import `require('../../bin/nax')._private`.
- `src` never imports from `bin/nax.js`; dependency direction is `bin -> src` only.
- All touched JavaScript has precise JSDoc, no `any`, no broad `Object`, and no long one-line typedefs.
- Root and subcommand help output remain stable.
- `npm run check`, focused tests, `npm test`, forbidden JSDoc scans, and `git diff --check` pass after each extraction pass.

STRATEGY
Extract from the outside inward: command glue and small handlers first; pure display/selection helpers next; GitHub planning and handoff modules after that; prompt delivery and progress before executors; local/GitHub executors before top-level run orchestration; `_private` bridge removed last. Do not extract `handleRun` first, because it touches almost everything and would just recreate the monolith in a new file.

NON-GOALS
- No behavior changes without explicit approval.
- No new `src/monolith.js`.
- No permanent `_private` bridge.
- No broad `JsonMap`/`unknown` laundering where a module owns real field shapes.
- No mixing unrelated extraction seams in one implementation pass.
close_reason: Completed child beads nax-h1n5.1 through nax-h1n5.18; bin/nax.js is deconstructed into typed src modules with a thin launcher. Verified with npm test, npm run check, CLI help, import-direction, and git diff --check.

title: Stabilize current nax command-registration baseline
description:
SCOPE
Checkpoint the current state before broad deconstruction. The repository currently has an in-progress command-registration extraction: `src/commands/nax.js` exists, `bin/nax.js` builds a handler map, `.gitignore` ignores `refactor/`, and `src/workflow-runner.js` has prior simplification changes. This bead decides whether that baseline is kept as-is, adjusted, or committed before further extraction.

WHY
Future extraction passes need a stable baseline. Without a checkpoint, regressions from command registration, workflow-runner simplification, and later module moves are hard to separate.

TASKS
- Review the current `src/commands/nax.js` extraction and confirm it is the desired home for Commander registration.
- Confirm `bin/nax.js` still exposes `buildProgram` and existing compatibility exports needed by tests.
- Run root help and subcommand help smoke checks for `nax --help`, `nax run --help`, and hidden legacy commands like `nax issue --help`.
- Run `npm run check`, `npm test`, `git diff --check`, and a forbidden-JSDoc scan on touched files.
- Either commit the current baseline or record an explicitly accepted dirty baseline before starting deeper moves.

ACCEPTANCE
- There is a known-good starting point for all deconstruction beads.
- Command help shape is unchanged from before deeper extraction.
- Tests and typecheck pass.
- Any remaining dirty files are intentional and documented in the bead comments or commit message.
close_reason: Baseline established before deeper deconstruction. Existing command-registration extraction is kept: src/commands/nax.js owns Commander setup, bin/nax.js still exposes buildProgram and the _private compatibility bridge used by tests. Verified node bin/nax.js --help, run --help, issue --help, comment --help, preview-boxes --help, preview-spinner --help, npm run check, npm test, and git diff --check. Known dirty baseline remains intentional: .gitignore refactor ignore, bin/nax.js command-registration changes, src/workflow-runner.js simplification, docs plan, src/commands/. Forbidden JSDoc scan still reports legacy any/Object annotations in bin/nax.js and a few tests; that is accepted as pre-existing debt to remove during the extraction beads.

title: Extract GitHub issue/comment planning core and CLI handlers
description:
SCOPE
Split GitHub issue/comment planning into a pure-ish planning module and a CLI interaction module.

MOVE TO `src/github/issue-plan.js`
- `ROUND_LABEL_BY_PROMPT`
- `parseCsv` if no better shared location exists
- manual/auto context readers and `joinContext`
- round-result option fetching
- `createIssue`, `createComment`, `createPullRequestComment`, `createDiscussionComment`
- issue/PR metadata loaders
- model inference, PR URL parsing, linked PR extraction, comment target resolution
- `printPlan`, `printCommentPlan`
- `buildPlan`, `buildCommentPlan`
- GitHub source-run conversion and blob offload helpers
- `buildAndMaybeFallbackPlan`

MOVE TO `src/cli/issue.js`
- `pickPromptInteractively`
- `selectIssueGroup`
- `chooseInteractively`
- `chooseCommentInteractively`
- `handleIssue`
- `handleComment`

WHY
Issue/comment planning is a major chunk of `bin/nax.js` and sits upstream of GitHub execution. Keeping interaction separate from plan building makes tests and later command-handler wiring cleaner.

TESTS
Move tests for `buildPlan`, `buildCommentPlan`, comment-target resolution, linked PR extraction, prompt fallback/offload, and interactive choice-free paths to direct module imports. Add focused handler smoke tests with injected dependencies where possible.

ACCEPTANCE
- `bin/nax.js` no longer contains issue/comment plan construction.
- Public exports for `buildPlan`, `buildCommentPlan`, and comment helpers remain compatible.
- No GitHub/network calls are introduced into pure planning tests.
close_reason: Extracted GitHub issue/comment planning into src/github/issue-plan.js and CLI issue/comment handlers into src/cli/issue.js. Tests now import plan/comment-target helpers directly; focused tests, CLI help guardrail, import-direction guardrail, JSDoc scan, syntax checks, and npm run typecheck pass.

description:

Handoff helper tests now import the module directly; syntax, JSDoc scan, focused flow tests, CLI help guardrail, import-direction guardrail, and npm run typecheck pass. handleHandoff remains in bin as orchestration until command-map/top-level extraction.

close_reason: Extracted prompt delivery/blob cleanup helpers to src/workflow/prompt-delivery.js, restored bin helpers damaged by earlier failed extraction, added typed JSDoc/no-any coverage, and validated focused flow execution plus CLI/import/JSDoc guards. Full project typecheck remains blocked by unrelated dashboard worktree files.

title: Extract local Netlify API workflow executor

SCOPE

title: Extract top-level run orchestration and retry handlers
description:
SCOPE
Move the remaining top-level run orchestration after the helper and executor modules exist. This is intentionally late in the graph.

close_reason: Moved run/retry orchestration out of bin into src/cli/legacy-main.js with typed src/cli run/retry facades; bin now delegates to src modules. Verified with npm test, npm run check, CLI help, import-direction, and git diff --check.

title: Create command handler map and final thin program module
description:
SCOPE
After command handlers live in `src/cli/*`, move handler-map construction out of `bin/nax.js` so the executable is just an entrypoint.

TARGET
- Create `src/commands/handlers.js` exporting the handler map consumed by `src/commands/nax.js`.
- Optionally create `src/commands/program.js` as the owner of `buildProgram`, composing `buildNaxProgram`, command options, defaults, and handlers.
- Reduce `bin/nax.js` to shebang, `require(../src/commands/program)`, parseAsync error handling, and minimal exports.

WHY
The current entrypoint still manually wires every handler into `buildNaxProgram`. Once handlers are in `src`, that wiring belongs in `src` too.

TESTS
Command help smoke tests are the main safety net. Also keep package-bin execution tests for `node bin/nax.js run ... --dry` and config-format integration tests.

ACCEPTANCE
- `bin/nax.js` is below 300 lines before `_private` removal, or has a clear line-count delta showing only compatibility exports keep it above that.
- Root and subcommand help output unchanged.
- No `src` module imports `bin/nax.js`.
close_reason: Added src/commands/program.js and src/commands/handlers.js command entry modules; reduced bin/nax.js to a thin launcher that imports src/commands/program. Verified with npm test, npm run check, CLI help, import-direction, and git diff --check.

title: Migrate tests off bin/nax.js _private bridge and remove compatibility exports
description:
SCOPE
Retire `bin/nax.js` as a library module after all behavior has moved to `src`.

TASKS
- Search all tests for `require('../../bin/nax')._private` and replace each import with the real `src` module that owns the function.
- Keep intentional public exports only, such as `buildProgram` and any documented library API the package truly supports.
- Remove compatibility re-exports from `bin/nax.js` once no tests or package consumers use them.
- Add or keep an import-direction test/scan proving no `src` module imports from `bin/nax.js`.
- Run a final forbidden JSDoc scan across `bin/nax.js` and all touched `src` modules.

WHY
As long as tests rely on `_private`, `bin/nax.js` remains a god-object. Removing the bridge is the final proof that module boundaries are real.

ACCEPTANCE
- `rg "_private|../../bin/nax" tests` shows no private-test dependency, except any intentionally documented `buildProgram` import.
- `bin/nax.js` is under 300 lines.
- `npm run check`, `npm test`, command help smoke, import-direction scan, forbidden JSDoc scan, and `git diff --check` all pass.
- The epic acceptance criteria are satisfied.
close_reason: Removed bin/nax.js _private bridge and moved tests to real src module imports where applicable; bin now exports only the program facade. Verified with npm test, npm run check, CLI help, import-direction, and git diff --check.

title: Add deconstruction guardrails and command-help smoke tests
description:
SCOPE
Create the repeatable verification guardrails that every later extraction bead must use. This is not a behavior refactor; it is the safety net for moving thousands of lines out of `bin/nax.js`.

TASKS
- Add focused tests or test helpers that assert root/subcommand command registration still loads after modules move. At minimum cover `nax --help`, `nax run --help`, and hidden compatibility commands such as `issue`, `comment`, `preview-boxes`, and `preview-spinner`.
- Add or document a small command-help smoke command that can be run in every extraction pass without network access.
- Add a forbidden JSDoc scan command for touched files: no `any`, no `Record<string, any>`, no broad `Object`, no `@ts-ignore`/`@ts-expect-error`, no long one-line typedefs.
- Record the import-direction invariant: `bin/nax.js` may import `src`, but `src` must never import `bin/nax.js`.
- Add a lightweight dependency-direction check if practical, or document the `rg` command to enforce it.

ACCEPTANCE
- Later beads have a single checklist for syntax, typecheck, tests, help smoke, JSDoc scan, and import-direction scan.
- Guardrails do not require gh/netlify auth and can run in CI.
- No CLI behavior changes.
close_reason: Added local guardrails: tests/unit/cli-help-smoke.test.js covers root, run, issue, comment, preview-boxes, and preview-spinner help through buildProgram; package scripts check:cli-help, check:import-direction, and check:jsdoc-types provide repeatable smoke/import/JSDoc scans. Verified check:cli-help, check:import-direction, node --check for new scripts, and the new unit smoke test pass. check:jsdoc-types intentionally reports existing bin/nax.js legacy any/Object annotations until later extraction beads remove them.

title: Extract Commander option normalization into src/commands/options.js
description:
SCOPE
Move command-framework glue out of `bin/nax.js` and colocate it with `src/commands/nax.js`.

MOVE

- `commandOptions`

- `normalizeOptionAliases`
- `mergeCommandOptions`

TARGET
Create `src/commands/options.js` with precise JSDoc typedefs for the normalized CLI option bag and the Commander subset used by option source tracking. Update `src/commands/nax.js` and `bin/nax.js` to import from it.

WHY
These helpers are Commander integration details, not workflow behavior. Moving them first reduces `bin/nax.js`, removes duplicated option types from the executable, and gives command-handler extraction a stable options API.

TESTS
Add focused unit tests for alias and inheritance behavior:
- `--dry` sets `dryRun`.
- `--site-id` sets `netlifySiteId`.
- `--force` sets `yes`.
- `--where` maps to `transport` when transport is unset or `auto`.
- Parent command options are inherited only when the parent option was explicitly set.

ACCEPTANCE
- `bin/nax.js` loses the command option helper block.
- `src/commands/nax.js` consumes the new module.
- Existing CLI help and command behavior are unchanged.
- Tests import the new options module directly instead of reaching through `_private`.
close_reason: Extracted Commander option normalization to src/commands/options.js with precise JSDoc typedefs. bin/nax.js now imports actionOptions, collectOption, and mergeCommandOptions. Added tests/unit/command-options.test.js for repeatable collectors, dry/site-id/force/where aliases, cost env behavior, and explicit parent option inheritance. Verified focused tests, npm run check, check:cli-help, check:import-direction, and touched-file JSDoc scan.

title: Extract isolated ci, sync, and init command adapters
description:
SCOPE
Move low-coupling command handlers out of `bin/nax.js` before touching workflow execution.

MOVE
- `normalizeCiCommand` and `handleCi` to `src/cli/ci.js`.
- `handleSync` to `src/cli/sync.js`.
- `printInitResult`, `shouldEnableGithubActions`, and `handleInit` to `src/cli/init.js` if the dependency shape stays small; otherwise split `init` into a follow-up bead comment before implementation.

WHY
These adapters already delegate to existing `src` modules (`netlify-runtime`, `github-actions-sync`, `agent-runner-sync`, `init`) and are much less coupled than `handleRun`. They are the right first behavior extractions.

TESTS
- Move `handleCi` tests from `tests/unit/netlify-runtime.test.js` to import `src/cli/ci.js` directly.
- Add sync handler tests for `last`, numeric GitHub Actions run id, GitHub Actions run URL, and unsupported target.
- Add init handler tests only if moving `handleInit`; inject prompts/TTY where needed.

COMPATIBILITY
Keep `bin/nax.js._private.handleCi` and `handleSync` as temporary re-exports until all tests and downstream callers are migrated.

ACCEPTANCE
- `bin/nax.js` no longer implements `ci` and `sync` behavior.
- Handler modules have precise local typedefs and no `any`.
- Command registration still receives the same handler names and return semantics.
close_reason: Extracted low-coupling command adapters: src/cli/ci.js owns normalizeCiCommand/handleCi, src/cli/sync.js owns handleSync, and src/cli/init.js owns printInitResult/shouldEnableGithubActions/handleInit. bin/nax.js now imports the handlers and no longer implements ci/sync/init behavior. Updated handleCi tests to import src/cli/ci.js directly and added tests/unit/cli-sync.test.js for last, numeric GitHub Actions run IDs, GitHub Actions URLs, and unsupported targets. Verified node --check, focused adapter tests, npm run typecheck, check:cli-help, check:import-direction, and touched-file JSDoc scan.

Verified node --check, npm run typecheck, focused flow-execution tests, check:cli-help, check:import-direction, and touched-file JSDoc scan.

description:

Move GitHub issue/action size-budget helpers out of `bin/nax.js` into `src/github/prompt-budget.js`. This is separate from the broader future `src/prompt-budget.js` feature bead; this bead is an isomorphic extraction of current GitHub-specific behavior only.

MOVE

- `utf8ByteLength`

title: Source spring cleaning: co-locate src modules and define runtime boundaries
description:
WHY: `src/` has grown into a flat set of top-level modules where unrelated responsibilities are hard to find and future runtime expansion is risky. This cleanup keeps the existing single npm package and CommonJS/JSDoc runtime, but reorganizes source code by responsibility, deduplicates shared dashboard/server contracts, and makes local filesystem/runtime dependencies explicit.

GOAL: Make the source tree easier to navigate and safer to extend without introducing a pnpm workspace, package split, lockfile migration, or publish-layout change. The CLI remains `nax` from `@davidwells/netlify-agent-executor`; bundled workflows and dashboard build output continue shipping with the CLI.

SCOPE:
- Add shared `src/contracts/` TypeScript/JSDoc-consumable DTO contracts for dashboard API, workflow graph/details, follow-up payloads, and runner events.
- Add boundary checks for portable dashboard API, dashboard web, and `src/core`.
- Extract portable helpers required by `src/dashboard/api/serializers.js`, especially the existing `../../run-state` leak via `isUnfinishedRun`.
- Define storage/runtime interfaces as plain source modules: WorkflowCatalog, RunStore, EventStore, ArtifactStore, BlobStore, AgentRunnerTransport.
- Co-locate CLI command modules, workflow engine modules, `.nax` storage/artifact modules, and GitHub/Netlify integrations under clearer directories.
- Use compatibility wrappers at old paths during migration and delete them only after internal imports/tests move.

NON-GOALS:
- No pnpm workspace.
- No package split.
- No lockfile migration.
- No runtime CommonJS-to-TypeScript conversion.
- No full hosted Netlify Function orchestrator.

SOURCE PLAN: docs/src-spring-cleaning-plan.md

DONE WHEN: The important code paths are grouped by subsystem, duplicated dashboard DTOs are removed, dashboard API portability is enforced by checks, tests/builds pass, and top-level `src/` contains only intentional composition/root compatibility files.
close_reason: Completed all 12 source spring-cleaning beads: imports now target canonical co-located modules, compatibility wrappers removed, boundaries/contracts/interfaces added, and validation passed.

title: Guardrails: boundary checker, source inventory, and contract type spike
description:
WHY: Before moving files, we need guardrails that make the intended source boundaries visible. The plan deliberately avoids workspace/package tooling, so import-direction checks become the enforcement mechanism.

SCOPE:
- Extend `scripts/check-import-direction.js` or add `scripts/check-boundaries.js`.
- Add boundary rules for:
  - `src/dashboard/api/`: portable API only; no `fs`, no `child_process`, no direct local `.nax` modules like `src/run-state.js`, no `src/dashboard/storage`, no `src/dashboard/runtime/local-files.js`.
  - `src/dashboard/web/src/`: browser-safe code only; no Node built-ins, no dashboard API internals, no workflows/integrations imports.
  - `src/core/`: pure/portable helpers only; no `fs`, no `child_process`, no `src/integrations`, no `src/storage/local`, no `src/dashboard`.
- Keep the dashboard API rule report-only until the known `src/dashboard/api/serializers.js` leak is removed by the dashboard boundary bead. Do not add a permanent grandfather exception.
- Add a source inventory check/report for new top-level `src/*.js` files during cleanup.
- Spike one JS JSDoc import from `src/contracts` after the contracts skeleton exists enough to prove typecheck resolution. If a TS-source contract cannot be consumed directly from JS JSDoc, document and implement the minimal `.d.ts`/path approach before bulk type moves.

ACCEPTANCE:
- `npm run check` includes the boundary checker or an explicit script invoked by check.
- A synthetic forbidden import is caught in tests or by a documented fixture command.
- The checker reports the current `src/dashboard/api/serializers.js` leak by name while report-only mode is active.
- No behavior changes to CLI/dashboard runtime.
- `npm run check`, `npm test`, and `npm run dashboard:build` have a captured baseline before relocation work starts.

TESTS:
- Unit-test or script-test the checker against a temp fixture tree or controlled sample files.
- Include assertions for forbidden Node built-ins and forbidden internal paths.
close_reason: Implemented import boundary checker, source inventory gate, fixture tests, and captured green baseline: npm run check, npm test, npm run dashboard:build.

title: Core co-location: move only genuinely portable helpers into src/core
description:
WHY: `src/core/` should be genuinely portable. Moving mixed modules into core and promising to split them later would preserve the current ambiguity under a nicer path. This bead establishes core only for modules that pass the boundary rule at move time.

SCOPE:
- Move remaining clearly portable helpers into `src/core/` after the dashboard serializer prerequisite moves:
  - `src/constants.js` -> `src/core/constants.js`
  - prompt budget helpers -> `src/core/prompts/budget.js`
  - any other pure helpers that do not import `fs`, `child_process`, `src/integrations`, `src/storage/local`, or `src/dashboard`.
- Keep Phase 2 paths canonical for `status`, agent selection, and run resumability if already moved.
- Evaluate these modules individually before moving:
  - `agent-run-results.js`
  - `comment-markers.js`
  - `prompt-offload.js`
- If any candidate has runtime side effects, hidden local assumptions, or external integration coupling, keep it near its owning subsystem instead of forcing it into core.
- Keep wrappers at old paths while internal imports migrate.

ACCEPTANCE:
- Boundary checker proves `src/core` has no local-node, dashboard, integration, or local storage imports.
- No module is placed in core with a TODO to split later.
- Old paths remain compatible through wrappers until removal phase.

TESTS:
- Existing tests for constants/status/agent selection/prompt budget/result/comment marker/prompt offload behavior pass as applicable.
- `npm run check` and `npm test` pass.
close_reason: Moved pure constants and prompt budget helpers into src/core, extracted titleCase into core strings, updated callers to direct core paths, and intentionally kept Agent Runner/comment/blob helpers out of core after dependency review. Validated with npm run check and focused core/workflow tests.

title: Wrapper removal and import migration: finish the new source shape
description:
WHY: Compatibility wrappers let the cleanup proceed safely, but leaving them forever means the old flat structure remains the practical API for internal code. This cleanup is only complete when internal imports point at the new subsystem paths and old wrappers are either intentionally public or removed.

SCOPE:
- Use `rg` to find old path imports in `src/` and `tests/` for each moved module.
- Migrate imports to new paths in focused batches, keeping changes behavior-preserving.
- Update tests to import new paths where the module is internal.
- Delete compatibility wrappers only when no internal consumer remains.
- Keep wrappers only for paths intentionally treated as public/downstream compatibility paths, and document why they remain.
- Ensure the source inventory check prevents new top-level `src/*.js` implementation files from appearing during or after cleanup.

ACCEPTANCE:
- Top-level `src/` contains only intentional composition roots, asset directories, or documented compatibility wrappers.
- `rg` confirms old internal paths are no longer imported by `src/` or tests, except documented public wrappers.
- Boundary checker prevents drift back to the old flat shape.
- No command, dashboard, workflow, or artifact behavior changes.

TESTS:
- Full `npm run check`.
- Full `npm test`.
- `npm run dashboard:build`.
- `npm run dashboard:smoke` before deleting dashboard-adjacent wrappers.
close_reason: Migrated internal source and test imports off compatibility wrapper paths, deleted old top-level/commands/workflow wrapper files, tightened top-level source inventory, and validated with npm run check, npm test, dashboard:build, dashboard:smoke, and import-direction scan.

title: Final validation and docs update for source spring-cleaning
description:
WHY: This plan cuts across CLI, dashboard API/web, workflow execution, local storage, artifacts, GitHub/Netlify integrations, and type contracts. A final validation/documentation bead prevents the cleanup from landing as a pile of file moves without proof that the user-facing product still works.

SCOPE:
- Run and record final validation commands:
  - `npm run check`
  - `npm test`
  - `npm run dashboard:build`
  - `npm run dashboard:smoke`
- Verify important smoke behavior manually or through existing tests:
  - `nax --help` and subcommand help are stable.
  - `nax list` still sees bundled flows.
  - `nax review --dry --force` still produces the expected dry-run flow plan.
  - Dashboard API routes still serve workflows/runs/details with the shared DTO shape.
- Update developer documentation if paths in README, AGENTS, docs, or bundled skill templates mention old implementation locations in a way that would confuse contributors.
- Summarize intentionally retained compatibility wrappers and why they remain.

ACCEPTANCE:
- All final validation commands pass.
- Documentation does not point contributors at removed implementation paths.
- A short completion note exists in the relevant bead or PR summary explaining the final directory structure and remaining wrappers.
- No pnpm/package/lockfile changes were introduced.

TESTS:
- This bead is primarily the final test gate; failures should produce precise follow-up beads rather than being papered over.
close_reason: Final docs updated to reflect implemented source layout and no remaining compatibility wrappers. Validation passed: npm run check, npm test, npm run dashboard:build, npm run dashboard:smoke, node bin/nax.js --help, node bin/nax.js list, node bin/nax.js review --dry --force. Dependency graph has no cycles.

title: Shared contracts: deduplicate dashboard DTO and runner event types
description:
WHY: Dashboard DTOs are duplicated between server/CLI JSDoc (`src/types.js` and serializers) and frontend TypeScript (`src/dashboard/web/src/types.ts`). The API serializers define the actual wire shape, so the frontend and server should share one contract source before larger file moves.

SCOPE:
- Create `src/contracts/` with focused TypeScript contract files, likely:
  - `dashboard.ts`
  - `runner-events.ts`
  - `workflow.ts`
  - `index.ts`
- Move the dashboard API DTO types out of `src/dashboard/web/src/types.ts` into contracts:
  - WorkflowStep, Workflow, WorkflowGraphNodeData, WorkflowGraph
  - WorkflowListResponse, WorkflowGraphResponse
  - DashboardCapabilities, HealthResponse
  - Target, DryRunOptions, DryRunResult, DryRunResponse
  - DashboardRun, StartRunResponse, RunsPagination, RunsResponse, RunsListData, RunGraphResponse
  - RunDetailsSection, RunFollowupTarget, RunFollowupArtifact, RunDetailsWorkflowStep, RunDetails, RunDetailsResponse
  - RunFollowupRequest, RunFollowupSubmission, RunFollowupResponse
  - RunnerEvent
- Convert `src/dashboard/web/src/types.ts` into a compatibility re-export from `src/contracts` using the correct relative path from `src/dashboard/web/src/types.ts` to `src/contracts` (`../../../contracts`) unless a tsconfig path alias is added.
- Update dashboard frontend imports only if needed; prefer preserving existing imports through the re-export first.
- Add JSDoc imports from contracts in `src/dashboard/api/serializers.js` and/or API modules where useful, but do not mix this with runtime relocation.

ACCEPTANCE:
- No duplicated dashboard DTO definitions remain in dashboard web.
- TypeScript dashboard code compiles using the shared contracts.
- JS JSDoc can consume at least one contract type without introducing `any`.
- `npm run typecheck` passes.
- `npm run dashboard:build` passes because this is a dashboard type/UI-adjacent change.

TESTS:
- Existing dashboard TS unit tests continue to compile.
- Add a minimal contract import/typecheck case if needed to lock in JS-JSDoc interop.
close_reason: Moved shared dashboard DTO and runner event types into src/contracts, converted dashboard types.ts to a re-export, proved JS JSDoc contract import resolution, and validated typecheck/dashboard build.

title: Dashboard API boundary: move serializers helpers into portable core
description:
WHY: `src/dashboard/api/app.js` is already designed around injected stores/mutations, but `src/dashboard/api/serializers.js` currently violates the desired portable API boundary by importing top-level runtime/domain modules directly: `../../run-state` for `isUnfinishedRun`, `../../agent-selection` for model normalization, and `../../status` for status helpers. The dashboard API boundary cannot be enforced honestly until these helpers move to portable locations.

SCOPE:
- Extract pure resumability helpers from `src/run-state.js` into `src/core/runs/resumable.js`:
  - `isUnfinishedRun`
  - the helper chain it needs, such as `hasInFlightRuns`, `hasRepairableRuns`, `isCompletedStep`, `hasRemainingInterruptedSteps`, `isNetlifyApiTransport`, and `transportMatches`, if they are pure and do not touch `fs`.
- Keep local file/state listing and writes in `src/run-state.js` for now; update it to import the pure resumability helper from `src/core/runs/resumable.js`.
- Move cancelled/failed/terminal status helpers from `src/status.js` into `src/core/status.js` if they are pure; keep a compatibility wrapper at the old path.
- Move `normalizeAgentList`, `normalizeStepModels`, and related pure model-selection helpers from `src/agent-selection.js` into `src/core/agents/selection.js`; keep old-path wrapper.
- Update `src/dashboard/api/serializers.js` so it imports only portable `src/core/*` helpers, not `src/run-state.js` or other top-level runtime modules.
- Turn the dashboard API boundary rule from report-only to enforced once this leak is gone.

ACCEPTANCE:
- `src/dashboard/api/serializers.js` no longer imports `src/run-state.js`, `src/agent-selection.js`, `src/status.js`, or other top-level runtime modules.
- Boundary checker blocks future local-node imports under `src/dashboard/api`.
- `src/run-state.js` behavior remains unchanged for unfinished-run detection.
- No broad `Object`/`any` JSDoc types are introduced.

TESTS:
- Existing `run-state`, `dashboard-api-primitives`, `dashboard-api-app`, `agent-selection`, and status-related tests pass.
- Add focused tests for `src/core/runs/resumable.js` if existing run-state coverage does not directly exercise all extracted branches.
- Run `npm run check` and `npm test`.
close_reason: Moved status and agent-selection helpers into src/core, extracted run resumability helpers into src/core/runs/resumable, switched dashboard serializers to core imports, and tightened boundary checker to fail old runtime imports.

title: Storage/runtime interfaces: name the local-vs-hosted seams
description:
WHY: Future local-vs-hosted runtime expansion needs explicit interfaces, but it does not require a workspace or package split. The dashboard API already accepts injected stores and mutations; naming those interfaces makes the current local filesystem implementation clear and gives a clean target for future Netlify Blob/API-backed implementations.

SCOPE:
- Create `src/storage/interfaces.js` containing precise JSDoc typedefs for:
  - WorkflowCatalog
  - RunStore
  - EventStore
  - ArtifactStore
  - BlobStore
  - AgentRunnerTransport
  - DashboardMutations and LiveRuns if these fit better here than under `src/dashboard/api`.
- Keep interfaces as plain source typedefs only; do not build a hosted implementation in this bead.
- Update `src/dashboard/api/app.js` JSDoc to use these interface typedefs for its options object instead of anonymous broad shapes.
- Ensure local dashboard stores (`src/dashboard/storage/local-*.js`) clearly satisfy these interfaces.
- Clarify `src/dashboard/shared/run-details.js` as local-only if it still reads directly from `fs`, or plan/move it under a local runtime path later. Do not pretend it is portable while it reads local artifacts.

ACCEPTANCE:
- `src/storage/interfaces.js` exists and documents the exact methods and return shapes required by API/runtime composition.
- `createDashboardApi()` option types refer to named interfaces.
- Local dashboard store modules still pass tests and need no behavior change.
- No hosted behavior is promised or stubbed beyond clearly documented interface shape.

TESTS:
- Existing dashboard API and dashboard local storage tests pass.
- Typecheck confirms JSDoc references resolve.
- `npm run check` and `npm test` pass.
close_reason: Added named storage/runtime seam interfaces under src/storage/interfaces.js and updated createDashboardApi options to reference them; validated API/store tests, JSDoc checks, and typecheck.

title: Dashboard dev API: consolidate Vite API with real serializers/stores
description:
WHY: `src/dashboard/web/vite.config.mjs` currently embeds a separate read-only API and duplicate public serialization logic (`publicFlow`, `publicRunState`). That can drift from the real Hono API and shared serializers, undermining the contract cleanup.

SCOPE:
- Preferred path: add an API-only/local backend mode for `nax dashboard` or document/use the existing `dashboard:dev:real` flow so Vite proxies `/api` to the real dashboard API via `NAX_DASHBOARD_API_URL`.
- Update Vite dev config to proxy API calls to the real backend when configured.
- If a fully proxied dev mode is not feasible in this bead, remove duplicated serializer logic from `vite.config.mjs` by reusing `src/dashboard/api/serializers.js` and local store modules directly.
- Delete local copies of `publicFlow` and `publicRunState` from the Vite config once real serializers/stores are used.
- Preserve the developer workflow for dashboard browsing without starting a full run unless intentionally requested.

ACCEPTANCE:
- Vite dev API and real dashboard API return the same DTO shape for workflows, runs, run graph, and run details.
- Duplicate serializer implementations are removed from `src/dashboard/web/vite.config.mjs`.
- `npm run dashboard:dev:real` remains documented/usable, or a better API-proxy script is added.
- Dashboard UI behavior is unchanged.

TESTS:
- Add or update tests that compare key Vite/read-only response shapes to real serializer output if practical.
- Run `npm run dashboard:build`.
- Run dashboard e2e/smoke coverage if this changes dev server/API behavior.
close_reason: Removed duplicated publicFlow/publicRunState implementations from Vite fallback dev API, reused dashboard/api serializers, added a regression test, and validated dashboard build/import boundaries.

title: CLI co-location: move command registration and handlers under src/cli/commands
description:
WHY: CLI command registration and command handlers are currently split between `src/commands/*` and loose `src/cli/*.js` files. For this cleanup, the mental model should be one CLI subsystem with command registration, option normalization, and command-specific handlers co-located.

SCOPE:
- Move current command registration files:
  - `src/commands/program.js` -> `src/cli/commands/program.js`
  - `src/commands/nax.js` -> `src/cli/commands/nax.js`
  - `src/commands/options.js` -> `src/cli/commands/options.js`
  - `src/commands/handlers.js` -> `src/cli/commands/handlers.js`
- Move existing CLI command modules under `src/cli/commands/`:
  - `ci.js`, `init.js`, `issue.js`, `run.js`, `retry.js`, `sync.js`, `handoff.js`
- Move presentation-only helpers under `src/cli/display/` where useful:
  - `flow-list.js` -> `src/cli/display/flow-list.js`
  - Extract handoff/flow-list display helpers only when it reduces coupling; do not split just to split.
- Update `bin/nax.js` to load the new `src/cli/commands/program.js` target, or keep a temporary wrapper at `src/commands/program.js` until consumers/tests are migrated.
- Preserve command help output exactly unless a difference is intentionally approved.

ACCEPTANCE:
- No loose command-specific files are stranded directly under `src/cli/` except intentional composition roots like `main.js`.
- Old `src/commands/*` paths are wrappers only during migration.
- `nax --help` and subcommand help output remain stable.
- Command behavior is unchanged.

TESTS:
- `tests/unit/cli-help-smoke.test.js` passes.
- Command option tests, init/sync/handoff/issue/run/retry tests pass.
- Run `npm run check` and `npm test`.
close_reason: Moved command implementations under src/cli/commands, moved flow list rendering under src/cli/display, left src/commands as compatibility wrappers, updated internal/test imports, and validated npm run check plus CLI tests/help smoke.

title: Storage/artifact co-location: group .nax state, artifacts, blob refs, and events
description:
WHY: `.nax` persistence, blob refs, event logs, and artifacts are central concepts, but today they are spread across top-level `src/*.js` files. Grouping them makes local storage behavior easier to audit and gives future hosted/blob-backed implementations a clear comparison point.

SCOPE:
- Move local state/cache files under `src/storage/local/`:
  - `src/run-state.js` -> `src/storage/local/run-state.js`
  - `src/blob-ref-registry.js` -> `src/storage/local/blob-ref-registry.js`
  - `src/blob-debug-cache.js` -> `src/storage/local/blob-debug-cache.js`
- Move artifact writers under `src/workflows/artifacts/`:
  - `src/workflow-artifacts.js`
  - `src/agent-runner-artifacts.js`
  - `src/agent-session-artifacts.js`
- Move runner event files under `src/workflows/events/`:
  - `src/workflow-events.js`
  - `src/runner-event-log.js`
  - `src/runner-events.js`
- Keep old top-level paths as CommonJS wrappers until internal imports/tests migrate.
- Update imports in nearby modules opportunistically, but avoid a giant all-at-once rewrite.

ACCEPTANCE:
- `.nax` state paths, artifact layout, latest symlink behavior, blob ref registry behavior, and event JSONL behavior are unchanged.
- Top-level `src/` file count drops materially.
- Old implementation files are wrappers only.

TESTS:
- Run-state tests pass.
- Workflow artifact, agent runner artifact, agent session artifact tests pass.
- Blob ref/debug cache tests pass.
- Runner event/event-log tests pass.
- Dashboard local storage tests pass because dashboard reads these artifacts/state.
close_reason: Moved local run state/blob cache/blob registry under src/storage/local, moved artifact writers under src/workflows/artifacts, moved runner event modules under src/workflows/events, left top-level wrappers, and validated full check plus state/artifact/blob/event/dashboard storage tests.

title: Workflow co-location: group catalog, engine, followups, human review, and round results
description:
WHY: Workflow orchestration is the heart of `nax`, but its files are split between top-level modules, `src/workflow/`, and follow-up modules. A future agent should be able to find flow catalog, prompt loading, execution, follow-up handling, artifacts, and round results under one `src/workflows/` subsystem.

SCOPE:
- Move catalog/prompt modules:
  - `src/flows.js` -> `src/workflows/catalog/flows.js`
  - `src/prompts.js` -> `src/workflows/catalog/prompts.js`
- Move engine modules:
  - `src/workflow-runner.js` -> `src/workflows/engine/runner.js`
  - `src/workflow/*` -> `src/workflows/engine/*`
- Move follow-up modules:
  - `src/followup-context.js` -> `src/workflows/followups/context.js`
  - `src/followup-delivery.js` -> `src/workflows/followups/delivery.js`
  - `src/followup-persistence.js` -> `src/workflows/followups/persistence.js`
  - `src/followup-plan.js` -> `src/workflows/followups/plan.js`
  - `src/handoff-runner.js` -> `src/workflows/followups/runner.js`
- Move `src/human-review.js` and `src/round-results.js` under `src/workflows/`.
- Keep old paths as wrappers and migrate imports gradually.
- Do not change flow semantics, flow search order, prompt loading, prompt interpolation, or workflow state schema in this bead.

ACCEPTANCE:
- `nax list` still shows the same bundled/project workflows.
- `nax review --dry --force` produces equivalent output/plan.
- Flow loading supports the same YAML/JSON/JS/TS/TOML formats through existing configorama behavior.
- Old imports are wrappers only after migration.

TESTS:
- `tests/unit/flows.test.js`, workflow runner tests, flow execution tests, follow-up tests, human review tests, prompt/round result tests pass.
- Run `npm run check` and `npm test`.
close_reason: Moved workflow catalog, prompt loading, engine modules, follow-up modules, human review, and round-results under src/workflows with compatibility wrappers; preserved bundled flow paths and validated full check plus workflow/follow-up test slice.

title: Integration co-location: group GitHub, Netlify, git target, and notification boundaries
description:
WHY: GitHub, Netlify, target/review context, and notifications are integrations rather than core domain code. Co-locating them under `src/integrations/` makes external process/API boundaries easier to find and keeps portable core/dashboard API code honest.

SCOPE:
- Move GitHub integration modules:
  - `src/gh-cli.js` -> `src/integrations/github/gh-cli.js`
  - `src/github-actions-sync.js` -> `src/integrations/github/actions-sync.js`
  - `src/github/issue-plan.js` -> `src/integrations/github/issue-plan.js`
  - `src/github/polling.js` -> `src/integrations/github/polling.js`
- Move Netlify integration modules:
  - `src/local-runner.js` -> `src/integrations/netlify/local-runner.js`
  - `src/netlify/*` -> `src/integrations/netlify/*`
  - `src/init.js` -> `src/integrations/netlify/init.js` because this is runtime/project setup, distinct from `src/cli/commands/init.js` command handling.
  - Keep `src/netlify-runtime.js` and `src/netlify-blobs.js` as compatibility wrappers.
- Move git target/review context modules under `src/integrations/git/`:
  - `src/target.js`
  - `src/review-context.js`
- Move `src/transports.js` and `src/notifications.js` under integrations where dependency review indicates.
- Keep wrappers and migrate imports in focused batches.

ACCEPTANCE:
- Netlify project selection, Netlify API client, blob CLI operations, runtime classification, GitHub issue/polling/action sync, target selection, and review context behavior are unchanged.
- No portable `src/core` or `src/dashboard/api` module imports integrations.
- CLI and workflow code import integrations through the new paths or wrappers during transition.

TESTS:
- Netlify API/client/blob/runtime/local-runner tests pass.
- GitHub action sync, gh-cli, issue-plan/polling, target, review-context, transports, init tests pass.
- CLI command tests pass.
close_reason: Moved integration implementations under src/integrations, removed old integration pass-through wrappers, migrated imports/tests to direct integration paths, fixed Netlify init template path, and validated with npm run check plus focused integration tests.

title: Structured findings contract + actionable handoff targets
description:
WHY: nax already extracts '## 2. Structured Findings' JSON blocks from agent comments (src/round-results.js) but only to feed follow-up prompts. The terminal artifact of a workflow — the ranked consensus — is markdown a human must copy somewhere. The whole point of consensus output is to become WORK: GitHub issues, beads, PR comments. Today that last mile is manual paste.

GOAL: Make findings a first-class machine-readable artifact, then grow nax handoff from 'copy markdown' into 'route results into the tool where work happens'.

DESIGN:
- findings.json per workflow: normalized [{ id, title, severity, agent(s), confidence, files, summary, consensusRank }] built from per-agent structured blocks + the synthesize step output; persisted next to summary.md by workflow-artifacts.js.
- Prompt contract: bundled flow prompts already ask for structured output; tighten the schema in prompts (one canonical JSON shape, documented) so parsing is reliable across claude/gemini/codex.
- nax handoff --to github-issues: create one GH issue per consensus finding (labels: severity, source workflow link, originating agents) with --limit and interactive multi-select; idempotent via marker in issue body (skip already-created).
- nax handoff --to beads: emit br-importable markdown (br create -f format) or directly shell to br when present, so findings drop straight into the local issue graph.

SERVES: 'orchestration is the artifact' philosophy — extends artifact from prose to actionable records. This is the feature that turns nax from a review tool into a pipeline stage.

ACCEPTANCE: after nax review, nax handoff --to github-issues --limit 5 creates 5 labeled issues from top consensus findings; rerunning creates zero duplicates.

title: findings.json: normalized machine-readable findings artifact
description:
WHAT: New src/findings.js. Input: run state + per-step round results (reuse src/round-results.js extraction). Output: .nax/workflows/<id>/artifacts/findings.json.

SCHEMA (document in README + JSDoc typedef):
{ schemaVersion: 1, workflowRunId, flowId, generatedAt,
  findings: [{ id (stable slug from title), title, severity (critical|high|medium|low|info), summary, files: [paths], agents: [who reported], confirmedBy: [agents agreeing in cross-review], consensusRank (from synthesize step order, null if absent), sourceIssues: [urls] }] }

PARSING RULES: tolerate agents deviating from the schema — per-agent block parsed best-effort, failures recorded as { agent, parseError } in a diagnostics array rather than throwing; the consensus step is authoritative for rank. Dedupe across agents by normalized title similarity (conservative: exact slug match only; fuzzy matching is a non-goal v1).

ALSO: tighten the structured-output instructions in bundled flow prompts (src/flows/*/prompts) to one canonical JSON shape with severity enum + files array, so all three agents emit parseable blocks. Keep instructions short; agents drift on long schemas.

ACCEPTANCE: running review on a repo with seeded issues yields findings.json where every consensus item carries rank + source issue URLs; malformed agent block degrades to diagnostics entry, not a crash.

title: nax handoff --to github-issues (consensus -> labeled issues)
description:
WHAT: Extend nax handoff with --to github-issues. Reads findings.json from the selected workflow (default latest). Interactive multi-select of findings in TTY (clack multiselect, preselect consensusRank <= 5); flags for scripted use: --limit N, --severity high, --force.

ISSUE FORMAT: title = finding title; body = summary + files + originating agents + link back to source issues + workflow run id; labels = nax-finding, severity:<level> (create labels if missing, tolerate label-create failure). Embed idempotency marker '<!-- nax-finding:<workflowRunId>:<findingId> -->' (extend src/comment-markers.js); before creating, search existing issues for the marker and skip with a 'already filed as #N' notice.

IMPLEMENTATION: reuse src/gh-cli.js; dry-run support via existing --dry conventions (print plan, create nothing).

ACCEPTANCE: epic acceptance scenario (5 issues, zero dupes on rerun) plus --dry prints the exact plan.

title: nax handoff --to beads (findings -> br import)
description:
WHAT: --to beads target. If br binary on PATH and a .beads workspace exists at repo root: create one bead per selected finding via br create (type bug for severity>=high else task, priority mapped from severity, description = same body as the github-issues target, external-ref = source issue URL). Otherwise: write .nax/workflows/<id>/artifacts/findings-beads.md in br bulk-import format (br create -f) and print the import command.

IDEMPOTENCY: external-ref carries workflowRunId:findingId; before creating, br list --json scan for that ref, skip existing.

WHY: David's whole workflow is beads-driven; review findings landing directly in the dependency graph closes the loop between remote multi-agent review and local agent swarm execution.

ACCEPTANCE: nax handoff --to beads --limit 3 on a repo with beads creates 3 beads with mapped priorities; rerun creates none.

title: Tests: findings parsing, issue creation idempotency, beads export
description:
WHAT: tests/unit/findings.test.js + handoff-targets.test.js.

FINDINGS: feed REAL captured agent comment bodies (collect fixtures from actual past runs in .nax/agent-sessions — real data, not invented mocks) through extraction; assert schema conformance, dedupe-by-slug, parse-error -> diagnostics path, consensus rank assignment, stable finding ids across reruns.

GITHUB TARGET: exec seam on gh-cli (same pattern as existing gh-cli.test.js): assert issue payloads (title/body/labels/marker), idempotency skip when search returns marker hit, --dry creates nothing, --severity filter.

BEADS TARGET: run against a real temp .beads workspace using the actual br binary when available (skip-with-notice if br absent): create, verify via br list --json, rerun -> zero new. Bulk-import file format golden-checked.

LOGGING: print every generated issue body / bead description on failure for direct inspection.

title: Visualizer follow-up composer: run follow-up and fresh seeded agent runners
description:
WHY: The visualizer now has a RunDetailsModal with a Send to next agent action, but the most important action is still disabled: Run a followup. Users need to take workflow/agent results and immediately send them into the next agent action without manually copying paths or markdown. This must support both true follow-up sessions on existing Netlify Agent Runner threads and fresh seeded agent runner threads for new/multiple models.

PRODUCT GOAL: Add one compose surface from RunDetailsModal with two modes: Follow up on existing agent results and Start fresh agent runner. The user must provide new instructions, can choose which result artifacts to include, can select one or more models, and gets clear post-submit links/notifications. Fresh one-off agent runs should be represented as durable one-step pseudo-workflow runs so the existing React Flow and run details surfaces still apply.

KEY DECISIONS: user prompt is required; selected artifacts are context only; default target is the last meaningful result; matching prior model uses a true follow-up session; additional/non-matching models become fresh seeded runners; original workflow target SHA is the default target; large context should use existing blob/offload policy; API returns after remote acceptance and does not wait for terminal completion; remote acceptance plus local persistence failure returns success with warnings.

SOURCE SPEC: docs/ai/plans/nax-run-followup-from-visualizer.md. The beads under this epic should be self-contained enough to implement without reopening the spec.
close_reason: Completed visualizer follow-up composer end to end: contracts, endpoint, context delivery, submission/persistence, UI, notifications, graph/run polish, tests, and docs.

title: Run-details follow-up targets and artifact contracts
description:
WHAT: Extend src/visualize-run-details.js and web/src/types.ts so run details expose explicit follow-up targets and selectable artifacts. The UI must not infer follow-up eligibility from rendered markdown labels or paths. Add details.followupTargets and details.followupArtifacts beside existing summaryMarkdown/finalMarkdown/sections.

FOLLOWUP TARGET SHAPE: include id, kind (workflow-summary, step-summary, agent-result, runner-summary, session-result), label, agent, stepId, stepTitle, runnerId, sessionId, status, path, absolutePath, links, defaultMode (follow-up-thread or fresh-runner), and isDefault.

ARTIFACT SHAPE: include id, kind (workflow-summary, step-summary, agent-result, runner-summary, session-result, metadata-json, usage-json, attempt-markdown, blob-debug), label, path, absolutePath, sizeBytes, defaultSelected, advanced, and source metadata. Human outputs are visible by default; JSON/attempt/blob-debug artifacts are advanced.

DEFAULTING: default target/default selected artifact is the last meaningful result: final step summary/result if present, else workflow summary, else latest completed agent session result. The order must be deterministic and stable across refreshes.

WHY: The composer needs precise choices and defaults because not every workflow has a clean final summary. Explicit contracts prevent fragile markdown parsing and let tests assert behavior directly.

ACCEPTANCE: GET /api/runs/:id/details includes followupTargets and followupArtifacts for fixture workflows; target ordering follows the specified fallback chain; artifact IDs are stable enough for POST validation; existing RunDetailsModal rendering remains unchanged before the composer is wired.
close_reason: Implemented run details follow-up targets/artifacts and frontend types; verified with tests/unit/visualize-server.test.js and npm run typecheck.

title: Add post-submit notifications, refresh, and navigation behavior
description:
WHAT: After POST /api/runs/:id/followups returns 202, close the composer, show a Mantine notification, refresh run details/graph in the background, and navigate/open a new pseudo-workflow run when the response contains one.

NOTIFICATIONS: success title Follow-up submitted. Include action links when possible: Open Netlify run/session, Open local result artifact, Open new run view for fresh pseudo-workflow. Warning title when warnings are present: Follow-up submitted, but local artifact persistence needs attention. Warnings should be visible without burying the remote link.

DEPENDENCY: if Mantine notifications are not installed/configured, add the minimal provider/dependency in the app shell using existing Mantine patterns. Avoid global ad hoc toast utilities.

REFRESH: current RunDetailsModal should refresh its details after submit. Current graph should refresh only real persisted graph state. Do not fake a node just to make the UI feel live.

ACCEPTANCE: successful submit triggers notification and closes modal; warnings are displayed; details refresh is called; response with fresh pseudo-workflow opens/navigates to that run; response without persisted graph state does not add fake nodes.
close_reason: Added Mantine notifications, success/warning actions, composer close-on-submit, detail refresh, run-list refresh, and automatic selection of persisted fresh pseudo-workflows; verified npm run typecheck and npm run visualize:build.

title: Graph and run-list polish for fresh and persisted follow-up submissions
description:
WHAT: Ensure visualizer graph/run-list behavior matches persistence semantics. Fresh one-off agent runs render as durable one-step pseudo-workflow runs. True follow-up graph nodes are appended only when the submission is persisted into the current workflow run state.

FRESH RUN VIEW: listRuns should include the pseudo-workflow. getRunGraph should show a one-node React Flow graph with selected model chips/runs. getRunDetails should show the submitted/follow-up artifact and metadata. The run title should be clear, e.g. Follow-up on <workflow title> / <Agent> or Agent Run with prompt excerpt as subtitle where available.

FOLLOW-UP APPEND: if persistence adds a real follow-up step/node to current workflow run state, flowToGraph should render it naturally. If not persisted, no visual node is added; notification links are the source of truth.

WHY: This avoids a second special-case visualization model for single runs and avoids lying to users with UI-only graph state.

ACCEPTANCE: pseudo-workflow appears in Recent Runs, opens in graph, has details; persisted follow-up nodes render only from state; non-persisted follow-up response does not mutate graph.
close_reason: Fresh follow-ups persist as durable one-step pseudo-workflows with source-aware titles, appear through listRuns/getRunGraph/getRunDetails, and App selects persisted fresh runs without faking graph nodes; verified visualize-server/followup-persistence tests and npm run typecheck.

title: Backend and service tests for visualizer follow-up
description:
WHAT: Add comprehensive node:test coverage for backend/service behavior introduced by this epic. Tests should use real temp directories, actual artifact writers/readers where practical, and injected Netlify CLI seams rather than mocking business logic.

COVERAGE: run-details followupTargets/followupArtifacts, artifact resolver path safety, context package labeled sections, prompt delivery inline/blob/error paths, submission plan matrix, POST /api/runs/:id/followups validation and success, existingRunnerId use for true follow-up, fresh runner submission for additional models, persistence warning behavior after remote acceptance.

FILES LIKELY TO TOUCH: tests/unit/visualize-server.test.js, tests/unit/handoff-sources.test.js, tests/unit/local-runner.test.js, tests/unit/flow-execution.test.js, tests/unit/workflow-artifacts.test.js, plus new focused tests if needed.

LOGGING: failures should print useful context: request body, resolved artifacts, submission plan, response payload, and relevant temp artifact paths.

ACCEPTANCE: unit/server tests can be run with node --import tsx --test for the affected files and fail with actionable diagnostics.
close_reason: Added/verified backend follow-up coverage for run-details contracts, artifact safety, context packaging, delivery inline/blob/error paths, submission planning, endpoint validation/success, true follow-up existingRunnerId, fresh submissions, pseudo-workflow persistence, and persistence warnings after remote acceptance; verified 42 focused backend tests and npm run typecheck.

title: Frontend and interaction regression tests for follow-up composer
description:
WHAT: Add frontend tests for RunDetailsModal/RunFollowupModal behavior and any e2e/spec coverage available in this repo. The tests should verify the actual UX contract, not only snapshots.

COVERAGE: menu item opens composer, composer initializes defaults from details.followupTargets/followupArtifacts, prompt required validation, artifact picker visible/advanced behavior, model chips and mixed submission summary, request body construction, successful submit closes modal and calls onSubmitted, warning response displays warning path, missing follow-up data disables action safely.

E2E/INTEGRATION: If the repo has Playwright visualizer coverage, add a mock API scenario: open run details, click Send to next agent -> Run a followup, select artifacts/models, submit, assert POST body and success notification. Keep tests deterministic and avoid real Netlify calls.

ACCEPTANCE: npm run typecheck passes; relevant frontend tests pass; visualizer build passes; failures print component state/request payload enough to debug quickly.
close_reason: Added run-followup composer unit coverage and Playwright interaction regression for RecentRuns details, composer defaults, mixed model submission, and persisted fresh follow-up display.

title: Document visualizer follow-up behavior and update plan references
description:
WHAT: Update user/developer docs after implementation so the new visualizer follow-up surface is discoverable and accurately scoped.

SCOPE: mention that RunDetailsModal can send results to next agents, explain the two modes (follow up on existing agent results vs start fresh agent runner), note that a prompt is required, describe artifact selection and multiple model behavior, and document that HTTP submission returns after remote acceptance rather than completion. Mention warnings semantics if remote accepted but local persistence failed.

WHERE: README or visualizer docs if present; docs/ai plan/spec can remain as historical context but should not be the only user-facing explanation. If CLI handoff docs are touched, preserve current CLI behavior and clarify relationship to visualizer follow-up.

ACCEPTANCE: docs match implemented labels and API behavior; no stale claim that Run a followup is not implemented; limitations/non-goals are clear for GitHub transport and no-wait behavior.
close_reason: Documented visualizer follow-up behavior in README and marked the implementation plan as shipped with API, UX, persistence, warning, and MVP limitation notes.

title: Server-side artifact resolver and follow-up context package builder
description:
WHAT: Build a server-side resolver for selected follow-up artifact IDs and a context package assembler. The frontend may send artifact IDs and kinds, but must never send trusted markdown contents. Server code resolves IDs against details.followupArtifacts, validates paths, reads files, and builds one labeled context package.

SECURITY: Every artifact absolutePath must realpath under the project .nax directory. Reject unknown IDs, missing files, directories, symlink escapes, and paths outside .nax with concrete 400 errors. Do not leak token values or blob payload snippets in errors.

PACKAGE FORMAT: for each selected artifact, append a labeled markdown section:

## Artifact: <label>

Source: <relative path>

<contents>

Preserve artifact ordering from run details. If no artifacts are selected, context delivery is none, but the user prompt is still required.

WHY: Context selection is a core UX requirement. Building the context server-side gives us a trust boundary and makes API submissions reproducible from local artifacts.

ACCEPTANCE: unit tests cover valid artifact packages, multiple artifacts as labeled sections, unknown artifact ID rejection, missing file rejection, unsafe path rejection, and advanced artifact inclusion. The builder returns both markdown and metadata: artifactCount, source paths, total bytes.
close_reason: Implemented src/followup-context.js with trusted artifact resolution/context packaging and path-safety tests; verified followup-context.test.js and npm run typecheck.

title: Extract reusable non-interactive handoff runner module with DI
description:
WHAT: Extract the reusable handoff/follow-up submission core from bin/nax.js into a source module such as src/handoff-runner.js or src/agent-handoff-run.js. The visualizer server must not import bin/nax.js.

REUSE SURFACE: expose non-interactive operations for buildHandoffPrompt/buildFollowupPrompt, submitFreshAgentRunner, submitFollowupSession, and submitFollowupPlan. True follow-up uses submitLocalAgentRun with run.existingRunnerId. Fresh runner uses the existing local Netlify API submission path and existing link/artifact helpers where possible.

DI REQUIREMENT: Do not rewrite terminal behavior blindly. The current runSingleNetlifyAgent mixes console logging, progress reporters, wait logic, and persistence. Extract behind injected logger/reporter/progress hooks. CLI passes the current terminal logger/reporter; server passes no-op or collector hooks.

CHARACTERIZATION FIRST: Add tests around the extracted seam before moving behavior so CLI handoff behavior remains stable. Existing module-private functions readSelectedHandoffSource, runFreshHandoffAgent, and runSingleNetlifyAgent are not importable today; extraction must account for that.

WHY: Both CLI handoff and visualizer follow-up need the same semantics. A shared source module avoids duplicated remote submission and artifact persistence behavior.

ACCEPTANCE: CLI handoff tests still pass; new tests prove buildHandoffPrompt output is unchanged; server-side code can submit fresh and existing-runner plans without importing bin/nax.js; terminal-only output is injectable and absent in server use.
close_reason: Implemented src/handoff-runner.js with DI submission seam and persistence hooks; verified handoff-runner.test.js and npm run typecheck.

title: Apply prompt delivery/blob policy to follow-up context packages
description:
WHAT: Reuse existing prompt size/offload infrastructure for selected follow-up artifact context. Small context packages can be included inline. Large context packages must use the existing Netlify blob/offload policy or a thin shared wrapper around it. Do not invent a separate large-context mechanism.

PROMPT SEMANTICS: The user prompt remains the primary instruction. Artifact content is context. Follow-up prompts should tell the agent to use existing thread context when available and fetch/read attached prior-results context if details are missing or incomplete.

DELIVERY RESULT: Return context metadata to the API response: artifactCount, delivery (inline, blob, none), and blobRef when blob-backed. If context is too large and safe delivery is unavailable, fail before remote submission with a concrete error explaining missing Netlify site/auth or disabled offload.

RELIABILITY: Never submit a prompt that violates the existing safe prompt budget. Preserve existing blob redaction rules and local debug/registry behavior where applicable.

WHY: Follow-up context can be large, especially when multiple artifacts are selected. The repo already solved the Netlify argv problem; this feature should reuse that path.

ACCEPTANCE: tests cover inline delivery for small context, blob delivery for oversized context, disabled/missing blob context failure before submission, and response metadata. No token or blob payload content appears in logs/errors.
close_reason: Implemented follow-up context delivery helper using prompt-offload blob primitives; verified followup-delivery.test.js and npm run typecheck.

title: Resolve follow-up submission plan across modes and models
description:
WHAT: Implement pure submission planning for requested mode, selected target, selected models, and available prior runners. The planner does not perform network I/O; it outputs concrete submissions that the runner module will execute.

RULES: user prompt is required separately by validation. If selected target has an existing runner and requested model matches the target agent, that model gets mode continue-runner. If requested model does not match the target agent, it becomes fresh-runner. If target has no runner, all models become fresh-runner.
