For multiple models, at most one true follow-up occurs per compatible existing runner/model pair; additional models start fresh seeded runners. Do not submit multiple different models into the same existing runner thread.

DEFAULTS: default model is the target's previous agent when available. Default target is supplied by run-details contracts. Target SHA defaults to the original workflow target.

OUTPUT: each planned submission includes agent, mode, source target, runnerId when continuing, source artifact IDs, target SHA/branch, and a user-facing summary label such as Codex: follow-up session or Claude: fresh runner.

WHY: Multi-model semantics are subtle and must be testable independently from UI and Netlify calls.

ACCEPTANCE: unit tests cover matching single model -> continue-runner, non-matching single model -> fresh-runner, multiple models -> mixed plan, no runner -> all fresh, unsupported model rejection, stable user-facing summary strings.
close_reason: Implemented pure follow-up submission planner with multi-model/fresh-runner semantics; verified followup-plan.test.js and npm run typecheck.

title: Add POST /api/runs/:id/followups visualizer endpoint
description:
WHAT: Add POST /api/runs/:runId/followups to src/visualize-server.js. This mutation validates input, resolves selected artifacts, builds context delivery, creates a submission plan, submits true follow-up/fresh runner requests, and returns 202 after remote acceptance.

AUTH: call assertToken like existing mutation routes. GET run-details remains read-only and does not need token.

REQUEST: mode (follow-up-thread or fresh-runner), prompt, models, source workflow/target/section/runner/session metadata, artifact IDs/kinds, optional target SHA/branch/source. Server defaults target.source to workflow-target and validates everything against local run details.

RESPONSE: followup object with id, status submitted, sourceWorkflowRunId, target, context metadata, submissions array, and warnings. Each submission includes mode, agent, runnerId, sessionId, status submitted, links, artifact paths when available, and per-submission warnings.

ERRORS: reject empty prompt, no models, unknown run, invalid artifact IDs, unsafe paths, unsupported models, missing Netlify site/auth for fresh submissions, incompatible continue-runner requests, and unsafe oversized context before submission.

REMOTE ACCEPTANCE EDGE: if Netlify accepts but local artifact persistence fails, return 202 with warnings and remote links rather than failing the whole request.

ACCEPTANCE: server tests cover token rejection, unknown run, empty prompt, invalid artifact, unsafe path, continue-runner submission using existingRunnerId, fresh submission for additional models, and 202 response shape.
close_reason: Implemented POST /api/runs/:id/followups with auth, artifact/context resolution, prompt delivery, submission planning, accepted-run response, and fresh pseudo-workflow persistence; verified visualize-server.test.js and npm run typecheck.

title: Persist submitted follow-ups and fresh one-step pseudo-workflows
description:
WHAT: Persist accepted visualizer follow-up submissions into existing artifact structures so the visualizer can refresh and show them. Fresh one-off agent runs must become durable one-step pseudo-workflow runs, not a separate visualization model.

FOLLOW-UP SESSION ARTIFACTS: for true follow-up submissions, persist normal agent-session/agent-runner artifacts when enough data is available, with status submitted if terminal result is not known. Store source metadata: type visualizer-followup, mode follow-up-thread, sourceWorkflowRunId, sourceTargetId, sourceArtifactIds, user prompt length, target SHA/branch, and context delivery metadata.

FRESH PSEUDO-WORKFLOW: create .nax/workflows/<timestamp>-agent-run with a single step (title Fresh Agent Runner or similar), selected model runs, source metadata, target SHA/branch, and artifacts compatible with listRuns, getRunGraph, getRunDetails, and Recent Runs. Graph should naturally render as one node.

DO NOT: add a workflow-level followups.jsonl ledger in MVP. Do not fake graph nodes unless persistence supports them.

WHY: The UI should reuse the existing run list, graph, and details machinery for one-off agent runs. Submitted artifacts also make refresh/sync behavior coherent.

ACCEPTANCE: after accepted fresh runner submission, /api/runs lists a durable pseudo-workflow; /api/runs/:id/graph returns a one-node graph; /api/runs/:id/details opens normally; accepted follow-up session artifacts can be found by handoff sources or run details where persisted.
close_reason: Implemented submitted follow-up persistence for fresh one-step pseudo-workflows with renderable run-state artifacts; verified followup-persistence.test.js and npm run typecheck.

title: Wire RunDetailsModal to open RunFollowupModal
description:
WHAT: Add web/src/components/RunFollowupModal.tsx and wire the existing disabled Run a followup menu item in RunDetailsModal. The menu opens the composer modal; it never submits directly. Existing copy actions remain unchanged.

PROPS: composer receives opened, onClose, run, details, activeEntry, and onSubmitted. RunDetailsModal owns open/close state and passes the current details/entry. It refreshes details after successful submit.

INITIALIZATION: default target/artifacts/models come from details.followupTargets and details.followupArtifacts. If follow-up data is missing, the action should be disabled with a clear tooltip/disabled state, not a runtime crash.

LAYOUT: use Mantine controls already used in the app. Keep the modal dense and operational, not a landing-page experience. Required prompt box, mode selector, artifact picker, model chips, metadata preview, and submission summary all live in the composer.

ACCEPTANCE: clicking Run a followup opens the modal; closing preserves no stale submission state; missing follow-up contracts disable the action safely; existing RunDetailsModal content/timeline/TOC behavior remains unchanged.
close_reason: Wired RunDetailsModal actions menu to open RunFollowupModal with safe disabled state and refresh-on-submit; verified npm run typecheck and npm run visualize:build.

title: Build artifact picker, model chips, validation, and submission plan UI
description:
WHAT: Implement the actual composer interaction inside RunFollowupModal.

CONTROLS: segmented mode selector (Follow up on <Agent> Results / Start fresh agent runner), required textarea labelled What should the next agent do?, artifact picker with human outputs visible and advanced artifacts behind disclosure, model chips for Claude/Gemini/Codex, metadata preview, and pre-submit plan summary.

DEFAULTS: default target is the server-provided isDefault target. Default artifacts are defaultSelected artifacts. Default model is selected prior agent when available; Start fresh agent runner can default to previous workflow models or Codex fallback. User can change target, artifacts, and models before submitting.

VALIDATION: disable submit while prompt is empty, no model selected, no valid target for follow-up-thread, or artifact selection is invalid. Show clear inline messages. If artifact selection is empty, allow only when the user intentionally chooses no context, not accidentally from an uninitialized state.

SUBMISSION SUMMARY: show lines like Codex: follow-up session, Claude: fresh runner, Gemini: fresh runner so multi-model semantics are explicit before credits are spent.

ACCEPTANCE: frontend tests cover prompt required, default artifacts selected, model default matches prior result, multiple model mixed plan display, intentional no-context path, and request body construction.
close_reason: Built RunFollowupModal composer controls: target/mode selectors, model chips, artifact picker with advanced disclosure, no-context confirmation, required prompt validation, and plan/request helpers; verified run-followup-composer.test.ts, npm run typecheck, and npm run visualize:build.

title: Diff-scoped review context: changed-files ledger, --base, PR metadata
description:
WHY: Auto-injected context today = pinned SHA + open-PR ledger. The single highest-leverage context a reviewer-agent needs is missing: WHAT CHANGED. Agents burn their first N minutes rediscovering the diff scope, and on large repos they sometimes review the wrong thing entirely. nax already accepts --branch '#123' PR selectors but injects no PR metadata.

GOAL: every review-shaped flow knows the change surface before the agent starts.

PIECES: changed-files ledger vs a base ref (git diff --stat, capped), --base flag with sensible default (repo default branch via gh), and PR title/body/changed-files injection when branch is a PR selector. All three flow through the same auto-context section that --no-auto-context already disables, and through prompt-budget sections so big diffs compact instead of exploding prompts.

SERVES: better agent outputs for zero user effort — the purest form of accretive.

title: Changed-files ledger in auto-context + --base flag
description:
WHAT: Extend src/review-context.js buildAutomaticContext with a 'Changed files vs <base>' section: git diff --stat --find-renames <base>...<pinnedSha> rendered as a markdown list (path, +adds/-dels), capped at 50 files with '...and N more (run git diff --stat <base>...<sha>)' overflow line.

BASE RESOLUTION ORDER: --base <ref> flag > PR base branch when --branch is '#123' > repo default branch (gh repo view --json defaultBranchRef, cached per run) > origin/HEAD fallback. When base == pinned SHA (reviewing the default branch itself), section says 'reviewing branch tip, no base diff' rather than an empty list.

BUDGET: section registered as droppable tier-1 content in prompt-budget (big diffs are first to compact, the stat summary line always survives).

EDGES: shallow clones / missing base ref -> warn once, omit section, never fail the run; binary files shown without counts.

ACCEPTANCE: nax review --branch fix/auth shows the fix/auth-vs-default-branch file list inside the submitted prompt context block; --no-auto-context still removes everything.

title: PR metadata context when --branch is a PR selector
description:
WHAT: When --branch '#123' is used (PR selector already supported for checkout), inject a 'Pull request under review' context section: PR title, author, base<-head, body (truncated 2KB), review state counts, and link — via gh pr view 123 --json. Changed-files ledger (other bead) automatically uses the PR base as diff base.

WHY: cross-review and synthesize prompts gain the PR's stated INTENT, which is the thing agents cannot derive from the diff. Intent-vs-implementation mismatch is exactly the class of finding multi-model review is best at.

EDGES: PR body containing our own marker comments (comment-markers.js) stripped to avoid prompt-injection-by-recursion; closed/merged PR -> warn and proceed (still reviewable); fork PRs where head repo differs -> note it.

ACCEPTANCE: nax review --branch '#123' submitted prompts contain PR title/intent section; plain branch runs are unchanged.

title: Tests: diff ledger, base resolution, PR context
description:
WHAT: extend tests/unit/review-context.test.js with real temp git repos (init, commit, branch — the repo's existing test style already shells real git; no mocked git).

CASES:
- ledger correctness: known commits -> exact file list with counts; rename detected; 50-file cap + overflow line (generate 60 files); binary file row
- base resolution precedence: --base beats PR base beats default branch (seam for gh default-branch lookup); missing base ref -> section omitted + single warning
- base == HEAD case message
- PR metadata: gh seam returns fixture pr view payload (captured from a real gh call once, stored as fixture); body truncation at 2KB boundary; marker stripping; merged-PR warning path
- integration: full automatic context block ordering stable (sha, PR ledger, changed files, PR metadata) and registered section names match prompt-budget expectations

LOGGING: print built context block on failure.

title: nax watch: live status view for in-flight workflows
description:
WHAT: nax watch [run-id] attaches READ-ONLY to an in-flight (or most recent) workflow and renders a live status board: per step, per agent — status, elapsed, runner/session links, usage so far; refresh by polling workflow.json (written continuously by the orchestrating process) plus, for in-flight netlify-api runs, the same session-status endpoint waitForLocalAgentRuns uses. Re-renders boxes in place (reuse the progress-frame handling that the terminal-resize fix in commit dac0f3c touched); falls back to append-only lines in non-TTY.

USE CASES: (a) second terminal during a long run; (b) checking on a run the orchestrator of which is on another machine but shares the checkout; (c) demoing what nax is doing. Watch NEVER mutates state — no resume offers, no lock acquisition (explicitly compatible with the run-lock bead: read-only commands ignore lock).

EXIT: q or ctrl-c detaches; auto-exits with final summary when the watched run reaches terminal state; --once renders one frame and exits (scriptable, composes with --json for a machine status snapshot — reuses the json-mode summary shape).

TESTS (tests/unit/watch.test.js): frame rendering from fixture workflow.json states (running/partial/interrupted/done), terminal-state auto-exit, --once --json snapshot shape, non-TTY line mode, zero writes to .nax during watch (fs watcher assertion on the temp tree).

ACCEPTANCE: start a review in terminal A, nax watch in terminal B shows live per-agent progress and exits with the summary when A finishes.

title: Recorded-transcript test harness: capture real transport traffic, replay for execution tests
description:
WHY: flow-execution.test.js (80KB) is the crown jewel but every new transport-touching feature hand-builds seam stubs. There is no library of REAL transport payloads (Netlify runner API responses, gh CLI outputs), so stubs drift from reality — the classic way orchestration bugs survive testing. The repo rule is right: never test mocked behavior. The answer for orchestration logic is recorded real traffic, replayed deterministically.

DESIGN:
- NAX_RECORD=path env: when set, the gh-cli exec seam and local-runner fetch layer tee every request/response (redacting tokens, site ids, emails) into a transcript JSONL: { seq, channel: gh|netlify-api, request: {...}, response: {...}, durationMs }.
- tests/fixtures/transcripts/<scenario>.jsonl: checked-in recordings from real runs (a tiny public test repo + real Netlify site produce them; refresh occasionally via a documented script).
- Replay driver: feeds recorded responses back through the SAME seams in order, with mismatch diagnostics (expected request N to be POST /runners, got ...) so behavior drift fails loudly with context.
- This is NOT mocking logic — it is replaying captured real-system data through I/O boundaries, the recorded-fixture pattern. State files, interpolation, budget logic, artifact writing all run for real.

VALUE MULTIPLIER: every epic in this graph (retry, resume, findings, budget abort, watch) gains a realistic scenario harness; recording also doubles as a debugging tool (NAX_RECORD on a failing user run = attachable repro).

ACCEPTANCE: review-flow happy-path transcript replays green; corrupting one response field produces a pointed mismatch error naming seq + channel.

title: Transcript recorder at gh + netlify-api seams (NAX_RECORD)
description:
WHAT: Implement the recording tee described in the epic. Channels: src/gh-cli.js runGh (record argv, exit code, stdout, stderr) and the local-runner HTTP layer (method, path, request body, status, response body). Redaction pass before write: NETLIFY_AUTH_TOKEN values, Authorization headers, emails, site ids -> stable placeholders (<SITE_ID_1>) so transcripts are committable; placeholder mapping kept in-memory per recording for internal consistency.

FORMAT: JSONL, one envelope per exchange { seq, ts (relative ms from start, not wall clock), channel, request, response }. A header line records nax version + flow id + transport for provenance.

OVERHEAD: zero when NAX_RECORD unset (single env check); recording failures warn and disable recording, never affect the run.

Document in docs/ai/ how to record a fresh scenario (the refresh script bead of this epic automates it).

title: Replay driver + first recorded scenarios (review happy path, failure, resume)
description:
WHAT: tests/helpers/replay.js: load transcript JSONL, install response-feeding implementations into the gh/netlify seams, assert request sequence matches recording (method/path/key fields; configurable loose-match for volatile fields like timestamps). On mismatch: structured error with seq, channel, expected vs actual, and the last 3 exchanges for context.

SCENARIOS TO RECORD AND CHECK IN (from a real throwaway repo/site, redacted):
1. review flow, netlify-api, 3 agents, happy path end-to-end
2. one agent terminal failure mid-step (captures real failure response shapes)
3. interrupt + resume (two transcripts stitched: pre-kill and post-resume)
4. github-actions transport dispatch + artifact poll

TESTS: drive the actual handleRun engine against each replay; assert final workflow.json status/steps, artifact tree shape, summary.md headings. These become the regression net the retry/resume/findings epics extend with their own recordings.

REFRESH SCRIPT: scripts/record-transcripts.sh documenting/automating re-recording against the test repo (manual, occasional — recordings are durable fixtures, not per-CI).

title: Visualize Phase 2: browser dry-run and command preview
description: Add browser-triggered dry-run and command preview. The UI should let an operator choose branch, transport, model filters, context, step, and from-step controls, then run the same dry-run semantics as `nax run <flow> --dry --force` without creating `.nax` artifacts or remote work.
close_reason: Completed Phase 2 browser dry-run and command preview with validated API, subprocess preview execution, controls, output panel, and tests.

title: Define dry-run API option contract and validation
description:
WHAT: Define and implement the request/response contract for browser dry-runs. The endpoint should accept workflow id plus structured options for branch, transport, selected models, context text, step, fromStep, projectRoot-derived context, and future-safe flags. It must reject unknown workflow ids, invalid transports, invalid step ids, and invalid model names before invoking any command path.

WHY: Dry-run is the first mutating-shaped endpoint. Even though it should not create remote work, this is where the UI/server option validation shape gets established for real runs.

ACCEPTANCE: Invalid workflow/transport/step/model inputs return structured 400/404 JSON with stable codes. Valid inputs normalize into the same option names the CLI run path expects. No arbitrary command strings are accepted from the browser.
close_reason: Defined dry-run API contract with transport, branch, context, step/fromStep, and model validation plus token-protected POST semantics.

title: Add dry-run endpoint backed by existing Nax preview semantics
description:
WHAT: Add `POST /api/workflows/:id/dry-run` to the visualize server. It should execute the equivalent of `nax run <flow> --dry --force` with the validated options and return or stream the preview output without writing `.nax/` artifacts or creating GitHub/Netlify work.

IMPLEMENTATION OPTIONS: The fastest path is subprocess delegation with an argv array calling `node bin/nax.js run <flow> --dry --force ...`. A cleaner path is extracting the dry-run renderer, but extraction can wait until the orchestration refactor phase if subprocess delegation is simpler. In either case, never shell-concatenate user input.

ACCEPTANCE: Dry-run output matches the CLI preview closely enough for operator trust. Running dry-run from UI leaves no `.nax/` directory in a clean temp project. Exit code, stdout, stderr, duration, and command metadata are captured.
close_reason: Implemented POST /api/workflows/:id/dry-run backed by safe argv-array subprocess delegation to node bin/nax.js run --dry --force, returning command lifecycle/stdout/stderr metadata.

title: Build dry-run output panel and lifecycle states
description:
WHAT: Add a run panel in the UI for dry-run output. It should show lifecycle state, stdout/stderr or rendered text, validation errors, command metadata, exit code, and duration. It should handle long output without resizing the rest of the workbench unpredictably.

WHY: The browser UI should not hide the terminal truth. For early versions, showing the exact preview output is more valuable than inventing a separate visualization of dry-run semantics.

ACCEPTANCE: Submitting dry-run shows pending/running/succeeded/failed states. Errors are visible and actionable. The panel can be cleared or rerun. Output does not overlap the graph or inspector on narrow screens.
close_reason: Added dry-run output panel with running, idle, error, command, exit, duration, stdout, and stderr display states.

title: Add workflow option controls for dry-run preview
description:
WHAT: Add toolbar controls for dry-run options: branch, transport, selected agent models, context text, step, and from-step. These controls should be populated from the selected workflow where possible and should serialize into the dry-run API contract.

DETAILS: Keep dry-run as the primary action. Real run controls should remain disabled or absent in this phase. Model filters should update the UI preview state where practical, but the dry-run endpoint remains the source of truth for actual execution preview.

ACCEPTANCE: Operator can choose a workflow, adjust branch/transport/models/context/step/from-step, and submit dry-run. Invalid local form state is caught before request where obvious and revalidated on the server.
close_reason: Added branch, transport, step, from-step, context, and model controls that serialize into the dry-run API contract.

title: Test dry-run API, validation, and no-artifact behavior
description:
WHAT: Add tests for browser dry-run behavior. Cover server validation, no-artifact guarantee, valid command argv construction, subprocess failure reporting, and UI API-client behavior where practical.

DETAILS: Use temp project roots and existing node test patterns. Avoid real remote calls. For subprocess tests, use test seams or a harmless command path if available; assert no shell string is built from user input.

ACCEPTANCE: Tests prove `POST /api/workflows/review/dry-run` can run in a temp project without `.nax` writes, invalid ids/options return structured errors, and failures include stderr/exit metadata.
close_reason: Added dry-run API tests for token enforcement, invalid step/model validation, preview output, and no .nax artifact writes; reran browser smoke.

title: Dashboard React Query server-state standardization
description:
WHY
The dashboard currently hand-rolls server-state fetching, loading/error flags, refresh counters, polling, and mutation refresh logic across App.tsx and RunDetailsModal.tsx. That duplication creates stale-response races and divergent views of the same run data. The recent queued/running/completed status issues happened in this overlap between persisted server state, React Flow graph state, run-details state, and live SSE reducer state.

GOAL
Adopt @tanstack/react-query as the dashboard client server-state layer so requests to and from the local dashboard server are smart, deduped, cached, and invalidated through common hooks. This epic covers query infrastructure, typed query keys, shared read hooks, mutation hooks, REST polling ownership, SSE-to-cache reconciliation, cleanup of manual request plumbing, and tests.

BOUNDARIES
Use Query for ordinary REST server state and mutations. Keep UI-only state in React state. Keep the existing liveRunReducer and runEventsStream for the SSE event stream; do not replace live event state with Query. Do not introduce TanStack Router here; the routing plan is separate. Do not move copy/open-file button transient state into Query.

SOURCE PLAN
docs/ai/plans/nax-dashboard-react-query-server-state.md

ACCEPTANCE
- All ordinary dashboard REST reads have shared query hooks and typed query keys.
- Duplicate consumers share cache and in-flight requests for the same resource.
- Mutations patch or invalidate the relevant run caches in one place.
- SSE remains the immediate live-progress source, while terminal/durable events reconcile Query caches.
- Manual refreshKey-style broad reloads are removed or reduced to targeted query invalidation.
- npm run typecheck, npm run dashboard:build, and npm run dashboard:smoke pass at the end of the epic.

NON-GOALS
- No router migration.
- No SSE replacement.
- No optimistic remote agent completion/running states.
- No visual polish mixed into this state migration.
close_reason: Completed Dashboard React Query server-state standardization.

title: React Query provider, client defaults, and typed dashboard query keys
description:
SCOPE
Install @tanstack/react-query and add the dashboard Query infrastructure without migrating any fetches yet. This should be a no-behavior-change foundation commit.

WORK
- Add @tanstack/react-query to package dependencies.
- Create src/dashboard/web/src/query-client.ts with a single dashboardQueryClient. Defaults should be conservative for the local dashboard: retry 1 for queries, retry 0 for mutations, refetchOnWindowFocus false, staleTime around 1000ms, gcTime around 5 minutes.
- Create src/dashboard/web/src/query-keys.ts with typed key factories under one dashboard namespace: health, workflows, workflowGraph(workflowId), runs, runGraph(runId), runDetails(runId).
- Wrap the Vite dashboard React root in QueryClientProvider.
- Do not migrate App.tsx or RunDetailsModal fetches in this bead except provider wiring.

REASONING
This isolates dependency/provider risk from semantic state migration. If this bead regresses anything, the problem is package/provider setup rather than cache behavior. Query keys must be centralized immediately so later beads do not invent ad hoc arrays.

ACCEPTANCE
- Dashboard still loads with no visible behavior change.
- No ordinary REST reads are migrated yet.
- Query key factories are imported from one module and use only server-fetch inputs.
- npm run typecheck passes.
- npm run dashboard:build passes per repo UI-change rule.

FILES LIKELY TO TOUCH
- package.json/package-lock.json
- src/dashboard/web/src/main.tsx or equivalent dashboard entry
- src/dashboard/web/src/query-client.ts
- src/dashboard/web/src/query-keys.ts
close_reason: Completed React Query provider, client defaults, and typed dashboard query keys.

title: E2E smoke coverage for deduped dashboard server-state behavior
description:
SCOPE
Extend or document dashboard smoke coverage so the React Query migration is verified from the user path, not only helper tests.

WORK
- Inspect tests/e2e/dashboard.spec.js and the current dashboard smoke harness before changing it.
- Add pragmatic smoke coverage for the high-risk Query migration paths when feasible:
  - workflow list and graph load through the shared query path
  - selecting a Recent Run loads a concrete run graph
  - opening details for the same run from two surfaces does not create duplicate visible state or stale timeline content
  - cancelling/review/follow-up action paths update graph/details through invalidation rather than manual refresh where fake/test server support exists
- If direct network request counting is easy in Playwright, assert no duplicate GET /api/runs/:id/details in a repeated-open scenario while an in-flight request is shared; if it is brittle, prefer behavior assertions and leave request-counting to targeted unit/integration tests.
- Keep logs detailed: print route/request counts or selected run ids on failure.
- Keep the smoke test offline/local and compatible with existing dashboard test setup.

REASONING
The migration changes request ownership. Helper tests catch key/cache bugs, but a smoke path proves users can still load workflows, inspect runs, and open details without stale state.

ACCEPTANCE
- npm run dashboard:smoke passes.
- The smoke test covers at least one workflow-definition graph path and one concrete run/details path.
- Any request-count assertions are robust and do not flake on background refetches.
- Failure output includes enough request/run context for debugging.

FILES LIKELY TO TOUCH
- tests/e2e/dashboard.spec.js
- possibly test fixture setup for dashboard runs
close_reason: Updated dashboard smoke coverage for deduped server-state behavior.

title: Shared read query hooks for health, workflows, and runs
description:
SCOPE
Create the common read-query hook layer and migrate the simplest App.tsx server reads: health, workflows, and recent runs.

WORK
- Create src/dashboard/web/src/queries/dashboard-queries.ts or an equivalent queries module.
- Add useDashboardHealthQuery(), useWorkflowsQuery(), and useRunsQuery().
- Keep src/dashboard/web/src/api.ts as the low-level request boundary; hooks should wrap getHealth, listWorkflows, and listRuns.
- Move or reuse pure run-list helpers so useRunsQuery can expose the merged active+durable run list without duplicating merge behavior.
- Replace the App.tsx getHealth effect with healthQuery-derived projectRoot.
- Replace the App.tsx listWorkflows effect with workflowsQuery-derived workflows.
- Replace refreshRuns/listRuns effect paths where possible with useRunsQuery and targeted refetch/invalidation.
- Preserve selectedWorkflowId as local UI/navigation state until the separate router plan lands.
- Preserve default workflow selection behavior after workflows load.

REASONING
Health/workflows/runs are the lowest-risk reads and establish the hook style before graph/detail migration. They also remove the first broad refreshKey use cases.

ACCEPTANCE
- Workflow list loads and default selection still works.
- Project root still renders in the header.
- Recent Runs still renders active and durable runs in the same order/shape as before.
- loadingWorkflows/error display are derived from query state or intentionally bridged during migration.
- Existing manual UI state remains local.
- npm run typecheck passes.
- npm run dashboard:build passes.

FILES LIKELY TO TOUCH
- src/dashboard/web/src/queries/dashboard-queries.ts
- src/dashboard/web/src/App.tsx
- src/dashboard/web/src/run-list helpers if extracted
- tests if helper extraction creates pure functions worth covering
close_reason: Completed shared React Query read hooks for health, workflows, and runs.

title: Migrate workflow definition graph reads to React Query
description:
SCOPE
Move the workflow-definition graph read path from manual effects/local graph state to useWorkflowGraphQuery while preserving configure-mode behavior.

WORK
- Add useWorkflowGraphQuery(workflowId, options) if it was not completed in the read-hooks bead.
- Replace the App.tsx getWorkflowGraph(selectedWorkflowId) effect for workflow-definition mode.
- Derive the displayed graph from workflowGraphQuery.data when there is no selected run id.
- Keep selectedWorkflowId, selectedNode, dryRunOptions, dry-run simulation timers, and URL compatibility local.
- Preserve side effects on workflow changes: clear dry-run simulation, reset live reducer, reset workflow-specific dry-run options, and maintain the existing ?workflow= compatibility until router migration.
- Keep background refetches from blanking the existing graph unless the workflow id changed.

REASONING
Workflow definition graphs are normal server reads. Moving this before run graphs keeps configure-mode behavior isolated from run-inspection behavior.

ACCEPTANCE
- Switching workflows loads the correct graph.
- Agent pill toggles still modify dryRunOptions in configure mode.
- Step clicks still update the inspector, not the run-details modal, in configure mode.
- Dry-run simulation still operates on the current workflow graph.
- Rapid workflow switching does not leave a stale graph visible for the wrong workflow id.
- npm run typecheck passes.
- npm run dashboard:build passes.

FILES LIKELY TO TOUCH
- src/dashboard/web/src/queries/dashboard-queries.ts
- src/dashboard/web/src/App.tsx
- possibly src/dashboard/web/src/components/WorkflowCanvas.tsx only if loading-state props need tightening
close_reason: Migrated workflow definition graph reads to React Query.

title: Migrate selected run graph fetch and polling to React Query
description:
SCOPE
Move concrete run graph loading and active-run REST polling into useRunGraphQuery while preserving inspect-mode graph behavior and live SSE overlay behavior.

WORK
- Add useRunGraphQuery(runId, options).
- Change selectRun(run) to select the run id and clear/prepare local UI state, not directly fetch getRunGraph.
- Hydrate local UI from runGraphQuery.data: selectedWorkflowId, dryRunOptions branch/transport/step/fromStep, stepModelsFromRunGraph(response.graph), selectedNode reset, and current URL compatibility.
- Derive the rendered graph from runGraphQuery.data when selectedRunId is set; derive from workflowGraphQuery otherwise.
- Replace the manual 7000ms getRunGraph(selectedRunId) polling effect with a conditional query refetchInterval. Use false when no selected run, graph absent, or historical graph has no active remote runs; use 7000ms only while graphHasActiveRemoteRuns(graph) is true.
- When a run graph response includes response.run, patch that run into the runs query cache instead of maintaining a separate local setRuns path.
- Keep the live reducer overlay intact; do not use Query for SSE status events.

REASONING
Run graph is the most visible duplicate server state. It drives React Flow, selected run metadata, and the details modal. Query should own the durable run graph while liveRunReducer remains the immediate active-run overlay.

ACCEPTANCE
- Selecting a Recent Run loads the correct concrete run graph.
- Loaded run graph stays in inspect mode: card/agent clicks open run details, not configure controls.
- Completed historical runs do not poll forever.
- Active run graphs refetch while remote work is active and stop polling after terminal state.
- Run graph responses update the Recent Runs cache without manual refresh.
- npm run typecheck passes.
- npm run dashboard:build passes.

FILES LIKELY TO TOUCH
- src/dashboard/web/src/queries/dashboard-queries.ts
- src/dashboard/web/src/App.tsx
- cache helper module if run-list patching is extracted
- tests for active graph polling predicate if extracted
close_reason: Migrated selected run graph fetch and polling to React Query.

title: Migrate RunDetailsModal details fetch, polling, and live refresh to React Query
description:
SCOPE
Centralize GET /api/runs/:id/details through useRunDetailsQuery and remove RunDetailsModal manual fetch/loading/error/polling state.

WORK
- Add useRunDetailsQuery(runId, options).
- Replace detailsLoading, detailsError, detailsResponse, and refreshDetails() with the query result in RunDetailsModal.
- Keep UI-only state local: activeTimelineId, selectionWarning, detailsView, contentView, followupSubmitting, TOC state, and scroll state.
- Keep initialSelector out of the query key; it should affect active timeline selection only, not cache identity.
- Keep liveRevision out of the query key; liveRevision should debounce a refetch or query invalidation for the same runDetails(runId) key.
- Replace cancellable-entry 7-second manual polling with query refetchInterval while the modal is open and any entry has a cancel target.
- Convert cancel/review/follow-up refresh call sites to query refetch or invalidation, pending the mutation-hook bead.
- Preserve queued/running/completed timeline labels and live queued/running panel behavior.

REASONING
Run details can be opened from Recent Runs and graph step/agent clicks. A shared query prevents duplicate in-flight details requests and lets mutation/live refreshes reconcile one cache entry instead of overwriting component-local copies.

ACCEPTANCE
- Opening run details from Recent Runs still defaults to summary.
- Opening details from graph step/agent still resolves the correct initial timeline entry.
- Reopening details for the same run reuses cached data while refetching as appropriate.
- Details stay visible during background polling/refetch; no blanking on every refresh.
- Cancellable entries still refresh periodically.
- Live revisions can refresh details without creating new cache entries.
- npm run typecheck passes.
- npm run dashboard:build passes.

FILES LIKELY TO TOUCH
- src/dashboard/web/src/queries/dashboard-queries.ts
- src/dashboard/web/src/components/RunDetailsModal.tsx
- possibly RunFollowupModal/RunFollowupContent if details access is threaded differently
close_reason: Migrated RunDetailsModal details fetch, polling, and live refresh to React Query.

title: Dashboard mutation hooks and centralized cache invalidation
description:
SCOPE
Introduce Query mutation hooks for dashboard server writes and move cache update/invalidation rules out of component event handlers.

WORK
- Create src/dashboard/web/src/queries/dashboard-mutations.ts or equivalent.
- Add mutation hooks for runWorkflowDryRun, startWorkflowRun, cancelWorkflowRun, cancelFollowupRun, approveHumanReviewGate, cancelHumanReviewGate, and startRunFollowup.
- Keep openLocalFile out of Query for now; it is local OS side-effect state with no durable cache.
- Create cache helpers as needed: replaceRunInList, upsertRunInRunsCache, maybe invalidateRunViews(queryClient, runId).
- On start run: upsert returned run in runs cache if possible and invalidate runs.
- On cancel workflow run: upsert returned run, invalidate runGraph(runId), invalidate runDetails(runId).
- On cancel follow-up: upsert returned source run, invalidate source run graph/details.
- On approve/cancel review: upsert returned run when available and invalidate run graph/details.
- On start follow-up: invalidate runs, source run details, source run graph, and any persisted follow-up run views returned by the response.
- Replace component-local refreshDetails/refreshRuns/selectRun reload chains gradually with mutation onSuccess cache behavior.
- Do not optimistically mark remote agents completed/running; prefer returned server state or invalidation.

REASONING
Mutations are where stale cache bugs most often appear. Centralizing invalidation makes server writes predictable and prevents every UI surface from inventing its own refresh behavior.

ACCEPTANCE
- Start run updates Recent Runs without a broad manual refresh.
- Cancel active run updates run list, graph, and details after the server response.
- Follow-up submit and cancel update the source run views.
- Review approve/cancel updates the relevant step/run views.
- Mutation errors remain visible at the relevant action surface.
- No optimistic remote completion/running state is introduced.
- npm run typecheck passes.
- npm run dashboard:build passes.

FILES LIKELY TO TOUCH
- src/dashboard/web/src/queries/dashboard-mutations.ts
- src/dashboard/web/src/query-keys.ts
- src/dashboard/web/src/App.tsx
- src/dashboard/web/src/components/RunDetailsModal.tsx
- src/dashboard/web/src/components/RunFollowupModal.tsx
- cache helper tests
close_reason: Added dashboard mutation hooks with centralized cache invalidation.

title: Bridge SSE lifecycle events to React Query cache reconciliation
description:
SCOPE
Add a narrow bridge from live RunnerEvent handling to Query cache operations while keeping the SSE reducer as the live-progress source.

WORK
- Add a helper that accepts queryClient, RunnerEvent, and enough UI context to know the current/open run ids when needed.
- Call the helper from the existing runEventsStream event dispatch path after dispatchLiveRun.
- On workflow_started: patch/seed active run state as today and invalidate or patch runs cache.
- On artifact_written: invalidate runDetails(runId) if that details query is active/cached or the modal is open for that run; optionally invalidate runGraph(runId) only if graph artifact presence depends on it.
- On terminal workflow/exit events: invalidate runs, runGraph(runId), and runDetails(runId) when applicable.
- Do not invalidate on every agent_status or step_status; liveRunReducer already updates immediate UI and high-frequency invalidation would waste requests.
- Keep runEventsStream reconnect logic outside Query.

REASONING
Query should reconcile durable server snapshots at meaningful boundaries, not fight the live reducer on every status tick. This bead protects responsiveness while ensuring terminal/artifact state is eventually reflected in cached server views.

ACCEPTANCE
- Active run progress still updates immediately through liveRunReducer.
- Terminal run events refresh Recent Runs and concrete run graph without manual refresh.
- New artifacts can appear in an open/cached RunDetailsModal after event-driven invalidation.
- Frequent step_status/agent_status events do not trigger repeated REST refetches.
- SSE reconnect behavior remains unchanged.
- npm run typecheck passes.
- npm run dashboard:build passes.

FILES LIKELY TO TOUCH
- src/dashboard/web/src/App.tsx
- src/dashboard/web/src/liveRunReducer.ts only if event typing needs refinement
- src/dashboard/web/src/queries/query-event-bridge.ts or equivalent
- tests for event-to-invalidation helper if kept pure
close_reason: Bridged SSE lifecycle events to React Query cache reconciliation.

title: Remove duplicate manual request plumbing after Query migration
description:
SCOPE
After read hooks, graph/details queries, mutations, and SSE cache reconciliation land, remove obsolete manual request state and broad refresh plumbing from the dashboard.

WORK
- Remove refreshKey if all remaining refresh paths can use queryClient.invalidateQueries/refetchQueries.
- Remove refreshRuns() once all callers use useRunsQuery or mutation cache helpers.
- Remove manual loading/error flags that are fully represented by query or mutation state.
- Remove stale cancelled-effect guards for migrated fetches.
- Consolidate statusText from query states and selected workflow/run state.
- Search for direct ordinary REST reads in components after migration; getRunDetails, getRunGraph, getWorkflowGraph, listRuns, listWorkflows, and getHealth should be used only inside query hooks or low-level API tests.
- Keep direct start/cancel/follow-up/open-file calls only where intentionally wrapped by mutation hooks or explicitly excluded.

REASONING
The migration only pays off if the old manual paths disappear. Leaving both systems in place would recreate duplicate sources of truth and make future bugs harder to diagnose.

ACCEPTANCE
- App.tsx no longer has duplicated manual loading/error state for migrated REST reads.
- Components do not directly call ordinary GET API functions after migration.
- Manual refresh uses targeted query invalidation.
- Existing local UI state remains local.
- npm run typecheck passes.
- npm run dashboard:build passes.
- npm run dashboard:smoke passes before epic closure.

FILES LIKELY TO TOUCH
- src/dashboard/web/src/App.tsx
- src/dashboard/web/src/components/RunDetailsModal.tsx
- any query/mutation modules created by this epic
close_reason: Removed duplicate manual dashboard request plumbing after Query migration and after query/e2e coverage closed.

title: Tests: query keys, cache helpers, and query migration invariants
description:
SCOPE
Add focused automated tests for the pure pieces introduced by the React Query migration, with enough coverage to catch cache-key and cache-update regressions before UI smoke tests.

WORK
- Add unit tests for dashboardQueryKeys: stable namespace, expected tuple structure, server ids included exactly, UI-only fields excluded.
- Add tests for mergeRunLists/replaceRunInList/upsertRunInRunsCache or equivalent cache helpers. Cover matching by runId and id, active+durable merge order, and replacement without duplication.
- Add tests for any extracted graph-active polling predicate: completed historical graphs should not poll, active remote graphs should poll.
- Add tests for SSE event-to-query invalidation helper if implemented as a pure helper: terminal events invalidate runs/runGraph/runDetails; high-frequency agent_status/step_status do not.
- If a lightweight component harness is practical, test RunDetailsModal under QueryClientProvider to verify initialSelector changes selected timeline without creating a different query key. Do not overbuild this if the current test stack makes it brittle.
- Ensure failure output prints the involved keys/cache payloads for quick diagnosis.

REASONING
Query migrations often fail through subtle key mismatches and cache replacement bugs. These helper tests are much cheaper and more reliable than trying to catch every case through Playwright.

ACCEPTANCE
- tests/unit/dashboard-query-keys.test.ts or equivalent exists.
- tests/unit/dashboard-query-cache.test.ts or equivalent exists.
- Tests run under the repo test command or a documented node --import tsx --test invocation.
- Tests include detailed assertion messages or logged payloads on failure.
- npm run typecheck passes.
- npm test or the relevant unit test command passes.

FILES LIKELY TO TOUCH
- tests/unit/dashboard-query-keys.test.ts
- tests/unit/dashboard-query-cache.test.ts
- src/dashboard/web/src/query-keys.ts
- cache helper module
- query event bridge helper
close_reason: Added query key, cache helper, and migration invariant unit coverage.

title: Tier 1+2: blob offload hardening, semantic flow validation, branch truth contract
description:
Convert docs/ai/plans/nax-tier1-tier2-plan.md into implementation work. The plan has three ordered workstreams: A) prompt blob offload is already mostly built, with hosted Agent Runner blob access manually verified, and now needs repeatable regression coverage plus hardening; B) semantic flow validation must fail malformed flows before spending credits; C) branch truth must become one immutable resolved target persisted in workflow.json and consumed everywhere instead of repeatedly re-deriving branch/SHA.

Overall intent: stop large fan-in runs from hitting argv/E2BIG, prevent malformed flows from burning agent credits, and eliminate silent branch drift between recorded metadata and the code agents actually review. Preserve the explicit invariants from the plan: do not silently truncate context after blob failures; keep the file-backed blob registry rather than moving to Netlify DB; treat GitHub Actions checkout truth as implicit/unverified unless a future cross-repo dispatch-with-ref design lands.

Implementation order: A hardening first, B can proceed in parallel, C last because it touches workflow.json, branch resolution, resume, artifacts, visualization, and both transports.
close_reason: Implemented and verified with npm run check, npm test, and diff-only no-any scan.

title: A: Prompt blob offload prove/harden/finish
description:
Workstream A from the Tier 1+2 plan. Prompt blob offload exists and is wired into the live submission path through src/netlify-blobs.js, src/prompt-offload.js, src/blob-ref-registry.js, src/blob-debug-cache.js, and bin/nax.js. Hosted Agent Runner blob access has been manually verified: local nax can write a Netlify Blob with a full PAT, and a hosted pod with scoped runner token can read the same store/key through the emitted netlify blobs:get command.

Remaining goal is not to rebuild the feature. It is to turn the manual runner-read proof into repeatable coverage, close hardening gaps found by review, and verify the large fan-in E2BIG scenario completes with bounded prompt delivery. Keep the invariant that if offload and safe compaction fail, nax fails before submit with byte counts and remediation hints rather than silently truncating context.
close_reason: Implemented and verified with npm run check, npm test, and diff-only no-any scan.

title: A0: Make hosted-runner blob read-back a repeatable probe
description:
Turn the manually verified hosted Agent Runner blob-read contract into a repeatable regression check. The external contract is: local nax writes a blob with full PAT credentials; the hosted runner pod reads the same store/key using scoped NETLIFY_AUTH_TOKEN and SITE_ID/NETLIFY_SITE_ID; the emitted netlify blobs:get command and hardcoded hosted CLI path work inside the pod.

Work:
- Run or adapt tests/integration/blob-roundtrip-probe.test.js with NAX_NETLIFY_BLOB_ROUNDTRIP_E2E=1 and NETLIFY_SITE_ID set against a real/disposable site.
- Capture the three risk points explicitly: hosted CLI path exists, runner env exposes usable auth/site id, local-principal write is readable by pod-principal read.
- Add scheduled/release-gated CI or a credential-gated smoke path so runner image, token scope, site-id propagation, and Netlify CLI behavior regress loudly.
- Keep unit tests as fast mocks, but document that they do not prove the external runner contract.

Acceptance:
- The probe can be run by one command documented in repo tooling.
- A missing credential skips or fails with a clear message, never a misleading unit-test pass.
- CI/release smoke can prove the hosted-read contract when credentials are present.
close_reason: Implemented and verified with npm run check, npm test, and diff-only no-any scan.

title: A9: Prevent GitHub blob cleanup before async consumers fetch
description:
Fix consensus S4. bin/nax.js cleanupWorkflowBlobsForRun can delete GitHub offload blobs after executeGithubFlow returns, but a custom GitHub step that does not waitFor agent-results may still have an async remote consumer that has not fetched the blob. Shipped fan-in flows wait for results, so this is latent, but custom flows make it real.

Work:
- Identify cleanup call sites for GitHub/off-runner consumers.
- For GitHub transport, only delete a blob when the consuming run completed, or when a context-fetch confirmation was recorded.
- If completion/fetch confirmation is uncertain, leave the ref active/pending for later TTL sweep rather than deleting early.
- Keep local success/failure cleanup behavior intact where consumers are known complete.

Tests:
- GitHub step without waitFor and with offloaded blob does not delete blob at end-of-flow.
- GitHub step with agent-results completion or confirmed fetch can clean safely.
- Remaining refs are sweepable by nax clean blobs.
close_reason: Implemented and verified with npm run check, npm test, and diff-only no-any scan.

title: A10: Fix context fetch classification to use command transcript proof
description:
Fix A.1n and fold in S6 if appropriate. classifyContextFetch currently inspects final assistant prose for markers/sentinel and uses a broad error regex that can match normal discussion of blobs:get, NETLIFY_AUTH_TOKEN, or blob errors. Runner blob access is verified, but exported artifacts can still report Offloaded context: failed when the classifier reads the wrong surface or treats prose as command failure.

Work:
- Audit applyContextFetchClassification and all surfaces it receives: final result text, command/tool transcript, runner/session metadata, and artifacts.
- Detect NAX-CONTEXT-LOADED and NAX-BLOB-SENTINEL from the command/tool transcript where blobs:get output actually appears, not only final prose.
- Treat real fetch failure as a non-zero command result or structured command error, not a substring in assistant prose.
- Remove the dead inlineOnlyNeedles branch or wire real inline-only needles from delivery metadata so the branch can fire meaningfully.
- Keep contextFetchStatus advisory, but make it trustworthy enough for cleanup decisions where used.

Tests:
- Final prose that discusses blobs:get/NETLIFY_AUTH_TOKEN is not classified as failed.
- Command transcript containing marker/sentinel is classified confirmed even if final reply omits the marker.
- Non-zero fetch command or explicit command error is classified failed.
- Dead inlineOnlyNeedles behavior is either removed or covered by real metadata-driven tests.
close_reason: Implemented and verified with npm run check, npm test, and diff-only no-any scan.

title: A11: Compact blob ref registry during clean
description:
Fix consensus S7. src/blob-ref-registry.js is append-only: each status transition appends a full record, and latestBlobRefs re-reads/de-dupes the whole file. That is acceptable short-term but long-lived projects will accumulate duplicate historical rows.

Work:
- Add registry compaction during nax clean blobs or sweep flow.
- Keep only the latest record per blob id while preserving enough audit detail for active/pending/error statuses.
- Make compaction atomic: write a temp file, fsync/rename or equivalent safe replace.
- Never drop active refs accidentally; prefer conservative retention on parse errors.

Tests:
- Registry with multiple status rows per id compacts to latest row per id.
- Active, cleaned, failed, pending-cleanup statuses retain correct latest state.
- Malformed JSONL line handling remains safe and diagnostic.
close_reason: Implemented and verified with npm run check, npm test, and diff-only no-any scan.

title: A12: Full oversized fan-in verification run
description:
Final A definition-of-done verification. Prove the original large fan-in/E2BIG failure is gone in a realistic flow, with safe prompt bytes tuned against real data and artifacts explaining delivery mode.

Work:
- Run a full ideas/review-style fan-in flow that previously exceeded argv limits.
- Ensure submitted prompt wrappers stay <= NAX_SAFE_PROMPT_BYTES and no local-runner args.push('--prompt', promptText) path receives an unsafe prompt.
- Verify promptDelivery/blobRefs/contextFetchStatus are visible in artifacts and visualization/handoff data.
- Record final NAX_SAFE_PROMPT_BYTES tuning rationale if adjusted.

Acceptance:
- Flow completes without E2BIG or manual trimming.
- Artifacts show blob delivery mode and no misleading context fetch failure after classifier fixes.
- Any remaining medium blob portability/cleanup items are explicitly deferred with rationale, not forgotten.
close_reason: Implemented and verified with npm run check, npm test, and diff-only no-any scan.

title: A1: Add local blob offload token/site preflight guard
description:
Close A.1a. The local ensureStepBlobOffload path needs the same explicit site/token guard the GitHub path has. setBlob requires netlify.siteId and NETLIFY_AUTH_TOKEN; without a guard the local path can fail late with raw CLI detail or fall into confusing behavior.

Work:
- Find the local path through prepareLocalPromptDelivery and ensureStepBlobOffload.
- Before attempting upload, verify Netlify site context and auth token are available.
- If compactPromptBytes is safely within NAX_SAFE_PROMPT_BYTES, fall back to compact delivery with an explicit warning; otherwise fail before submit with a clear actionable error naming NETLIFY_SITE_ID/site linking and NETLIFY_AUTH_TOKEN.
- Preserve fail-before-submit: do not send an oversized prompt or silently truncate.

Tests:
- Offload attempted with missing token/site falls back only when compact is safe.
- Unsafe compact plus missing token/site fails before runner creation.
- Error output is captured and asserted; no raw token/CLI stderr leaks.
close_reason: Implemented and verified with npm run check, npm test, and diff-only no-any scan.

title: A2: Verify retry path preserves offloaded prompt wrappers
description:
Close A.1b. src/local-runner.js still has older argv-limit retry compaction logic. Now that prepareLocalPromptDelivery can submit a small blob wrapper up front, retry handling must not re-expand or double-compact an already-offloaded run, and run.blobRef/promptDelivery metadata must survive retry.

Work:
- Trace waitForLocalAgentRuns and compactPromptForArgumentLimitRetry around src/local-runner.js:815-892.
- Detect already-offloaded prompt delivery and preserve the wrapper on retry.
- Ensure retry metadata, blobRef, promptDelivery, and contextFetch policy survive attempts.
- Keep legacy compaction behavior only for genuinely inline prompts that still hit argv limits.

Tests:
- Simulated argv-limit retry on an offloaded run keeps the wrapper text under budget and preserves blobRef.
- Simulated argv-limit retry on an inline oversized legacy path still compacts/fails as expected.
- Workflow artifacts record the final delivery mode accurately after retry.
close_reason: Implemented and verified with npm run check, npm test, and diff-only no-any scan.

title: A3: Verify Phase-0 compaction budget actually shrinks below safe bytes
description:
Close A.1c. The original prompt blob plan noted a Phase-0 bug where compactPromptText could equal promptText because the compaction ceiling was about 48KB while the failing prompt was about 45KB. Verify whether the fix actually landed in the current code and repair it if not.

Work:
- Inspect prompt compaction budget derivation used before offload fallback.
- Ensure compact targets NAX_SAFE_PROMPT_BYTES, not a stale 48KB-ish ceiling, when the failure mode is argv safety.
- Preserve structured findings/essential metadata according to existing prompt-offload invariants; do not silently truncate arbitrary mid-section content.

Tests:
- Oversized fan-in fixture produces compactPromptText byte length < original prompt byte length.
- compactPromptText byte length <= NAX_SAFE_PROMPT_BYTES.
- Boundary cases include exactly safe bytes, one byte over, and multibyte Unicode.
close_reason: Implemented and verified with npm run check, npm test, and diff-only no-any scan.

title: A4: Wire SIGINT/interrupted-run blob cleanup semantics
description:
Close A.1d. Cleanup currently fires on success/failure, but interrupted runs such as Ctrl-C can orphan blobs until TTL sweep. The graceful-shutdown path should leave blob refs in a recoverable state and make sweep behavior explicit.

Work:
- Trace graceful shutdown/interruption handling and cleanupWorkflowBlobsForRun.
- On interrupt, mark active blob refs pending-cleanup or clean them when safe without breaking resumability.
- Ensure sweepBlobRefs can collect interrupted-run refs after TTL and reports failures clearly.
- Avoid premature deletion for remote consumers; that separate GitHub race is covered by the GitHub cleanup bead.

Tests:
- Simulated interrupt after blob upload records pending-cleanup state.
- nax clean blobs/sweep deletes or reports the pending ref deterministically.
- Resumable workflow state is not corrupted by cleanup metadata.
close_reason: Implemented and verified with npm run check, npm test, and diff-only no-any scan.

title: A5: Serialize promptDelivery/blob metadata into artifacts
description:
Close A.1e. Ensure workflow artifacts serialize new prompt delivery metadata so visualization, handoff, and debugging can explain when context was offloaded and whether fetch verification was confirmed.

Work:
- Audit src/workflow-artifacts.js builders for promptDelivery, blobRefs, contextFetchStatus, contextFetchSignals, contextFetchConfirmed, safePromptBytes, offloadedPromptBytes, and local debug cache paths.
- Ensure summary.md and step/runner JSON preserve enough detail to diagnose offload mode without reopening workflow.json.
- Keep secret hygiene: literal env variable names are fine, actual NETLIFY_AUTH_TOKEN values must never be serialized.

Tests:
- A run with an offloaded step round-trips blob metadata through artifact build.
- summary.md and step.json agree on delivery mode and fetch status.
- Serialized artifacts contain no token values.
close_reason: Implemented and verified with npm run check, npm test, and diff-only no-any scan.

title: A6: Decide and document getBlob's production role
description:
Close A.1f. src/netlify-blobs.js exports getBlob, but it is currently dead outside tests. Decide whether nax itself should use getBlob to reconstruct prompts in show/visualize/debug flows, or whether it is intentionally present only to support runner/probe/test behavior.

Work:
- Search for all getBlob uses and adjacent debug/visualization needs.
- If useful, add a small nax-side read path for reconstructing full offloaded prompts from refs/debug cache with safe auth handling.
- If not useful now, annotate or document the intentional non-use so future maintainers do not treat it as accidental dead code.

Acceptance:
- The codebase no longer has ambiguous dead exported API around getBlob.
- If wired, tests prove prompt reconstruction works and does not leak secrets.
- If documented only, the comment/docs are concise and explain why deleteBlob/setBlob remain production-critical while getBlob is probe/debug oriented.
close_reason: Implemented and verified with npm run check, npm test, and diff-only no-any scan.

title: A7: Fix standalone nax issue offload context threading and blob registration
description:
Fix consensus S1/S2 together. Standalone nax issue calls ensureGithubPlanBlobOffload without stepState, then bin/nax.js:1077 dereferences stepState.promptBlobRef. The TypeError is caught as an offload failure and silently degrades to compact fallback, so prior-results blob offload never runs for standalone issues. The same missing run/project context means uploaded standalone issue blobs are not registered in .nax/blob-refs.jsonl, so nax clean blobs cannot sweep them.

Work:
- Add the immediate stepState?.promptBlobRef guard to remove the crash.
- Thread real projectRoot, runState, stepState, and step context through handleIssue/standalone issue generation, matching executeGithubFlow where possible.
- Prefer sharing ensureStepBlobOffload rather than duplicating GitHub-specific upload/registry/debug-cache logic.
- Ensure every successful upload, including no-stepState/standalone issue paths, registers a sweepable blob ref.
- Keep fallback behavior explicit and visible; no misleading silent downgrade.

Tests:
- Standalone issue path with oversized prior results offloads or cleanly falls back without throwing.
- Blob refs from standalone issue upload appear in the registry with projectRoot-backed state.
- Re-running cleanup can sweep those refs.
close_reason: Implemented and verified with npm run check, npm test, and diff-only no-any scan.

title: A8: Render blob fetch command through runner capability
description:
Fix consensus S3. src/prompt-offload.js hardcodes RUNNER_NETLIFY_CLI_PATH and env assumptions into every fetch instruction. This is acceptable for today's hosted Netlify Agent Runner pod and does not currently prove GitHub Actions is broken, because GitHub dispatch still executes the agent in that pod. It is still a portability flaw and should become an explicit runner/transport capability.

Work:
- Introduce a fetch command rendering seam, e.g. buildBlobFetchInstruction({ runnerCapabilities, ref }) or a transport capability object.
- Hosted Netlify runner remains the default: /opt/buildhome/node-deps/node_modules/.bin/netlify plus NETLIFY_AUTH_TOKEN and SITE_ID/NETLIFY_SITE_ID handling.
- Add explicit override/PATH fallback for future runtimes; a shell [ -x hostedPath ] fallback to netlify is acceptable if it keeps prompts short and readable.
- Document the runner contract: POSIX shell, Netlify CLI availability, token env, site id env, blob read scope.

Tests:
- Hosted default renders the existing working command shape.
- Alternate capability renders a PATH/explicit command without changing blob ref/store/key semantics.
- Current GitHub transport is classified as hosted-runner dispatch, not as a separate GitHub VM execution environment.
close_reason: Implemented and verified with npm run check, npm test, and diff-only no-any scan.

title: B: Semantic flow validator before submission
description:
Workstream B from the Tier 1+2 plan. Build a pure semantic validator for the resolved flow object so broken flows fail before any agent submission or credit spend. Validate after configorama has loaded/evaluated JS/TS/JSON/YAML/TOML flows and normalizeFlow has produced the runtime shape; do not validate raw syntax in a way that rejects legitimate dynamic config.

The validator should accumulate all errors with step ids, diagnostic codes, messages, and fix hints. Structural errors should throw/block execution: missing prompt files, input.step references to nonexistent or later steps, invalid submit/action enums. Agent validation belongs after interactive/model selection where the selected/known model registry is available. Dry-run must also validate.
close_reason: Implemented and verified with npm run check, npm test, and diff-only no-any scan.

title: B1: Implement structural flow validation on resolved flow objects
description:
Implement the structural half of Workstream B. Validation runs on the post-configorama, post-normalizeFlow object so dynamic JS/TS/JSON/YAML/TOML flows are judged exactly as runtime sees them.

Checks:
- Every step.prompt resolves relative to flow.dir and exists.
- Every input[].step references an existing earlier step id; nonexistent, self, and forward references are errors.
- step.submit is one of new-run or follow-up.
- step.action is one of issue or comment.
- Existing no-steps and unsupported waitFor errors remain covered.

Design:
- Return { errors: [{ stepId, code, message, hint }], warnings: [] } and accumulate all structural errors in one pass.
- Keep I/O limited to prompt file existence checks.
- Put this near normalizeFlow in src/flows.js or a small module it calls, so runtime and lint/doctor can share the same checker later.

Tests:
- Missing prompt file reports step id and resolved path.
- Bad input.step reports nonexistent/later/self references.
- Bogus submit/action reports allowed values.
- Multiple errors return together.
- Known valid bundled fixture flow returns no errors.
close_reason: Implemented and verified with npm run check, npm test, and diff-only no-any scan.

title: B2: Validate flow-declared agents after model selection
description:
Implement the agent/selection half of Workstream B. Agent validation belongs after prepareInteractiveFlowRun/model selection because only then does nax know the selected/available model set with the same semantics as execution.

Work:
- Reuse or extend flowAgentSet(flow) and assertValidAgentSelection rather than inventing a parallel registry.
- Validate step.agents and defaults.agents after defaults/selection are resolved.
- Error names the unknown agent, step id, and the known/available set.
- Keep this as a separate layer from structural normalizeFlow validation so pure flow loading does not need runtime selection state.

Tests:
- Flow-declared unknown agent fails before submission.
- Defaults.agents unknown value is caught.
- Valid selected agents pass.
- CLI-supplied selection validation remains unchanged.
close_reason: Implemented and verified with npm run check, npm test, and diff-only no-any scan.

title: B3: Wire validator before dry-run/run submission
description:
Wire Workstream B into execution. The validator must run after flow preparation but before the dry-run/run split and before any transport creates issues, runner sessions, comments, or agent runs.

Work:
- Insert structural and agent validation at the seam around bin/nax.js:6502 before the dry-run/run branch at about 6506.
- Dry-run validates and reports errors without requiring a transport.
- Non-dry run aborts with a lint-style all-errors report and spends zero credits.
- Warnings, if any, print but do not block; structural errors block.
- Do not build a duplicate nax doctor/lint implementation in this bead; expose reusable validator output for future doctor/lint surfaces.

Tests:
- Invalid flow exits before any gh/netlify/local-runner seam is called.
- Dry-run invalid flow reports diagnostics and exits nonzero.
- Warn-only flow proceeds.
- Output is stable enough for tests and actionable for users.
close_reason: Implemented and verified with npm run check, npm test, and diff-only no-any scan.

title: B4: Semantic flow validator test matrix
description:
Comprehensive tests for Workstream B. Keep tests focused on resolved-flow semantics and runtime fail-fast behavior.

Coverage:
- Missing prompt file, including resolved absolute path in diagnostic.
- input.step nonexistent, self-reference, and later-step reference.
- Unknown step/default agent after selection layer.
- submit/action bogus enum values.
- Valid bundled/fixture flow passes with no errors.
- Multiple errors return together rather than fail-fast on the first error.
- Invalid flow run path attempts no network/agent submission calls.

Test style:
- Use node --test and temp fixture dirs.
- Use real filesystem prompt files and real config load path where practical.
- Capture intentional stderr/stdout and assert it; keep test output clean.
close_reason: Implemented and verified with npm run check, npm test, and diff-only no-any scan.

title: C: Branch Truth Contract
description:
Workstream C from the Tier 1+2 plan. Resolve one immutable target at run start and thread it everywhere. Today branch/SHA truth is derived ad hoc in local-runner, review-context, bin/nax.js, resume paths, and artifacts. workflow.json has no durable target object; branch and branchSource are bolted on after normalization; resume can silently re-derive from the current checkout.

Goal: create a resolver returning { branch, ref, sha, sourceType, verified, caveats }, persist it in workflow.json, and make context building, submission, resume, artifacts, and visualization consume that target. Fail closed when a transport needs a proven target and the resolver cannot prove it. GitHub Actions issue/comment transport currently passes no ref, so for this repo record sourceType github-actions-implicit, verified false, and warn rather than pretending the operated-on checkout is proven.
close_reason: Implemented and verified with npm run check, npm test, and diff-only no-any scan.

title: C1: Build resolveTarget single branch/SHA resolver
description:
Implement the core Branch Truth Contract resolver. Consolidate resolveWorkflowBranch, resolveRemoteBranchSha, currentGitBranch, resolvePullRequestBranch, and remote-pinning logic into one source of target truth.

API target shape:
{ branch, ref, sha, sourceType, verified, caveats }
where sourceType includes explicit-branch, pull-request, current-branch, github-actions-implicit, and dry-run. sha is a 40-char string when proven, otherwise null. verified is true only when proven against the relevant remote/transport requirement.

Edge cases:
- Detached HEAD: caveat detached-head; require explicit --branch for transports that need a branch.
- Unpushed branch: caveat unpushed; for netlify-api/remote runner paths throw instead of guessing.
- Fork PR: caveat fork-pr; sha may be unverifiable.
- Dry run: sourceType dry-run and no false confidence.
- CI env: do not invent GITHUB_REF behavior unless explicitly designed; current plan says no GITHUB_REF reads exist.

Tests:
- Real temp git repos for current branch, explicit branch, detached HEAD, unpushed branch.
- PR selector/fork behavior through existing gh seams or captured fixtures.
- Netlify-api throws when proof is required and missing.
close_reason: Implemented and verified with npm run check, npm test, and diff-only no-any scan.

title: C2: Persist immutable target in workflow.json schema
description:
Persist Branch Truth Contract target in run state. workflow.json currently lacks durable target metadata; branch/branchSource are assigned post-normalization and pinnedSha lands inconsistently. That allows resume and artifacts to diverge from original reviewed code.

Work:
- Extend createRunState in src/run-state.js to accept and store target at run creation.
- Remove or replace post-hoc runState.branch/runState.branchSource assignment sites around bin/nax.js:6533-6534.
- Consider read-through aliases only if needed for compatibility; do not silently migrate older workflow files without an explicit decision.
- Ensure schema/version handling is tolerant for old workflow.json reads.

Tests:
- New run state contains target exactly as resolved.
- Old workflow.json without target remains readable where listing/resume needs it, with clear limitations.
- No code path mutates target after creation except explicit migration/compat code if approved.
close_reason: Implemented and verified with npm run check, npm test, and diff-only no-any scan.

title: C3: Thread target into context building and prompt generation
description:
Make automatic context and prompts consume runState.target instead of re-deriving branch/SHA. Current sites include readAutoContext in bin/nax.js, buildFlowRunContext, and buildAutomaticContext in src/review-context.js.

Work:
- Replace currentGitBranch/resolveCurrentBranch/resolvePinnedSha calls in context-building paths with the immutable target resolved at run start.
- Ensure prompt text describes the target branch/ref/SHA and caveats accurately.
- For github-actions-implicit targets, include the warning that recorded target is advisory/unverified and actual checkout is whatever the action runner uses.
- Preserve --no-auto-context behavior.

Tests:
- Context block reflects target.branch/sha/caveats from workflow state.
- Changing local git branch after run-state creation does not change generated/resumed context.
- Unverified GitHub implicit target warning appears where appropriate.
close_reason: Implemented and verified with npm run check, npm test, and diff-only no-any scan.

title: C4: Thread target into submission and resume paths
description:
Make agent submission and resume read the immutable target instead of re-deriving branch. Current ad hoc sites include local-runner createAgentRun/createAgentRunAsync --branch handling, submitLocalAgentRun, workflow-runner internal rerunner, and resume fallbacks in bin/nax.js around 6206/6426.

Work:
- Pass target.branch/ref/sha into local runner submission as the single source of branch truth.
- Remove fallback chains like runState.branch || options.branch || currentGitBranch for resumed runs.
- Ensure resume uses the original runState.target even if the user's checkout moved.
- Fail closed if target is missing and the operation requires proof; for legacy state, report a clear limitation.

Tests:
- Mock/local temp git branch changes after interruption; resume still targets original target.
- Local submission receives target branch from runState, not current checkout.
- Missing/legacy target behavior is explicit and tested.
close_reason: Implemented and verified with npm run check, npm test, and diff-only no-any scan.

title: C5: Record GitHub transport as implicit/unverified target
description:
Implement the plan's D2(a) record-and-warn approach for GitHub transport. createIssue/executeGithubFlow pass no ref; branch only reaches the agent in prompt text, while the GitHub Actions checkout target is implicit in netlify-labs/agent-runner-action. This repo should not pretend it can prove the operated-on checkout without a cross-repo dispatch-with-ref change.

Work:
- For GitHub issue/comment transport, resolve and persist target with sourceType github-actions-implicit, verified false, and caveat gha-implicit unless a true dispatch-with-ref path exists.
- Warn loudly in CLI/artifacts that operated-on code is whatever Actions checks out.
- Keep full dispatch-with-ref as a deferred follow-up, not hidden scope creep.

Tests:
- GitHub path records github-actions-implicit and verified false.
- Warning appears in human output/artifacts.
- No false 40-char verified SHA is fabricated for GitHub implicit checkout.
close_reason: Implemented and verified with npm run check, npm test, and diff-only no-any scan.

title: C6: Project target into artifacts and visualizer data
description:
Expose Branch Truth Contract target everywhere users inspect runs. The plan calls out src/workflow-artifacts.js builders, src/visualize-server.js, web/src/types.ts, RecentRuns, and DryRunPanel.

Work:
- Add target fields to workflow artifacts: branch, ref, sha, sourceType, verified, caveats.
- Expose target through visualize server JSON endpoints/types.
- Surface target in RecentRuns and DryRunPanel without oversized UI or misleading confidence.
- For unverified GitHub implicit targets, use clear warning/caveat language rather than green verified styling.

Tests:
- Artifact JSON and summary markdown include target.
- Visualize server response types include target.
- Frontend type checks pass.
- Component tests or focused rendering checks prove verified/unverified states render distinctly.
close_reason: Implemented and verified with npm run check, npm test, and diff-only no-any scan.

title: C7: Branch Truth Contract end-to-end tests
description:
Comprehensive tests for Workstream C. This is the regression net proving branch/SHA truth no longer drifts across run start, context, submission, resume, artifacts, and visualization.

Coverage:
- resolveTarget returns expected shape for explicit branch, PR selector, current branch, dry-run, detached HEAD, unpushed branch, fork PR, and GitHub implicit target.
- netlify-api throws when required SHA cannot be proven.
- createRunState persists target and resume reads it without re-deriving.
- Branch changes between initial run and resume do not alter target or prompt context.
- Artifacts and visualizer expose target consistently.
- GitHub path records github-actions-implicit, verified false, and warning.

Test style:
- Use real temp git repos rather than mocked git commands where practical.
- Use captured gh fixtures/seams for PR/fork behavior.
- Capture and assert warnings; keep output clean.
close_reason: Implemented and verified with npm run check, npm test, and diff-only no-any scan.

title: nax clean: artifact retention and pruning for .nax
description:
WHAT: .nax grows without bound (every run = workflow dir + N runner dirs + M session dirs). nax clean prunes safely:
- selection: --older-than 30d (default) or --keep-last N workflows; orphan runners/sessions (not referenced by any kept workflow.json) included
- PROTECTED always: latest symlink targets, unfinished/interrupted workflows (resumable state is sacred), anything referenced by a kept workflow, run.lock holder
- DEFAULT IS DRY: prints the deletion plan (counts, total bytes, oldest/newest dates) and requires --force to actually delete. Deletion = move to .nax/.trash/<timestamp>/ first, empty trash older than 7 days — two-stage so a mistake is recoverable.
- nax clean --status: just report disk usage by category (workflows/runners/sessions, count + bytes) without any plan.

IMPLEMENTATION: src/artifact-clean.js reusing handoff-sources listing + run-state readers for reference graphs. Never glob-delete; walk known structures only, refuse to touch paths outside .nax (realpath check, symlink-escape safe).

TESTS (tests/unit/artifact-clean.test.js): build real artifact trees in tmp via the actual artifact writers; assert protection rules (latest target survives, interrupted survives, referenced runner survives even if old), orphan detection, trash two-stage, byte accounting, --keep-last vs --older-than interplay (union of protections), dry-by-default (nothing deleted without --force), symlink-escape attempt refused.

ACCEPTANCE: repo with 20 old runs: nax clean shows plan; nax clean --force --older-than 7d removes them to trash, latest + interrupted intact; nax recent still works.

title: Agent + script ergonomics: nax doctor and --json robot mode
description:
WHY: nax has many moving prerequisites (gh auth, netlify auth, linked site, workflow file, repo secrets, node >= 18) and today failures surface one at a time, mid-run, as scattered errors (see README Troubleshooting table — every row is a doctor check).
